	Pankaj Barwal, 2015, Hydrographic survey is the science of measurement and description of features which affect maritime navigation, marine construction, dredging, offshore oil exploration/offshore oil drilling and related activities. Strong emphasis is placed on soundings, shorelines, tides, currents, seabed and submerged obstructions that relate to the previously mentioned activities. The term hydrography is sometimes used synonymous to describe maritime cartography, which in the final stages of the hydrographic process uses the raw data collected through hydrographic survey into information usable by the end user.Hydrography is collected under rules which vary depending on the acceptance authority. Traditionally conducted by ships with a sounding line or echo sounding, surveys are increasingly conducted with the aid of aircraft and sophisticated electronic sensor systems in shallow waters.

If h is the difference in level between end points separated by l, then the slope correction is  . The second term may be neglected if the value of h in a 20 m distance is less than
	Vijay Patel, 2013, Second term neglect and l = 20.So h2 = 2L.h2 = 2*20.h = 3.3m.Near equal to 3m.
	Namrathasai, 2014, Would you please explain the calculations! Why are you taken that h2/2l=1?
	Aman, 2014, If we use any value lesser than 3 and not greater than 3, then answer for slope correction either using the complete formula given above or by eliminating the second term, we will get the same answer. Thus it means that the second term can be eliminated the right answer is 3 and distance kept as 20m.
	Vivek Chandel, 2014, For slopes of 10 percent or less, the correction to be applied to L for a difference d in elevation between tape ends, or for a horizontal offset d between tape ends, may be computed from, Cs=(h^2)/(2L).For a slope greater than 10 percent, Cs may be determined from,Cs={(h^2)/(2L)}+{(h^4)/8(L^3)}.

	Abhilash Jana, 2015, When distances are measured along the slope, the equivalent horizontal distance may be determined by applying a slope correction.The vertical slope angle of the length measured must be measured.  (Refer to the figure on the other side).Thus, For gentle slopes, m<20%.Ch = h^2/2l.For steep slopes, 20% <= m <= 30%.Ch = h^2/2l+h^4/8l^3.For very steep slopes, m>30%.Ch = l(1-cos0).

	Imran Hossain, 2016, ** If h is the difference in level between endpoints separated by l, then the slope correction is  c=(h^2/2l)+{h^4/8(l^3)}.** If h is less than 3m in a length often the 20 m, then the quantity h^4/8(l^3)  can be neglected. Hence c = h^2/2l.Source. Dr. B.C. Punmia.

We need to find, for which value of h the slope will be less than 0.04.Sub h = 0.5, we get slope = 0.025;Sub h = 1,    we get slope = 0.050;Sub h = 2,    we get slope = 0.100;Sub h = 3,    we get slope = 0.150;Therefore Option A (h=0.5m) is the correct answer.

The intercept of a staff
	Aman, 2014, If staff is not vertical, the reading will be too great. If staff is normal to the line of sight the value will be minimum or accurate. It will not be maximum. The intercept of staff increases when it is tilted away from norman and decreases or gets minimum when moved towards normal.

	Rabindra, 2018, In the survey, it is important to have greater sensitivity for greater accuracy. Sensitivity is defined by the level tube, it changes with many factors like the viscosity of fluid, radius of curvature of the bubble, dia of the tube, the internal roughness of tube etc. Greater is the radius of curvature greater is the sensitivity.
	Lekkala Vyshnavi, 2018, Plese explain it clearly.
	Nitin Mate, 2018, Yes, explain it clearly.
	Prasenjit Bhowmick, 2018, Bubble tube sensitive is proportional with length The radius of curvature of the arc and dia of pipe.
	Sivaji, 2019, Greater is the radius of the Curvature lesser is the sensitivity.Sensitivity = Length of one division on tube / Radius of curvature.= L/R.If R is more, sensitivity is less.
	Kiran Kp, 2019, The bubble tube is small, Then how m came?
	Md.Abu Nayem, 2019, Right, explain it clearly.
	Aravind, 2019, The axis of the bubble tube is not an axis passing through the bubble tube-like telescope. It's an arc that passes through the midpoint of the bubble tube length. For a deflection of theta degree at the center of the earth what deflection it makes on the surface of the earth is called its sensitivity. Similarly, the arc of the bubble tube also has a center at the center of the earth and so the radius of curvature is generally taken as 100m.
	Gopi Krishna, 2020, Could you please explain briefly?
	Sabhavat Amru Nayak, 2021, Can you explain it clearly?
	Dharmaraj Dhakal, 2021, Can you explain it clearly?



If S is the length of a subchord and R is the radius of simple curve, the angle of deflection between its tangent and sub-chord, in minutes, is equal to
	S.Venkata Siva, 2015, Give me description.
	S.Venkata Siva, 2015, Give me description.
	Jaswant Nayal, 2015, Please guys give description.
	Pj_Baba, 2015, Answer = (S/2r)*180*60/pi = 1718.87 S/R.
	Josh, 2015, I don't think this answer (E) is correct. It is only correct if the sub chord, S, was actually the sub arc length. Otherwise, the real answer should be sin^-1 (S/2R)*60.
	Prakash Barik, 2016, Please give some description my friend.
	Hazem, 2016, Yes Josh, you are completely right.
	Umair, 2016, Yes, E is 100% correct answer.
	Prem, 2016, Please describe the question briefly.
	Abhishek Kumar, 2016, Please describe it clearly.
	Mohammed Salahuddin Tirandaz, 2016, Not understanding, Please describe it clearly.
	Yamanoor, 2016, Angle of deflection=S/2R radians.= 180 * S/2 * R degree,= 180 * 60 * S/2 R*  minutes, = 1718.9S/R.
	Naz, 2017, Angle of reflection = 180 * s * 60/2R degree.= 1718.9S/R.
	Gman, 2017, The angle of deflection = ((180/π) * s * 60)/(2 * R)°.
	Daxa Rathod, 2017, Give me the description of this answer.
	Ved Prakash, 2017, Actually the angular method of curve setting ,by Rankine's deflection method the deflection angle b/w chord and point of tangency is =1720C/R i, where c is the first sub chord and R is the radius.
	Amal Zaf Bannu, 2017, E is the correct answer. I agree with the given answer.
	Shrikant, 2018, Angle of deflection= (360/4π)* S/R.This gives answer 28.64 S/R.which is in degrees,, soo one degree is 60 min.Thus, 28.64 S/R degrees in radians is answer E) 1718.9 S/R.
	Williams, 2018, Can anyone explain the solution in detail?
	Sameer Sopori, 2018, @Williams.For an angle of deflection in RADIAN      then use =S/2R.For an angle of deflection in DEGREE    then use =(S/2R)* (180/π),For an angle of deflection in MINUTES   then use = (S/2R)*(180/π)* 60.
	Nancy, 2019, @Sameer.Thankyou for the explanation.
	Sai Adithya, 2019, Thanks @Sameer Sopori.
	Govarthanan R, 2020, For an angle of deflection in MINUTES then use = (S/2R)*(180/π)* 60.= S/R*((180*60)/(2*(22/7)).=S/R *1718.18,=1718.18S/R.
	Cemti, 2021, @Govarthan.Please explain how did you get the 22/7?
	Nadeem, 2021, Angel of deflection in radian = S/2R.And in radian =180 * S/2R * °.  And in minute 180 * 60 * S/2R*°.After simplification =1718.87S/R.
	Karyeija Felex, 2021, For all practical purposes sub chord is equal to sub arc length, so E is correct.

One of the Lehmann's rules of plane tabling, is
	Saleena, 2017, Please, give more explanation about Lehmann's rule.
	Nitin Mate, 2018, Please, someone, describe shortly to understand.
	Sachin Giri, 2019, Lehmann's rules1. If the position of plane table station is inside the great triangle, its plotted position should be chosen inside the triangle of error.2. If the position of the plane table station is outside the great triangle, its plotted position should be chosen outside the triangle of error.3. The plotted position of the plane table should be so chosen that its distance from the resistors is proportional to the distance of the plane table station from the field positions of the considered objects.4. The plotted position of the plane table should be so chosen that it is to the same side of all the three rays. 4(a). If the position of the plane table station is outside the great circle, its plotted position should be so chosen thatIt lies on the same side of the ray to the most distant point as the intersection k of the other two rays.The intersection of two rays to the nearer points, is midway between the plotted position of the station and the ray to the most distant point.lehmann+rule+plane+table+survey.4(b). If the position of plane table station is outside the great triangle but inside the great circle, the plotted position of the station is so chosen that the ray to the middle point lies between plotted station position and the intersection of the rays to the two extreme points.
	Nabin Acharya, 2019, Thanks @Sachin.
	Cheeku Sharma, 2020, Thanks @Sachin.
	Sudarshan Agale, 2020, Thanks @Sachin.
	Aniket Kumar, 2021, Thanks @Sachin.

Two concave lenses of 60 cm focal length are cemented on either side of a convex lens of 15 cm focal length. The focal length of the combination is
	Pawan Kumar, 2013, 1/f =  -1/60 -1/60 +1/15 = 1/30/f = 30.
	Karan Dumbare, 2014, 1/f = 1/F1+1/F2.1/60 = 1/15+1/F2.F = 30.
	Manasa Reddy, 2014, If we substitute d values in above formula am getting f2=20.
	Nitin, 2014, 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2.1/f = 1/60 + 1/60.= 1/30. So combination is 30.
	M.Lokeswara Rao, 2014, 1/f = 1/f1+1/f2.1/60 = 1/15+1/f2.f2 = 30.
	Ravi, 2014, 1/f = 1/f1+1/f2.1/60 = 1/15+1/f2.1/60-1/15 = 1/f2.1/f2 = 1/30.f2 = 30.
	Aswin, 2014, How is this calculated? Please explain.
	Saubhagya Laxmi, 2015, 1/60-1/15 = -1/20 then how the answer z 30?
	Suresh Awasthi, 2015, Formula: 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2.According to the problem 60 cm on the either side i.e. f1 = f2 = 60 & focal length combination f = ?Now, 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2.1/f = 1/60 + 1/60.= 1/30. So combination is 30.
	Priya, 2015, Please explain again.
	Lokesh, 2015, Convex is magnifying glass, makes image larger. So its behavior is making big +ve. Concave is indicated as -ve. Now three lenses are combined to make single lens. To get the property of new lens we have to find out its focal length, it can be calculated by 1/f = 1/f1+1/f2+1/f3.f1 = f2 = 60, f3 = 15. You will get f = 30.
	Sameer Kashmiri, 2016, 1/f =- (1/f1) - (1/f2) + (1/f3).1/f =- (1/60) - (1/60) + (1/15).1/f =- (1/30) + (1/15).1/f = 15/450.1/f = 1/30.So, f = 30.
	Sammy, 2016, 1/f = 1/f1 + 1/f2.1/60 = 1/15 + 1/f2.So, f2 = 30.
	Katakamsaketh, 2016, We know that the focal length of the concave lens is negative and convex lens is positive then the focal length of the combination can be found out by using lens formula.
	R@Ghv, 2016, It's a three lens combination so I agree with you @Sammer Kashmiri.
	Siddh, 2016, Two concave lens = 60cm and convex lens = 15cm,then 1/f = -1/f1 - 1/f2 + 1/f3             = -1/60 - 1/60 + 1/15             = -1/60 - 1/60 + 4/60             = 4 - 2/60             = 2/60             = 1/30.So, the combination is 30 cm.
	Amit Ami Sharma, 2016, Yes, you are correct @Lokesh.

	Pankaj Yadav, 2017, 1/f= -1/60-1/60.1/f= -1/30.1/f' = -1/30+1/15.1/f'= 1/30,f'=30.
	Ratan Pal, 2017, I think 1/f=1/15 -1/60 -1/60.

Depending upon the position of a survey line, the direction of the reference meridian to the line can be either clockwise or anticlockwise. In the expression of the reduced bearing value of a line, quadrant has to be mentioned in which the line lies.

If L is the perimeter of a closed traverse, ΔD is the closing error in departure, the correction for the departure of a traverse side of length l, according to Bowditch rule, is
	Kaushik Ali, 2016, The closing error is distributed along the perimeter.It will be = D/L. The correction of the departure of traverse length will be = (D/L) l.
	Dhojsaud, 2018, According to Bowditch Rule;=Total error departure X Length of that side/ Perimeter of the traverse.Therefore Right Answer is Option 'D'.



Pick up the correct statement from the following:
	Neeraj, 2018, Transiting:It is also known as plunging or reversing. It is the process of turning the telescope about its horizontal axis thought 180° in the vertical plane thus bringing it upside down and making it point exactly in opposite direction.Swinging the Telescope:It means turning the telescope about its vertical axis in the horizontal plane. A swing is called right or left according as the telescope is rotated clockwise or counterclockwise.Face Left: If the vertical circle of the instrument is on the left of the observer while taking a reading, the position is called the face left and the observation taken on the horizontal or the vertical circle in this position is known as the face left observation.Face Right: If the vertical circle of the instrument is on the right of the observer while taking a reading, the position is called the face light, and the observation taken on the horizontal or the vertical circle in this position is known as the face right observation.Changing Face: It is the operation of bringing the vertical circle to the right of the observer if originally it is to the left, and vice- versa. It is done in two steps: firstly revolve the telescope through 180° in a vertical plane and then rotate it through 180° in the horizontal plane i.e. first transit the telescope and then swing it through 180°.
	Shubham, 2018, Good explanation, Thanks @Neeraj.
	Paras Maskare, 2018, Thanks @Neeraj.
	Chitra, 2019, Thanks @Neeraj.
	Asad, 2020, Excellent explanation@ Thanks Neeraj.
	Siddhi Mahtre, 2021, Nice. Thanks all.

If θ is the slope of the ground and l is the measured distance, the correction is
	Danesh, 2014, Slope correction= L-D.Here L=l.     D=lcos@.So Correction = l-lcos@.             = l(1-cos@).             = 2lsin^@/2.
	Vamsi Suppu, 2014, Slope correction = L-D.L=l.cos@=l/D.D=ICOS@.Correction = L-D.          = l-lcos@.          = l(1-cos@).          = l(2sin@/2).
	Saravanaraja, 2017, How D=lcosθ?
	Akki, 2018, D means? Give expansion for the formula.
	Priyanka Shah, 2018, @All.how comes D=I cosθ. Please explain it.
	Ameiry Hassan, 2018, Slope correction = l-D, where l is sloping distance and D horizontal distance, construct a right-angled triangle ABC whose hypotenuse is l, adjacent D and the angle subtended between l and D be theta (θ) solve for D using trigonometric ratio u will get D= lcosθ then recall, Correction = l-D                                                                                                                                                                                                                 =l-lcosθ                                                                                                                                                                                                                  = l(1-cos θ)Using the concept of half angle formula 1-cosθ= 2sin^2θ/2 and therefore                                                                              correction= 2lsin^2(θ/2).
	Kuldeep Kumar, 2020, Right answer is A.

Offsets are measured with an accuracy of 1 in 40. If the point on the paper from both sources of error (due to angular and measurement errors) is not to exceed 0.05 cm on a scale of 1 cm = 20 m, the maximum length of offset should be limited to
	Anvesh, 2015, How it will come?
	Subhajit, 2015, s*r/(40*1.414).= (20*40)/56.56.= 14.14 answer.
	Rajesh, 2015, Mention the formula clearly to calculate.
	Ganpati Patel, 2015, L = s*r*error/1.414.= 20*40*0.05/1.414 = 28.28 m.
	Darshika, 2015, L = 0.05*s*r/1.414 = 28.28 m.
	Ihab, 2016, Why 1.414?
	Abhay, 2016, The center-to-center dimension for a 45-degree bend is equal to the desired size of the offset times the cosecant 1.414. A cosecant is used to determine the distance between the centers of the two bends used to make an offset. A 45-degree angle has a cosecant of 1.414.
	Satish, 2016, Due to angular & linear sin45 = 0.7 then it is 1.414.
	Akhilesh Yadav, 2017, The displacement due to angular error =Lsin.and it should be equal to displacement due to Linear error = L/r.Corresponding displacement on paper=√2(L/r)(1/S)= √2(Lsin-/S).Corresponding if a limit of accuracy in plotting is 0.05cm then,√2(L/r)(1/S)=.05,L= (.05x40x20)/√2.= 28.28.Where   S=1 in accuracy.              r=1 in scale.
	Naga Raju Gowd, 2017, How answer is 28.28?I am not getting this. Please explain me.
	Mohamed Elbesy, 2017, I think 200 is the correct answer.1/40 accuracy on the map means 50 cm on the ground according to scale.This means the offset is rounded to the nearest 50 cm and this applies only on 200 m.
	Gaurav, 2017, Nowhere is mentioned that linear measurement is equal to angular measurement then, what is the reason to get answer 28.28?
	Jay Panchal, 2017, Why 1.414?
	Aashish, 2017, This formula is applicable maximum errorrs/√2.28.28 correct answer.
	Er Meghanada (Bhadrak), 2017, L = 0.05"40"20*14.14  = 28.28 m.
	Chandu, 2018, The maximum length or limiting length=L * S/sin0.S=scale,0=45°
	Sachin Patil, 2020, What does 1 in 40 refer? Please explain.
	Shubham P, 2020, Formula : Limiting error = (L/sin@) * (1/r) * (1/S).&angle; = 45' for max limiting offset. 1/r = 1:40 = accuracy of measurement. 1/S= 1:20 = scale. Limiting error = 0.05 cm.L = 20*(2)^0.5.L= 28.28 m.
	Vijender Thakur, 2020, How come 1.414? Please explain the answer in detail.
	Abdus Samad Khan, 2021, Cosec = 1/sin.Than sin45 =.707.Cosec = 1/.707 = 1.414.

The bearings of the lines AB and BC are 146° 30' and 68° 30'. The included angle ABC is
	Kala, 2013, Answer: 146-68 = 78.78+180 = 258.360-258 = 102.
	Pawan Kumar, 2013, 146'30'-90' = 56'30.90'-56'30' = 33'30+68'30 = 102'00'.
	Prithvee Raj, 2014, INCLUDED ANGLE = 180'-146'30'+68'30' = 102'00'.
	Danesh, 2014, Included angle = f.b of next line - b.b of previous line.             =  f.b of BC - b.b of AB.             = 68.30 - 146.30.             = 78.              +180 = 102.
	Dhaval Naam To Suna Hi Hoga, 2015, Simple 180-146°30'+78°30'=102.
	Chandan, 2015, 180°-146°30' = 33°30'.68°30'+33°30' = 102°.
	Darshik, 2015, 146.5-90 = 56.5.90-56.5 = 33.5.33.5+68.5 = 102.
	Darshan H A, 2015, Included angle = Bearing of previous line - Bearing of next line = bearing of ba - bearing of bc = (180+146.5)-(68.5) = 258 which is exterior angle. Hence interior angle = 360-258 = 102.
	Pravesh Verma, 2016, Both f.b.So BC of B.B =68°30'+180° = 248°30'.AB of f.b. = 146°30'./ABC = 248°30' - 146°30' = 102° answer.
	Satyendra Patel, 2016, 180-146°30' + 68°30' = 102'.
	Apurba, 2017, 360 - (180 + 146°30) + 68°30 = 102.
	Indrajit Maiti, 2017, The bb of AB - fb of BC = {(180°+146°30')- 68°30'}= 258°,Angle = (360°-258°) =102°.
	Sandip Mondal, 2017, FB of AB=146°30'. BB of AB=180°+146°30'=326°30.Ext<ABC=326°30'-BB of BC=326°30'-68*30'=258°.Int<ABC=360°-258°=102° ans.

The slope correction for a length of 30 m along a gradient of 1 in 20, is
	Sandeep, 2013, (1.5)2 / 2x3000  = 3.75 cm.Note: Length 30 m, gradient 1/20 ; h= 1.5.Equation  : h2/2l.
	Hitesh Kumar, 2014, Slope correction= h/2*2l.
	Priya, 2014, Can any one explain this properly?
	Dash, 2014, What is Gradient?
	Ahishek, 2014, How h = 1.5?
	Anil Soni, 2015, For 20 m, h = 1 m. So for 30 m, h = 1.5 m.
	Pranab, 2015, h = 1.5 m & l = 30 m. So that = h2/2l = (1.5)2/2*30 = 0.0375 cm.
	Prashant, 2015, (H2/2L)+(H4/8L3) as gradient is 1 in 20. i.e For 20 m, h = 1 m. So for 30 m, h = 1.5 m which is less than 3 m. Therefore second term of equation should be neglected.Answer is (1.5)2/2x3000 = 3.75 cm.
	Engr Attaullah Khan Tareen, 2015, For 20 m, h = 1 m. So for 30 m, h = 1.5 m.So that = h^2/2l = (1.5) 2/2*30 = 0.0375 m.1 m = 100 cm so 0.0375*100 = 3.75 cm.
	Maheshkumar, 2015, In 1 in 20 means, 20m = 1m.So total 30m length = 1.5m.In Gradient 1 in 20 Means.Imagine Triangle, length-30m & Height-1.5m.Use Pythagoras theorem.X2 = 302+1.52.X = 30.0375m.Change length = 30.0375-30.= 0.0375m.= 3.75cm.
	Kanhu, 2015, Slope correction for a sloping ground = h^2/2l.Where h = height.l = length of the ground.
	Ali, 2016, We know for 30 m length, h = 1.5And given gradient is 1 in 20 i.e 1/20So, the equation of slope correction is,= h^2 / 2l= 1.5^2 / 2*30 m= 2.25 / 60m= 0.0375 m or 3.75 cm
	Swamy, 2016, Hi, friends all give the good explanations. Thank you.
	Hima Bindu, 2016, How h =1.5?
	Md Faisal, 2016, In ABC triangle,AC = l = 30m.Slope = 1 : 20.1 in20 so in 30 = (1/20) * 30 = 1.5Therefore BC = 1.5m.And AB extends to BB'.And meet to points A as arc & B as a straight line.Here BB'= correction of line,= AC - AB.AB = (30^2 - 1.5^2)^1/2     = 29.962m.BB' = 30 - 29.962= 0.0375m or 3.75cm.
	Baloch, 2016, Slope correction = h^2 / 2l.h = 20/30 = 1.5.l = length. By submitting the value you will get the answer.

The main principle of surveying is to work
	Gyaneswar, 2016, According to whole to part principle whole area enclosed by main survey line & station. Then area divided into well conditioned triangles.This principle help to prevent accumulation error. If any error in the measurement of any sides of triangle not affect the whole work.
	Pavitra, 2016, To prevent the accumulation error. For example if we are going to draw a straight line using chain surveying whose length is more than four chainages (4*30m), by adopting part to whole, the small distraction in first chainage leads to a great error by reaching the final point. Hence by adopting whole to part we can reduce such errors.
	Habib, 2016, Main principle of servey is from whole to part of area.
	Pankaj Yadav, 2017, Survey work from whole to part has more accuracy. Error does not increase.
	Taimoor, 2020, My opinion is C. The main survey should be a higher level to the lower level.

ABCD is a regular parallelogram plot of land whose angle BAD is 60°. If the bearing of the line AB is 30°, the bearing of CD, is
	Hitesh Kumar, 2014, Bearing of CD = 180+30 = 210.
	Debashis Mahato, 2014, How its possible?
	Mohammed Libinshad. K, 2015, Since it is a regular pentagon.i.e. Bearing of AB = Bearing of DC.=> Bearing of CD = Back bearing of DC.= Back bearing of AB = 180+30 = 210.
	Raj, 2015, Back Bearing-Fore Bearing = 180.So BB-30 = 180,Now, BB = 180+30 = 210.
	Mohan Cenation, 2015, The bearing of AB is 30*. Since, it is less than 90*. So, we have to add 180* for it. Then it is (30*+180*=210*).
	Darshan H A, 2015, Total interior angle = (2n-4)*90 where n=4, no of sides.There fore total interior angle = ((2*4)-4)*90 = 360. Each included angle = total interior angle/n = 360/4 =90.The defection angle = 180-each included angle = 180-90 = 90.Then fore bearing of any line = Deflection angle + Fore bearing of previous line.Fore bearing of AB = 30, fore bearing of BC = 90+30 = 120, fore bearing of CD = 90+120 = 210, fore bearing of DA = 90+210 = 300.
	Manatosh, 2016, FB of line CD = BB of line AB. = FB of AB + 180°. = 30°+180° = 210°.
	Nikhil, 2017, Thanks for the brief description @Darshan H A.
	Shree G, 2017, Thanks for the brief description.
	Hitesh Singh, 2017, Here, 180 + 30 = 210.
	Sandip Mondal, 2017, <ABC=180°-60°=120°.FB of AB=30°BB of AB=30°+180°=210°FB of BC=210°+<ABC=210°+120°=330°BB of BC=330-180=150FB of CD=150+<BAD=150+60=210°.
	Chandan Kumar Singh, 2017, ANSWER-- CBearing of  AB =Bearing of DC   BECAUSE regular parallelogram. Bearing of DC = 30 DEGREE. NOW   bearing of CD = BB of DC =180+30=210 DEGREE.
	Kuldeep Nautiyal, 2017, Bearing of BC = bearing of BA + angle B.B of BC = 30+180+120.Bearing of CD = bearing of CB + angle C.Bearing of CD = (330 - 180) + 60.Angle B + angle A = 180  (sum of consecutive angle of a parallelogram are 180).
	Bhawna, 2018, Can anyone explain this with the help of pictorial representation?
	Prepared, 2019, 210°-180°=30°+30°=60°.So 210° is the right answer.
	Tushar Methiya, 2019, Bearing of AB is 30 & BAD is 60 then for CD angle is 180+30 = 210.

	Amit Ami Sharma, 2016, IRC recommends Spiral or clothoid as the ideal transition curve due to following reasons.1. It satisfies that rate of change of centrifugal acceleration is constant i.e., Ls.R = constant. Where Ls = length of transition curve R = radius of the curve.2. The calculation and field implementation of the spiral curve is simple and easy.3. It enhances aesthetics also.
	Abdulrasith, 2017, Clothing spiral means?Explain in clear.

	Nagendra Yadav, 2020, Curves fulfilling equation of an ideal transition curve are known as "Clothoids or true spirals".
	Assumi, 2020, For Highway - Lemniscate is best.For Railway - Cubic parabola and cubic spiral is recommended.
	Pankaj Kumar, 2021, Radius and centrifugal acceleration are change gradually and setting out of curve easy, jerk problem can be prevented in spiral/clothoid transition curve.

If the angular measurements of a traverse are more precise than its linear measurements, balancing of the traverse, is done by
	Shokat, 2016, Balancing of travers is done by?
	D.C., 2016, Reducing the error of closure is the balancing.
	Bhusha Nilekar, 2017, We can also do this by Bowditch and Empirical. I think option D is correct.
	Santhosh C, 2017, In Bowditch method, both angular and linear are given equal precision.In axis method, angular measurements are measured accurately and corrections applied to linear measurements only.
	Arfan Riaz, 2017, I think option D is correct.
	Aman, 2018, Can anyone explain clearly?
	Akkki, 2018, Balancing is done by two methods 1 transit and 2 Bowditch.If angular measurement are precise then transit method is used.If linear measurements are precise than angular then Bowditch method is used.
	Pankaj, 2019, Bowditch rule = this rule is used to balance the traverse when angular and linear measurement are (equally) precise.Transit rule = this rule is used when angular measurement is (more) precise than linear measurement.
	Rajesh, 2020, Yes. You are correct, thanks @Pankaj.
	Mariyadass Selvam, 2020, @All.All three options are used for balancing the closed traverse and if angular measurements are more precise than linear measurements then the answer is transit rule.

If the rate of gain of radial acceleration is 0.3 m per sec3 and full centrifugal ratio is developed. On the curve the ratio of the length of the transition curve of same radius on road and railway, is
	Anvesh, 2015, Please explain it.
	Suraj, 2015, Any one have this solution?
	Anand, 2015, Can anybody clarify it?
	Chirag, 2015, Please some one give the solution.
	Yousuf Mallick, 2016, Anybody help?
	Manas Kumar Sahoo, 2016, Can anybody clarify the concept?
	Student, 2016, For road centrifugal ratio = 1/4.For railway = 1/8.Centrifugal ratio = v^2/Rg.R = radius (v^2/r) = Acceleration rate * t. t = time t = L/v.L = length. v = velocity. Here we get L=(v^3)/(acceleration * r).Acceleration is given and r is same for road and railway.So, L is directly proportional to v^3 and from centrifugal ratio = v^2/Rg.v is directly proportional to square root of the centrifugal ratio.From here ratio of length for road and railway; L1/L2= (1/2)^3/(1/square root 8)^3.= 2 * square root 2.= 2 * 1.414.= 2.828.
	Sree, 2016, Can you explain clearly?
	Luhenba, 2016, Can anyone explain clearly please?
	Gopi, 2016, Can you explain clearly.
	Mohit Kumar, 2017, Explain the question properly.
	Mk, 2017, Sir please explain this question in detail.
	Learner, 2017, I can't understand it. Please explain it.
	Gurkamal Singh, 2017, Centrifugal ratio for road=1/4,Centrifugal ratio for railway=1/8,Centrifugal ratio(c.r.)=V^2/Rg where R is the radius and g is acc. due to gravity............Eq. 1,and also V^2/R=Acc.rate x t  where t is the time..........Eq. 2,V^2 is directly proportional to C.R. since R and g is constant.R is constant since same radius is given for road and railway.Therefore V is directly proportional to (C.R.)^(1/2),Now  from Eq. 2.t=V^2/acc. rate*R  ..........Eq. 3.And we also know that L=V*t where L is length v is vel. and t is time.Putting the value of t from Eq. 3 in above equation we will get, L=V^3/acc. rate*RNow L is directly proportional to V^3 since acc. rate and radius are constant for both road and railway.And V is directly proportional to C.R.^(1/2),hence L is directly proportional to C.R.^(3/2),Now L1(road)=k*(1/4)^(3/2).L2(railway)=k*(1/8)^(3/2).Dividing L1 by L2We get L1/L2=(sq. root of 8)=2*(sq. root of 2)=2*1.414=2.828.
	Asma, 2017, Please explain it clearly.
	Jay Panchal, 2017, Explain clearly?
	Ranjeet Kumar, 2017, Well said, Thanks @Gurkamal.

	Muhammad, 2018, Thanks @Gurkamal Singh.
	Pintu  Kumar, 2019, Centrifugal ratio for road = 1/4,Centrifugal ratio for railway = 1/8,Centrifugal ratio(c.r.)=V^2/Rg where R is the radius and g is acc. due to gravity ---> Eq. 1,and also V^2/R=Acc.rate x t where t is the time  ---> Eq. 2.V^2 is directly proportional to C.R. since R and g is constant.R is constant since the same radius is given for road and railway.Therefore V is directly proportional to (C.R.)^(1/2).Now from Eq. 2.t=V^2/acc. rate*R  ---> Eq. 3.And we also know that L=V*t, where L is length v, is vel. and t is time.Putting the value of t from Eq. 3 in the above equation we will get, L=V^3/acc. rate*R.Now, L is directly proportional to V^3 since acc. rate and radius are constant for both road and railway.And V is directly proportional to C.R.^(1/2),Hence L is directly proportional to C.R.^(3/2),Now L1(road)=k*(1/4)^(3/2).L2(railway)=k*(1/8)^(3/2).Dividing L1 by L2.We get L1/L2=( √8)=2*(√2) = 2*1.414 = 2.828.

	Asad, 2020, Centrifugal ratio for road = 1/4,Centrifugal ratio for railway = 1/8,Centrifugal ratio(c.r.)=V^2/Rg where R is the radius and g is acc. due to gravity ---> Eq. 1,and also V^2/R=Acc.rate x t where t is the time ---> Eq. 2.V^2 is directly proportional to C.R. since R and g is constant.R is constant since the same radius is given for road and railway.Therefore V is directly proportional to (C.R.)^(1/2).Now from Eq. 2.t=V^2/acc. rate*R ---> Eq. 3.And we also know that L=V*t, where L is length v, is vel. and t is time.Putting the value of t from Eq. 3 in the above equation we will get,L=V^3/acc. rate * R.Now, L is directly proportional to V^3 since acc. rate and radius are constant for both road and railway.And V is directly proportional to C.R.^(1/2),Hence L is directly proportional to C.R.^(3/2),Now L1(road)=k*(1/4)^(3/2).L2(railway)=k*(1/8)^(3/2).Dividing L1 by L2.We get L1/L2=(√8)=2*(√2) = 2*1.414 = 2.828.

	Sameer Kashmiri, 2013, Slope correction = h^2/2L.

	Yogii, 2016, 0.108 only coming ~.11 in slope error is only cumulative +0.11.

	Khushwant Rana, 2019, In two theodolite method, curves are staked out by angular measurements only. Accuracy attained in this method is quite high. Thus, the method is used when higher accuracy is required and when the topography is rough or field condition is difficult.Under "Fundamental Geometry of Circular Curve";The underlying principle of this method is that the deflection angle between a tangent (at any point on a circle) and a chord is equal to the angle which the chord subtends in the alternate segment.
	Anurag, 2019, It uses only angular measurement.

The desired sensitivity of a bubble tube with 2 mm divisions is 30". The radius of the bubble tube should be
	Rajni Kant, 2014, Sensitivity = [l*206265]/R.R = ((2/1000)*206265)/30 = 13.75.
	Chinna, 2015, How to taken *206265 value?
	Anshul, 2015, 1 radian = 206, 265 seconds.
	Sameer Kashmiri, 2016, If 30 = 2mm.Then 1 =60" =4mm.Then 1degree = 60 * 4 = 240mm.It means 360 degree = 360 * 40 = 86400mm.= 86.4 m.R = L/2 * 3.14.R = 86.4/2 * 3.14.R = 13.75 m.
	Rupayan Lahiri, 2016, @ Sameer Kashmiri.There is a mistake in the third line of your comment. It will be  360 * 240 = 86400.
	Pinku, 2016, 2mm deflect  with a small change of angle 30''30'' ---> 2mm60'' ---> 4mm1' ---> 4mm.For 360degree  ---> 360 * 60 * 4.So, 2 * 3.14 * r = 360 * 60 * 4.r = 13.75.
	Shailendrabsingh, 2016, Angle = arc/radius or =(d/r) radians.Because 1 Radian = 206265 second.So, = (d/r) * 206265 second.Given that d = 2mm or 2 * 10^-3m.So, ={(2 * -10^-3) * 206265}&div; r.Then @ = 412.53&div;r.Given, 30second.So r = 412.53&div;30,r = 13.75meter.
	Roy, 2017, Thanks @Pinku.
	Md Faisal, 2017, In 30'----2mm.So 360°=?(360*60*60)'*2mm/30'=length of circumference.So Lc=2*3.14*R.(360*60*60)'*2/30'=2*3.14*R.R=13.75m.
	Tanmoy Karmakar, 2018, 30'' = 2mm,1' = 4mm,1° = 240mm,360° = 240*360 = 86400mm,2*π*r  =  86400,So, r = 13757.9mm = 13.75 m.
	Kesavan, 2018, Sensitivity = (D/R)*205265. so, we need R=(D/sensitivity)*206265.      R = (0.002/30)*206265.      R = 13.75 m.
	Anjali, 2019, How the radius is inverse to sensitiveness?
	Roohullah Jan, 2021, It's quite simple guys.As we know S=R* π --->1)Where R is radius and π is Central angle in radian.So S= 2mm.π= 30" remember 1"= 0.00000484814 so 30" becomes 30* 0.00000484814= 0.0001454441 rad.Equation (1)=> 2=R*0.0001454441.R= 13750.98mm => 13.75 m.
	Moin, 2021, For 2 mm divisions, sensitivity = 412.43/R. This is a simple way to remember it. In general, sensitivity = 206265 * d/R.

A dumpy level was set up at the midpoint between two pegs A and B, 50 m apart and the staff readings at A and B were 1.22 and 1.06. With the level set up a A, the readings at A and B were 1.55 and 1.37. The collimation error per 100 m length of sight is
	M.Pagavan Doss, 2014, Distance between a and b = 50 m.When instrument at center=1.22 - 1.06 = .160.When instrument near A = 1.55-1.37 = .180.DIFF = .160-.180 = -.020(- INDICATE DOWNWARDS).COLLIMATION ERROR FOR 100M length = (.020/50)*100 = .04m inclined downward.
	Sammy, 2016, Best explanation.Thanks @M.Pagavan Doss.
	Md Saddam, 2016, Please help about B.O.D & C.O.D formula.
	Darshan, 2016, Here when you put level at centre all errors are eliminated . Hence difference in RL is 1.22 - 1.06 is 0.16 but the observed staff difference is 1.55 - 1.37 = 0.180. Therefore the error is 0.180 - 0.160 = 0.02 for 50 m, for 100m it is 0.04. It is positive value hence line of sight is upwards.
	Ashok, 2016, When the level is not set at the center what distance can I take instead of 50m?
	Swapnil, 2016, Thanks for explaining easily.
	Aarti Nepal, 2017, Thank you so much @M.Pagavan Doss.
	Sandeep, 2017, Thank you @Pagavan Doss.
	Sooraj, 2017, Thank you so much @Pagavan Doss.
	Deep, 2018, Formula of errors =(b1-a1)-(b2-a2)/2.Now, e = (1.06-1.22)-(1.37-1.55)/2 =0.01for 50 m and for 100 m error = 2 *0.01 = 0.02 upward.
	R,D Prajapati, 2018, THANKS @DOSS.
	Nishant, 2018, Yes, your are right @DEEP.
	Rohan, 2018, Thank you for the answer @DEEP.
	Sunny, 2019, Yes, you are right, thanks @Deep.
	Goutom Saikia, 2020, True level difference get when the level set midway between A & B.True level difference=1.22-1.06=.16 downward (Greater value give lower elevation so A is lower elevation than B).And when an instrument set near to a point collimation error eliminate.So we get true reading for  A.True reading at A=1.55.When instrument set near A then true reading at B=1.55-.16=1.39.Error=1.37-1.39=-.02.Error for 50 m=-0.02.Error for 100 m=2*(-.02)=-0.04.
	Abhijitth, 2021, Why are they not dividing by 2?(ha-hb)-(ha'-hb')/2.Isn't this the formula?
	Ankit, 2021, Agree @Abhijeet, why are they not dividing it by 2? Explain, please.
	Prince, 2021, @Abhijeet,This is the formula to be used in reciprocal levelling. Here the staff is kept at the centre first so the true difference between the elevations is the actual difference between them.
	Stagir, 2021, Thank you @Goutam.
	Karyeija Felex, 2021, @Ankit.The 1st setting is a two peg test which eliminates error thus giving true difference in level.We usually divide by two when the instrument is not at mid point so that errors on both BS and FS are eliminated.



Keeping the instrument height as 1  m, length of staff 4 m, the up gradient of the ground 1 in 10, the sight on the up slope must be less than
	Vinay, 2015, How to solve?

Two hill tops A and B 20 km apart are intervened by a third top C. If the top most contour of the three hill tops are of the same value, state whether the line of sight AB
	Saransh, 2014, It is given that hill tops of A&B are intervened y third top, but height of third top is not given.Therefore, m not able to understand how option B is correct.Question is not clear to me.
	Pavan, 2015, The answer should be C. In the question it is clearly. Defined that all the three hills top are occupied by contour of same value. Hence top point A, B and C are in the same line of sight.
	Snehangsu Bag, 2015, How answer would be 'B'?I think answer 'C' would be right.
	Anil, 2015, Line of sight will diverges downwards with distance. So B is the answer.
	Sammy, 2016, Hi, @Snehangsu.B is the correct answer because if the line of sight from A to B and C is the intervened point then obviously the line of sight passes below the hill top at C its because of the curvature of the earth.
	Dean Winchester, 2017, Yeah, good explanation @Sammy.
	Er Meghanada (Bhadrak.Odisha), 2017, Right answer @Sammy.
	Dennis Quansah, 2017, Thanks for the explanation @Sammy.
	Nayan, 2017, Well said @Sammy.
	Rishu, 2018, Is Earth Curvature considered when distance taking more than 250km?
	Henry, 2018, It could be due to curvature of earth surface as the line of sight seems to deviate upward.
	Anomii, 2018, Considering earth curvature, the intermittent point will be raised, line AB now being like a chord passes below the top of C.
	Atea, 2018, More than curvature it would be due to refraction.
	Chibi, 2019, More than 18km, We should consider the curvature of earth.
	Shubham, 2020, Hi. @Sammy, I think it is because of both due curvature & refraction effect. If we go only go with earth curvature effect the correction is negative (Cc=-.0785d2) it means the line of sight go above the hill top "c".and if we go with refraction effect than the correction is +tive (Cr=+(1/7)Cc) it means the line of sight gets deflected downward. For the above problem we consider both curvature & refraction effect which is (Cc+Cf)= -0.0673d2 it means the line sight gets deflected downward so the answer B is right.
	Akhilesh, 2020, Hi @Shubham what you said could also be wrong. The combined correction due to refraction and curvature is always negative indicating the value we got is more than the actual due to curvature of earth. So even if we consider both refraction and curvature;  the line of sight should be above C only. The logic we are missing is that if B is sighted from A, the line of sight will be obviously below the hill top. This is because in the given question it is mentioned, the top levels of all 3 hills are same. Now hill C is in between A and B. In brief, the distance between A and C is less than the distance between A and B. So accordingly, when you sight B it simply indicates that you are sighting relatively lower point than top level of C (the same reason you people told.. due to curvature of earth) so the line of sight will be obviously below C.
	Vishnu, 2020, Refraction and curvature effects should be considered when distance is above 500 metre (given 20 km).So, LOS pass below the top of C point.
	Shubham P, 2020, Answer @Akhilesh  Consider it practically, with total correction=Cc+Rc =(-) 26.94m.This is a correction for point B, means B will be around 27 meters below what we can see without considering hilltop C.Now if hilltop C is considered, then we can not sight B as C will be in between A&B actual level line.
	Irshad Ahmad, 2020, For long sights and accurate levelling work, the effects of the curvature of the earth and refraction of the line of sight shall have to be taken into consideration. The effect of curvature is greater than that of refraction, the combined effect causes the points to appear to be lower than they actually are.In this case distance between point A and B is more than that of Point A & C, the effect of the curvature of the earth and refraction of the line of sight is more in case of A & B compares to A and C, so point B will be lower than C and the line of sight from A to B passes below C.The Correct answer is option B.
	Pkota, 2021, Couldn't understand.In case the refraction effect taken into account for all hilltops of equal contour level then how can anybody sight at B as it is hidden by C itself.

Orientation of a plane table by solving two point problem is only adopted when
	Anand, 2019, Two points should be well-known points. i.e inaccessible.
	Prabhat, 2019, No, @Anand.Two points should be well-known points.i.e Whose position have already been plotted on the plan are selected.
	Pramod Tewari, 2020, D is the right answer.
	Shiva, 2021, Two Point Problem:.It is basically a resection method.Resection is a method of plane table surveying in which location of a plane table is unknown. Location of a plane table is determined by looking it to known points. It can be determined through:.1. Two-point problem.2. Three-point problem.The problem of finding the plotted position of the station point occupied by the plane table with the help of plotted positions of two well-defined points is known as solving a two-point problem.Hence, the orientation of plane-table, by solving the two-point problem, is adopted only when the given points are inaccessible.

Measuring with a 30 m chain, 0.01 m too short, introduces
	Danesh, 2014, If chain length is shorter than the original length then its +ive cumulative error in chaining. And if length of chain is longer than -ive cumulative error.

	Jack, 2016, Kindly verify.The error in chaining is -ve if the measured length is less than actual length.Suppose, the actual distance is 30m.And the chain is elongated by 1m.The measured distance will be 29m.The error will be 29 - 30= -1m.i.e negative error.
	Jayabrata Ghosh, 2017, Answer should be B. Because for negative error the measured length is less than actual length. And a cumulative error is proportional to L whereas compensating error is proportional to root L.
	Nitya, 2017, Compensating error removes self after completion of work or can say no effect on work.
	Harish, 2017, Answer is right.Tape measurers 30m while having a error 0.01m short, that mean the actual measured length is 29.99m.So positive cumulative error.
	Brijesh, 2017, If 20 chain is too short by 1m.the supposed it measures 40m(it is measured value),true value because chain is short=(40-2=38),error=M.V-T.V.         =40-38,          =+2.

The vertical angle between longitudinal axis of a freely suspended magnetic needle and a horizontal line at its pivot, is known
	Ogundele Dare, 2016, Magnetic dip, dip angle, or magnetic inclination is the angle made with the horizontal by the Earth's magnetic field lines. This angle varies at different points on the Earth's surface. Positive values of inclination indicate that the magnetic field of the Earth is pointing downward, into the Earth, at the point of measurement, and negative values indicate that it is pointing upward. The dip angle is in principle the angle made by the needle of a vertically held compass, though in practice ordinary compass needles may be weighted against dip or may be unable to move freely in the correct plane.

A dumpy level is set up with its eye-piece vertically over a peg A. The height from the top of peg A to the centre of the eye-piece is 1.540 m and the reading on peg B is 0.705 m. The level is then setup over B. The height of the eye-piece above peg B is 1.490 m and a reading on A is 2.195 m. The difference in level between A and B is
	Suresh Awasthi, 2015, 1. 1.540 m - 0.705 m = 0.835 m.2. 2.195 m - 1.490 m = 0.705 m.Now,Difference in level between A and B = (0.835 + 0.705)/2.= 0.770 m.
	Leon, 2015, Can you explain to me why did you divide by 2? According to me it can either be 0.835 m or 0.705 m?
	Darshan H A, 2015, Apply reciprocal leveling formula. Dividing 2 is done to eliminate error due to curvature and refraction.
	Bonnykumar, 2016, It is a theory of reciprocal levelling.
	Mahesh, 2016, Yes @Leon.It is 0.705 m.
	Pusp, 2016, Average is taken to eliminate an error. Because if there was no error then 0.835 m and 0.705 m would be equal.
	Shruti, 2017, Please explain the answer with relevant formulas.
	Hilly Kashyap, 2018, The true difference of level between A And B is;e= [(1.540-0.705)+(2.195-1.490)]/2 =0.770.
	Bharat Chaudhary, 2018, Yes, right @Suresh @Kashyap.
	Vimo, 2020, Yes, here we can apply the reciprocal levelling formula. Agree @Darshan.

Prismatic compass is considered more accurate than a surveyor's compass, because
	Ogundele Dare, 2016, The main difference between the two instruments is that the surveyor's compass is usually the larger and more accurate instrument, and is generally used on a stand or tripod. The prismatic compass, on the other hand, is often a small instrument which is held in the hand for observing and is therefore employed in the rougher classes of work. The graduations on this prismatic compass are situated on a light aluminium ring fastened to the needle, and the zero of the graduations coincides with the south point of the needle. The graduations, therefore, remain stationary with the needle, and the index turns with the sighting vanes. Since the circle is read at the observer's (rather than the target's) end, the graduations run clockwise from the south end of the needle (0° to 360°), whereas, in the surveyor's compass, the graduations run anti-clockwise from the north.The prismatic attachment consists of a 45° reflecting prism with the eye and reading faces made slightly convex so as to magnify the image of the graduations. This might be helpful for better understanding.
	Balveersingh, 2017, I think Option E is correct.
	Amit, 2017, Option E is correct.
	Garry, 2018, Yes E is correct answer.Because both these parameters helps in taking errorless readings.
	Sam, 2019, But surveyor compass more accurate then prismatic compass.
	Dhlora Wilson, 2019, Yes, option E is correct.
	Ashu, 2019, Graduation is in WCB does not mean that this is more accurate.
	P Koley, 2019, I also agree with option E.
	Sumit, 2019, Yes, E is correct one.
	Himan Dutta, 2021, I think Option E is correct.

The main plate of a transit is divided into 1080 equal divisions. 60 divisions of the vernier coincide exactly with 59 divisions of the main plate. The transit can read angles accurate upto
	Suresh Awasthi, 2015, 1080/60 = 18.According to the answer sheet answer is 20.
	Arvin Sethi, 2015, Least count is equal to ratio of (length of one division on main scale to no. of divisions of vernier) i.e least count= s/n.S = 360/1080.N = 60.Lc = 1/180 degrees = 20 seconds.
	Ansha, 2016, Please explain clearly from where 360 came.
	Parth, 2016, @Ansha.The total degree of rotation =360°.
	Kislay, 2016, Thank you all for explaining it.
	Jack, 2016, Least count = length of one division on the main scale/ no. of divisions of vernier.Legth of one division on main scale= 360/1080.= 0.3333333 degrees,= 0.3333333 x 3600 sec,= 1200 sec.No of divisions of vernier = 60.Therefore, Least count= 1200/60 = 20.
	Dinesh Prasad Sao, 2017, (1080/60) - (1080/59)= .3°=20".
	Lokesh Meena, 2017, Least Count = S/n.S = Least count of main scale = 360/1080n = no of divion on vernier scale = 60In transit main plate their is circle scale with 360 degreeSo S=360/1080 = 1/3 Now least count = 1/3 &div; 60 = 1/180 degree Convert in secondsL.C = 1/180 *60*60 =20 seconds.
	Subham, 2017, Total angle 360. Dividing in 1080 part.So 1 part in main scale = 1/3 degree or 20'.1 part of main scale dividing in vernier 60 partSo 1 part in vernier = 20'/60 = 1/3' = 20"
	Shubham Vighne, 2017, Least count = S - V.Where S is the length of one division  of main scale which is nothing but least count of main scale and V is the length of one division of vernier scale.From the above problem we can conclude that (n-1) division of main scale is divided into n division of vernier scale because 60 divisions of vernier coincide exactly with 59 divisions of the main scale.Therefore, n*V=(n-1)*S ------- (A)Now, S= L.C of main scale = 360/1080 = 1/3 degrees because in transit main plate there is a circle with 360 degrees. S= (1/3) degrees = (1/3)* 3600 sec= 1200 seconds.Now from (A) 60*V=59*1200                              V=1080 seconds. Therefore Least Count = S - V = 1200 - 1080 = 20 seconds.
	Srk, 2018, An angle-measuring instrument reading up to one-sixth of a degree on the main scale is equipped with a vernier having 19 main scale divisions divided into 20 parts. The correct least count for the instrument is 30 seconds.The Least Count=(1/6 *)/20    =>1/6*60*60  sec/20 =30" ans.

If a tacheometer is fitted with an anal-latic lens
	Ogundele D., 2016, The formula most widely used for finding the distances is:d = k  * s  +  c.Here, s is the stadia interval (top intercept minus bottom intercept); k and c are additive and multiplicative constants. If the radius of a simple curve is 600 m, the maximum length of the chord for calculating offsets, is taken
	Dev, 2016, Anyone can explain?
	N.P, 2016, @Dev.Length = r/20 = 600/20 = 30.
	Daffy, 2016, Please explain the formula clearly.
	Roshika Bhattarai, 2016, If the radius of a simple curve is R, the length of the chord for calculating offsets by the method of chords produced, should not exceed R/20.
	Annie, 2017, Well said @Roshika.
	Taba Amith, 2017, Nice explanation. Thanks.
	Md Firoj, 2018, Please derive the formula R/20.
	Kaushik Kanji, 2018, Chord length for curve setting is derived from the formula of errors. The greater chord length we take, the greater be the chances of error in measurement because while using the method 'OFFSET FROM CHORD PRODUCED' we take the chord distances whereas we consider them as straight lines. You will see while taking the first chord in the method, we mention that Chord length~arc length. Now, if we use R/10, the error is 1 in 2500, which is pretty significant. But if we use R/20, the error is 1 in 10, 10000 which is considerably low and cannot be seen or detected from a human perspective. Hence R/20 is the correct value.
	Roy, 2018, R/20 is absolutely right.
	Fahad, 2018, Thanks to all for explaining the answer.
	Safikul, 2020, Can you please help me to understand @Fahad.
	Teja, 2020, Thanks @Kaushik Kanji.
	Arshad Ch, 2020, L = r/20.

A standard steel tape of length 30 m and cross-section 15 x 1.0 mm was standardised at 25°C and at 30 kg pull. While measuring a base line at the same temperature, the pull applied was 40 kg. If the modulus of elasticity of steel tape is 2.2 x 106 kg/cm2, the correction to be applied is
	Jay Ghosh, 2015, Applied pull 40 kg and standardized pull 30 kg. So value of correction is +0.000909 m and answer is (C).
	Ajay, 2016, Correction = (P-p) * L/A * E => (40-30) * 30/15 * 2.2 * 10^6.
	Satish, 2016, But in my point of view c option is correct because pm >po then correction is positive. Can anyone agree with me?
	Ajmal Khan, 2016, No, @Satish the answer A is correct because the standard pull is 30 Kg and applied pull is 40 Kg, so the tape will measure long distance than actual, so the final result must be subtracted from measured distance. That's why it is negative one.
	Rk, 2016, I agree with @Satish as the length of the tape is more so measures length will less so correction will be less.
	Apcivil@N, 2016, It should be C as P is greater than Po +ve is the answer.
	B N Yadav, 2016, (Pm-Po)L/AE.If Pm is greater than Po, the correction will be positive  ie. +0.000909 m.
	Abhishek Anand, 2017, Ans must be C.As the applied pull is more than std pull so the length of tape increases.Hence the distance measured becomes less than actual.Correction = +'ve   if app>std pull.Correction = - ve    if app<std pull.Cp=(P-Ps)L/AE;    P=app pull.Ps=std pull.
	Rajesh, 2017, When pull the tape more than standard then the measurement will be less than 30m. So sure the correction to be added to measured length.

	Sahil Chavda, 2021, Concept:Line of sight: The sighting or pointing line of a telescope, defined by the optical centre of the objective and the intersection of crosshairs.Line of collimation: It is an imaginary line passing through the optical centre of the objective glass and its continuation.Axis of Telescope: The axis is an imaginary line passing through the optical centre of the object-glass and the optical centre of the eye-peace.Axis of the Bubble Tube: It is an imaginary line tangential to the longitudinal curve of the bubble tube at its middle point.So the given answer is wrong and the Correct answer is B.

In a closed traverse, sum of south latitudes exceeds the sum of north latitudes and the sum of east departures exceeds the sum of west departures, then, the closing line will lie in
	Jagrut, 2016, Please give me the reason.

	Ankit, 2017, I think the answer is South-East Quadrant.
	Vikas Saini, 2017, The answer is s-e quardant.
	Anil, 2017, Can anyone explain why SE?
	Mit, 2017, The sum of S is > then  Sum of N ---- > so in south. The  sum of E is > then sum of W ----> so in east.  So south - East  ( 4th quadrant).
	Lalan, 2017, Yes, south-east is right.
	Lalan, 2017, Here the sum of south latitude is more so projection will toward south. And similarly in the east direction. , and by trigonometry. If two component in east and south direction then. Line direction will be SE.
	Vikrant, 2018, South East is right.
	Josh, 2018, I think NW is correct.  Let's call A the actual closure point and A' the measured closure point.  Yes, A' is south and east of A.  But that's not what the question is asking. The question is asking which quadrant the closing line will be in.  The closing line is drawn from A' to A so it is drawn north and west of point A'.  Answer (A).
	Gayatri, 2018, Yes this answer is right. Means lies in N-W quadrant. Because whenever we draw the line, the point we get in quadrant SE, this is the initial point of the closing line. But it should be in origin. Draw the line to the origin then it will be NW quadrant.
	Praveen Pathhi, 2018, N>sW>E(+,-).3rd quadrant, NW.
	Anik Das, 2018, I also think that it's NW quadrant.
	Krishna Kant, 2018, It is South - East.
	Himanshu Gupta, 2018, Yes, given answer is correct, the question is asking "closing line ill lie in".
	Himal Rawal, 2019, South-East is right.
	Kuldip, 2019, S-E quadrant is the right answer.
	Saurabh, 2019, L=N-S   S=1+N.L=-1.D=E-D  E=1+W.D=1.(-,+) 4TH co-ordinate N-W.
	Nikhilesh, 2019, Closing error is given by: ΣL south  -  ΣL north  = 0.ΣD east  -  ΣD west  = 0. So Closing error will lie in the southeast quadrant.
	Rohan, 2020, Southeast is the answer!
	Akbar Merani, 2020, I think it's south east.
	Amrit, 2020, The right answer will be southeast. I agreed @Mit.
	Anil, 2020, I agree, the correct answer is south-east.
	Shubham P, 2020, I think the answer is correct, i.e.Closing line lies in NW quadrant here!For understanding this you need to know the closing point and closing line are two different terms. As per regular observation, we can say the closing point is in SE quadrant as (-L, +D).But the question asks about the closing line, not the closing point. The closing line is considered with respect to closing point as origin. Because closure point is supposed as an accurate position of traverse so a reference station also.Hence the closing line here will be lie in NW quadrant wrt Closure point.
	Kittu Lucky, 2020, South -East is the correct answer.
	Uday Prakash, 2020, According to me, the Answer is South East.
	Fahad, 2020, Thanks @Shubham.

	Rahul Sharma, 2021, Agree with @Shubham.
	Mjm Gcek, 2021, Agree @Subham.
	John, 2021, A is correct. The closing line lies in NW.



A sewer is laid from a manhole A to a manhole B, 250 m away along a gradient of 1 in 125. If the reduced level of the invert at A is 205.75 m and the height of the boning rod is 3 m, the reduced level of the sight rail at B, is
	Praveen, 2017, Can you explain it?
	Vinay, 2017, Answer C. 206.75.205.75 - 1*250/125 = 206.75 m.
	Atul Patel, 2017, 205.75 - 1 * 205/125 = 203.75,203.75 + 3 = 206.75.
	Arun, 2017, Why added 3 to 203.75?
	Karthik, 2017, @Arun3 is the height of boring rod.
	Piku, 2017, 205.75-(1 * 250/150)+3 = 206.75.
	Gaurav, 2018, Gradient is downward.
	Chadha, 2018, 205.75 - (250*1/125-3)= 206.75.
	Rajib Kumar, 2019, Please explain the solution.
	Mahesh, 2019, RL of invert at B.= 205.75 - (250 &div; 125) = 203.75.RL of sight at B = 203.75+3 = 206.75.
	Sophia, 2020, Please can someone explain why you're dividing it by 125?
	Xavi, 2020, @Sophia.The distance between A and B is 250m and B is at a lower level, so in layman terms, for every 125m distance there is a 1m decrease in RL, thus for 250m distance there will be 2m decrease in RL. In mathematical terms, they are simply dividing 250/125 to obtain 2.
	Ajay Kumar Yadav, 2020, 205.75 - (250/125) + 3 =206.75.
	Dawood Khan, 2021, Thankyou for the explanation @Xavi.
	Nabin, 2021, Invert is the lowest point of sewer and crown is the highest point, therefore, 205.75-2=203.75 is the invert of sewer B. Boning rod is T shaped rod with a vertical portion at invert and a horizontal portion at the crown. So when the boning rod reading is 3 it gives the crown reading of sewer B. Therefore RL of B =invert RL of B + boning rod height =(203.75+3) = 206.75.

If the length of a transition curve to be introduced between a straight and a circular curve of radius 500 m is 90 m, the maximum deflection angle to locate its junction point, is
	Armaan, 2015, Would you like to explain it?
	Mayank, 2015, Its 90C/(pie*R) = 90*90/(pie*500) and divide it by 189 to covert into degrees = 1^43^13^.
	Xyz, 2016, Can you please explain how to convert degree into degree, minutes, seconds in a scientific calculator?
	Rijaz, 2016, Please explain the answer.
	Amdahl, 2016, Anyone give me the correct explanation.
	Zeshan, 2017, The Maximum deflection angle of transition curve (in minutes)= (573.25*L^2)/(R*L).Therefore = (573.25*90*90)/(500*90).Answer = 103.18 minutes.Convert to degrees = 1° 43' 18".(1°= 60' ,1'= 60").
	Rajeshkumar, 2017, Option A is correct  (L2/6RL) * 180/π.
	Karan, 2018, Here R=90 and L= 500 is given then how can this answer is possible? Please explain the answer in detail.
	Adam Basha, 2019, L2/6RL. 500^2/(6*500*90)*(180). =1'43'7.14".
	Shivaraj Babar, 2020, ((573*90)/(500*60))=1°43'08".
	Er.Muskan, 2021, LENGTH OF CURVE= π * R * D/180.
	Dawood Khan, 2021, @Zeshan. How come 573.25?
	Roy Basak, 2021, CORRECT ANSWER IS A - 1°43"08'  -Cofirmed from textbookExplanation:Max Deflection angle = L^2/6RL (this is in radians),So in degrees, it will be =L ^2/6RL [180/π],In minutes it will be       = 1800 [L^2/RL].Thus applying this formula we get max Deflection angle = 1°43"08'.(For those asking where the 573 in the above explanations came from its simply 180/π =573).
	Roy Basak, 2021, CORRECT ANSWER IS A - 1°43"08'  -Cofirmed from textbookExplanation:Max Deflection angle = L^2/6RL (this is in radians),So in degrees, it will be =L ^2/6RL [180/π],In minutes it will be       = 1800 [L^2/RL].Thus applying this formula we get max Deflection angle = 1°43"08'.(For those asking where the 573 in the above explanations came from its simply 180/π =573).

The sag of 50 m tape weighing 4 kg under 5 kg tension is roughly
	Madhukar, 2015, What is the formula was used for calculation?
	Sandeep, 2016, Please give the formula, to solve the question.

	Hasan, 2017, Please, someone explain it clearly, I am not getting the exact answer.

	Harish, 2017, (LW^2/24*10^4)*P^2.((50*4^2)/(24*10^4))*5^2 = 0.0833.Ans is right.
	Neelam, 2017, In formula why you multiply 24 with 10^4?
	Jay Panchal, 2017, In formula why you multiply 24 with 10^4?
	Rafi, 2018, Can you please explain @ Harish,  Why you multiplied 10^4 with 24?
	Munesh Kumar Meena, 2018, Tell me the right formula.
	Suhasish Munshi, 2018, The correct steps:(W^2 * L)&div;(24 *P^2).W=weight of tape in kg/m,L=length of tape in m,P= pull applied or tension in kg,So, W= 5kg/50m= 0.1kg/m.L=50m and P=5kg.
	Prity, 2018, Why you multiplying with P^2 in W? Please explain the formula.
	Ahmed Mohamed Adan, 2018, Given:(W)^2=(4kg )^2 = 16kg.L=50. p=5^2=25.         Sag correction = 16*50/24*25.800/600 = -1.33ft.Note: Sag correction is Always negative.
	Vaish, 2019, ((4/50)^2 * 50)/(24*5^2) = 0.0833m.Y (4/50)----->w is the weight per meter length.
	Rani, 2019, (5-4)^2*50/(24*5^2) = .0833.
	Rishabh Purohit, 2019, Correction for sag = W^2*L / 24*P^2 , where w is in kg,N,kN etc.Correction for sag = w^2*L^3 / 24*P^2 , where w is in kg/m, N/m etc.
	Johny Chauhan, 2019, .083 m is not the correct answer for given data. Here, Given data will be same except W= 1kgSo, ( 1kg)^2 * (50 m) /[ 24 * ( 5kg)^2]= .083 m.

A dumpy level was set up at mid-point between pegs A and B, 80 m apart and the staff readings were 1.32 and 1.56. When the level was set up at a point 10 m from A on BA produced, the staff readings obtained at A and B were 1.11 and 1.39. The correct staff reading from this set up at S should be
	Satish, 2016, How to get this answer? Please explain it.
	Aamir, 2017, The difference in level b/w A and B:1.56-1.32=.24.Apparent difference = 1.39-1.11 = .28,True difference = 1.11 + .24 = 1.35,Collimation error in 80m = 1.39-1.35 = 0.04.Collimation error in 90m=(.04 * 90)&div;80 = .045m.Nd correction =.045m.Correct staff reading on B=1.390-.045=1.345m.
	Ishfaq Ahmad Sofi, 2017, For mid point station { 1.56-1.32=.24} for second station {1.39-1.11=.28}.The difference in reading shows collimation error , which is .28-.24=.04,.04 is collimation error in 80 m.therefore in 90m  = (.04/80)*90=.045,Now correct reading at B = 1.39 - .045 = 1.345.
	Gaurav, 2018, Correct reading is required at B.
	Josh, 2018, What is S here? Please give details.
	Karan, 2018, Here the sum of 1.39+0.045 is wrong it should be 1.435.
	Gagan, 2018, 1.32+1.56=2.88/2=1.44,1.11+1.39=2.50/2=1.25,Now 1.44+1.25=2.69/2= 1.345 ans.
	Shubh, 2018, What is S? Please explain it.
	Balvinder, 2019, Right, thanks @Gagan.
	Aman, 2019, How we take 90? Please tell me.
	Er. Ujjwal Simkhada, 2020, Initial distance (80m) +new distance near by A9 (i.e10m) = 90m.
	Abhijitth, 2021, Do they mean 10m away from A. i.e. the instrument is not lying in between A and B?Anyone explain.
	Nabin, 2021, @Abhijit.It says on BA produced so it is 10 m away from A.



The bearing of C from A is N 30° E and from B, 50 metres east of A, is N 60° W. The departure of C from A is
	Ganesh, 2016, 50 COS 30 = 25.So, it is correct answer.
	Suhas, 2016, Agree with you @Ganesh.
	Vatsal, 2016, 50 SIN 30 = 25 for departure.
	Civilian, 2016, Departure is L Sin θ.
	Faze, 2017, d = 50sin30 = 25m.
	Rajendra, 2017, 50 m is the AB how taken we In case of AC?
	Rahul Sovanshi, 2018, You are right @Civillian.
	Tanmoy, 2018, L sin α = 50 * sin 30 = 25 m.
	Philippines, 2019, I think the answer is 12.5m.The given distance 50m is for line AB , so you cannot use it directly to solve for departure on line CA.So using the triangle ABC , given the length of AB=50m and the angles can be computed using the given bearings. The side AC can be computed and AC=25m.Then, the departure of line AC is 25sin(30)= 12.5m.
	Kcube, 2019, I agree with you @Philippines.
	Indu, 2020, Yes, the answer is 12.5 and I agree @Philippines.
	Vimo, 2020, Right @@Philippines.B is 50 m east of A, here departure of C with respect to A has to be found hence RB is 30, so sin 30 is correct, but L is not 50. L has to be distance AC, which is from calculation we get it as 25m.

	Pkota, 2021, When we draw triangle ABC, we found angle A,B,C 60,30,90 and AB=50m.By applying geometry AC = ABcos60---> (1)And departure of AC will be ACcos60 ---> (2).From (1) put value of AC in (2) we got;Departure of AC= (AB cos60) cos60.50 x 0.5 x 0.5 = 12.5m.

If the long chord and tangent length of a circular curve of radius R are equal the angle of deflection, is
	Shahid Malik, 2017, Anyone explain this question clearly.
	Jeet Beniwal, 2017, Long chord = 2*Rsinθ/2,Tangent = Rtanθ/2.Equating both, we get,θ/2 = 60.θ =120.
	Ngm, 2018, How to comes  (θ/2)?
	Ngm, 2018, How to get 60?
	Josh, 2018, If LC = T, then you have an equilateral triangle: 60-60-60. The deflection angle at the PI is simply 180 - interior angle 60 = 120.
	Masood, 2020, Thanks @Jeet Beniwal.
	Abdul Aziz, 2020, Long chord = 2R*sinθ/2, and,For Tangent length= R* tanθ/2.A/c to question,  Long chord = tangent length.2R * sinθ/2  = R * tanθ/2.=> 2 sinθ/2 = (sinθ/2 ÷cosθ/2)=> 2 = 1÷ cosθ/2. => cosθ/2 = 1/2  = cos60°=> θ/2 = 60.=> θ =120.
	Waqas, 2021, Thanks @Abdul Aziz.
	John, 2021, Thanks @Abdul Aziz.

A level when set up 25 m from peg A and 50 m from peg B reads 2.847 on a staff held on A and 3.462 on a staff held on B, keeping bubble at its centre while reading. If the reduced levels of A and B are 283.665 m and 284.295 m respectively, the collimation error per 100 m is
	Idowu S A, 2015, Actual height difference between A and B = 284.295 - 283.665 = 0.63.Height difference between A and B based on staff readings = 3.462 - 2.847 = 0.615.Distance between A and B = 50-25 = 25 m.Therefore collimation error per 100 m.= (0.63 - 0.615)*(100/25).= 0.060 m.

If 50 m point of a 100 m tape is 50 cm off line, and 50 m sections are straight, an error is generated equal to
	Muhammad Ashraf, 2016, Can any one explain?
	Sundaes, 2016, Please explain it.
	Nawa Raj Budha, 2016, Please explain the solution.
	Samm, 2016, True length upto 50m portion is √(50^2 + 0.5^2)  = 50.0025.So error is 50 - 50.025 = 0.025 m in 50 m length.    50 m length error is 0.025 m.So in 100 m length error is 0.025 * 2 = 0.05 m, So error is  0.05/100 = 1/20,000.
	M Zahir, 2018, What is true length?
	Lalit Roy, 2019, True length upto 50 m portion is  √50^2+0.5^2 = 50.0025.Error = 50.0025-50= 0.0025.for 100 m length error = 0.0025*2=0.0050.Error for 100 m= 0.005/100=1/20000.
	Anjana, 2019, Yes @lalit is right.
	Yogi, 2020, The answer should be 1/40000.For 50m length is 50.025,And next 50 m is straight so it is 0.025/100.
	Civima, 2021, Error =h^2/2*L.For L= 50m and h= 0.5m , the error = 1/400.Therefore error per unit length of 50m (as the rest part of the chain is straight) equals  1/(400*50) = 1/20,000.
	John, 2021, Thanks @Civima.

The constant vertical distance between two adjacent contours, is called
	Ankush Narwal, 2018, Vertical distance between two contour is known Contour interval and horizontal distance between two contours is known Contour gradient.

The correction to be applied to each 30 metre chain length along θ° slope, is
	Sumit, 2017, How it is possible? Explain.
	Prashant, 2017, Anyone explain this answer.
	Dilip, 2017, Slope correction is = L(cosθ-1).Can you explain how this is wrong?
	Manish, 2017, The correct answer is Angle with θ l (1-cosθ).
	Ajaynakum, 2017, Slope correction = L (1-cosθ).Then A is correct answer.
	Nikhitha, 2017, How A will be correct? I think C is correct.
	Akash, 2018, Actual slope correction is always -ve therefore, Correction = -[L(1-cosX)].=(LcosX-L).=L(cosX-1) ; now L is 30m chain.So the answer comes out to be; =30(cosX-1) m.
	Akash, 2018, So, the correct option has to be "C"30(cosX-1)m.
	Jitendra Kumar, 2018, We know that formula for determining.L(1-cosθ) we also know that cosθ=1/secθThen     L(1-1/secθ)    L(secθ-1) Here L=30.Hence;30(secθ-1).So this is the Right answer.
	Shailendra, 2018, @Jitendra Kumar.Option C is correct.
	Prabil, 2018, @Jitendra KumarL{(secθ -1)/secθ}. So, Option C is correct.
	Gyan, 2018, The correction for chain length is L(secθ -1).
	Kiran, 2019, Please explain it @Gyan.
	Nungshi Jr, 2019, Hypotenusal allowance = chain length (secθ-1).
	Nungshi Jr, 2019, Hypotenusal allowance = chain length (secθ-1).
	James Bond, 2019, L'=30m, l=L' cosθ.So, (L'- l) = L' - L' cosθ= L'(1 - cosθ) = 30(1-cosθ)  it's answer C.
	Subha, 2019, What is Hypotenusal allowance @Nungshi jr.
	Adnan, 2019, L(secθ-1) is correct because ;SLOP .  L(1- cos0).1-cos0 ------ gives eq 1 eg,Now, cos0 = 1/sce0.= 1-1/sec0.= sec0-1/sec0 ------ eq 2.COMPARE eq 1, eq2.= sec0(1-cos0)= sec0-1= solve LHSsec0(1-1/sec0)We get  sec0 -1.
	Raaga Chakradhar, 2020, Slope correction is=L(cos0-1).But here the question is not about slope correction it is asking about hypotension allowance i,e the correction required to be applied for the given chain length for a given slope.So, in indirect chaining hypotension allowance is=L(sec0-1).
	Madhu, 2021, Option (A) is correct. The question is asking about hypotenusal allowance.I will explain. This is a bit lengthy. But since there is no option for uploading a figure, I have to type this much. So please read carefully.For surveying the distance we need is the horizontal distance. But for sloping grounds,  we can't directly measure the horizontal distance. So we will measure the sloping distance and apply the correction (by knowing the slope). We are doing it  (i) either by measuring the full sloping distance once and applying the correction at the office later by using trigonometric relations, (ii) or we will apply corrections at each chain length so that we don't have to do any corrections at the office. Keep in mind this much.Here we are using the second method.Suppose we are measuring a single chain distance L from A to B along a slope of 'theta' degree,  AB = L metre. The TRUE horizontal distance for this AB is Lcos(theta), which will be less than L metre. So what we do is we will mark a point B' whose TRUE length is L metre, which is ahead of actual measured point B. Then we will start the next chain from the point B' instead of B.For that,  we take Lm as the horizontal distance and find the sloping distance for that. AB'cos (θ) = LAB' = L /cos(θ),AB' = L sec (θ).Hypotenusal allowance (HA) = the distance BB' we have to mark ahead of B so that the measured length along the slope is the true horizontal distance. HA = AB - AB'       = L.sec(θ) - L,       = L [sec (θ) - 1].
	Shubham, 2021, We know that slope correction is L(1-cosθ) and this is only correct..If you take sec then where that denominator will go then? Please anyone explain me.
	Ashish, 2021, @Madhu.I agree with your answer. Great thanks.
	Tofik, 2021, @Madhu.AB not equal L it Lcosθ.

The staff reading at a distance of 80 m from a level with the bubble at its centre is 1.31 m. When the bubble is moved by 5 divisions out of the centre, the reading is 1.39 m. The angular value of the one division of the bubble, is
	Jay Ghosh, 2015, 206265 = s/d.s = 1.39-1.31 = 0.08 m/5 div.= (206265*0.08)/80.= 206.265 sec/5 div.= (206.265*1)/5.= 41.253 sec/1 div.Answer B.
	Shubham, 2017, How 206.265? @Jay Ghosh.

	Amy, 2019, Thanks @Gaurav.
	Gurdarshan Singh, 2020, Please answer with full explanation.
	Shubham P, 2020, We know angular value, α =( S/nD) * 206265.Here.S = Staff intercept = 1.39 - 1.31 = 0.08 meter.n = number of division given for change in reading = 5 divisions.D = linear distance between instrument and Staff = 80 meter.& multiplied by 206265 to convert an angular value from radian to seconds.Put the values and answer will be 41.253 seconds per division.

	Rohit, 2018, How is it possible?Because a line can show one thing at a time.

If deflection angles are measured in a closed traverse, the difference between the sum of the right-hand and that of the left hand angles should be equal to
	Pratim Pal, 2015, Please tell me the discussion of that question.
	Kunti, 2018, 360°-0°=360°.
	Yeshay, 2018, How explain?
	Ashu, 2019, 180 left hand + 180 on right hand = 360°.
	Deepanshu, 2020, In closed traverse, the summation of all deflection angle is 360 degree.
	Madhu, 2020, What is the open traverse of deflection angle?
	Prince, 2021, @Deepanshu,Isn't it a summation but here they are asking difference, please explain?
	Dani 273, 2021, I think 180° is correct.
	Atta Ur Rahman, 2021, The summation is 0 and the difference is 360. We consider left-hand angles as negative and right-hand angles as positive. So, obviously, their algebraic summation is 0 and their algebraic difference is 360.
	Zakir Hussain, 2021, In the Clockwise direction, the summation of deflection angles gives the sum of exterior angles and in an anti-clockwise direction, interior angles.So, the difference comes out to be 360°.Sum of exterior angles-sum of interior angles of any closed traverse=360°.

Contours of different elevations may cross each other only in the case of
	Shivam, 2017, Please describe this question.
	Megha Manjunatha, 2018, 1. Contour lines generally do not meet or intersect each other. If contour lines are meeting in some portion, it shows the existence of a vertical cliff.2. Contours of different elevations cannot cross each other. If contour lines cross each other, it shows the existence of overhanging cliffs or a cave.
	Dipesh Yadav, 2020, The contour line cannot cross each other except in the case of the overhanging cliff and vertical cliff. But in case of overhanging cliff, contour line may overlap and in case of vertical cliff, contour line unite at a point.

	Mahima Chandel, 2014, C= 2R*sin{(1/2)*I}.Where C= length of long chord.c=30 , R=300.=>(30/600)= sin{(1/2)*I}.=>(1/20) = sin{(1/2)*I}.=>I = 2*{sin^-1(1/20)}.=>I = 5.73 Degree.

The bearing of line AB is 152° 30' and angle ABC measured clockwise is 124° 28'. The bearing of BC is
	Karthikranga, 2014, How you get this answer can you explain the procedure for this?
	Dheeraj, 2014, Back bearing of ab line is 332 degree 30 min. Therefore angle reduced bearing with north is 360 minus above bearing which is 27 degrees 30 min. Then subtract this from above angle that is 124 degree 28 min minus 27 degree 30 min gives you answer.
	Juliet Osunde, 2014, Bearing of AB =  152° 30'Angle at B = 124°  28' The bearing of BC=?Back bearing= forward bearing + included angle,CB=152 30' + 124 28'= 276 58`. Since we asked to find the forward bearing i.e BC,BC= 276 58` - 180 = 96 58'.
	Mr. Salim Mulla, 2016, 152° 30'+ 180° = 332°30'.360° - 332° 30' = 27° 30°.Then do 124° 28' - 27° 30' = 96° 58'.Hope you understand.
	Sameer, 2018, Thanks a lot for explaining @Mr Salim.
	Alok, 2019, I am not getting the solution. Please, anyone explain me.
	Vikas Dubey, 2019, Bearing of any line = BB.of line + angle.If angle is more than 180' than, -360'.You will find the answer.
	Aswathy, 2019, Forward Bearing of AB =152°30'Back Bearing of AB= 180°-152°30=27°30'FB of BC= Included angle - BB of AB                =124°28'-27°30'                =96°58'.
	Adeel Rehman, 2020, Best Explanation, Thanks @Julliet Osunde.
	Burhan Ali, 2020, FB of AB=152°30'BB of AB=152°30 ' +180° = 332°30'FB of BC = BB of AB at B=332 °30' + 124°28' = 456°58'.= 456°58' - 360° = 96°58'(remember if angle is more then 360° Subtract 360°).
	Kanchan Thakur, 2020, Bearing of AB=152°30'<ABC=124°28'Bearing of BC=?Back bearing of AB = BA = F.B + 180 = 152°30' + 180° = 332°30'Bearing of bc = b.b of AB+<ABC = 332°30' + 124°28' = 456°58'This is more than 360 so; Bearing of BC = 456°58' - 360° = 96°58'.
	Kripa, 2020, Then for angle AC?
	Manvi, 2020, Please explain, how to calculate the angle AC?
	Prashanth, 2021, For anticlockwise how to measure it?

	Prakash Singh Thakuri, 2021, Fore bearing BC =  back bearing AB + clockwise Angle ±180° ±540°.BC = 152°30'+124°28'±180°±540°.BC = 276°58'-180°.BC = 96°58'.Fore bearing BC=96°58' answer.
	Philologue, 2021, Bearing of AB=152°30'.Angle ABC = 124°28'.Bearing BC=?Bearing BA = AB + 180 = 152°30'+180°= 332°30'. Bearing BC= AB + angle ABC = 332°30' + 124°28' = 456°58'.Then 456°58'-360°= 96°58'.

Correction per chain length of 100 links along a slope of α radians, is
	Navern, 2015, Would you explain?
	Hoddy, 2016, Can anyone explain it?
	Sathyadallu, 2017, Give me the Explanation.
	Kaushik Das, 2017, How?
	Amal Zad Khan, 2017, Please explain it.
	Nayan, 2017, Correction is = 100(sec a-1).
	Md Shekhar, 2017, Slope correction=h^2/2L;where h=L*angle in radian(a),Here L=100 links,So, slope correction=100*a^2.
	Deepa, 2017, @Md Shekhar.Then it will be 50a^2, right?
	Garry, 2018, Agree @ Deepa.The answer should be 50 a^2.
	Er Bibek, 2018, I agree on @Garry and @Deepa.It should be 50a^2.
	Sandeep Singh, 2018, 100a^2&div;2 = 50a^2.
	Sameer Sopori, 2018, ch= s(1-cosθ)ch= s- s cosθch= s - dBecause d= s. cosθ. For example: s=100, slope angle 45 degreed= 100 . 0.70710d= 70.71      THIS IS THE CORRECTION OF SLOPE LENGTH. ch= 100-70.71. ch= 29.29. Ch is the correction of measured slope distance due to slope;d is the horizontal distance.
	Prajwol, 2018, @Sameer Sopori.Please explain how come 100 α?
	Sajith Ca, 2018, Please explain the answer.
	Biswajit Koley, 2019, Some book answered it 1.5a^2/100.How it's come please explain?
	Shaa, 2019, @Biswajit Kiley. Your answer is correct for slope in degree. Here slope in radian.
	Blaski, 2020, In degree : (1.5a^2)/100.In radian : 100a.
	Kashyap, 2020, Please give me the correct answer.
	Rupesh Kumar Verma, 2020, Given data Chain length:- 100 linksThe slope of angle is (α) degree.Correction for length is given as:- Correction:- Chain length * Angle if Slope.So, correction = 100 * α.So, B is the correct answer.
	Sandi, 2020, How? anyone explain.
	Kartik, 2020, @Blaski.Please explain me the solution.

Binding Material- It bind coarse type of material and fine type of material with water.Provide Minimum setting time (30min) for coarse & fine Material.3. Fine Material- Some type of tiny pore space not filled so we used fine material & Provide good finishing.4. Water- Mixing of coarse, Fine, & Binding material without water called Dry Mortar.And then mix the water in Dry Mortar & make it Slurry Form.Slurry mortar fix easily in pores.5. Mortar is not reusable like brick and steel.

	&Lt;3Civil, 2015, 1) Cast iron:It is an alloy of iron, carbon and silicon and it is hard and brittle. Carbon content may be within 1.7% to 3% and carbon may be present as free carbon or iron carbide Fe3C.2) Wrought iron:This is a very pure iron where the iron content is of the order of 99.5%. It is produced by re-melting pig iron and some small amount of silicon, sulphur, or phosphorus may be present. It is tough, malleable and ductile and can easily be forged or welded.3) Steel:Steel is basically an alloy of iron and carbon in which the carbon content can be less than 1.7% and carbon is present in the form of iron carbide to impart hardness and strength. Two main categories of steel are (a) Plain carbon steel and (b) alloy steel.

	Rabindra, 2018, Wrought iron is an iron alloy with a very low carbon (less than 0.08%) content in contrast to cast iron (2.1% to 4%). It is a semi-fused mass of iron with fibrous slag inclusions (up to 2% by weight), which gives it a "grain" resembling wood that is visible when it is etched or bent to the point of failure. Wrought iron is tough, malleable, ductile, corrosion-resistant and easily welded. Before the development of effective methods of steelmaking and the availability of large quantities of steel, wrought iron was the most common form of malleable iron. It was given the name wrought because it was hammered, rolled or otherwise worked while hot enough to expel molten slag. The modern functional equivalent of wrought iron is mild or low carbon steel. Neither wrought iron nor mild steel contains enough carbon to be hardenable by heating and quenching

	Deepthi, 2013, Strength of concrete primarily depends upon the strength of cement paste. Strength of cement paste depends upon the dilution of paste or in other words, the strength of paste increases with cement content and decreases with air and water content.

	Gundo, 2014, The cement:mortar ratio is important in prac engineering work because cement features, precast or not, need a specific ratio to suit its specific function. Say for instance you're building a brick wall which has an opening; your c:m ratio (the first number represents one "part" cement in my country, and the latter represents aggregates such as sand, stones etc) between the bricks which a situated above the opening must be in such a way that it is stronger than the cm of the surrounding bricks.
