	Ashok Dhiman, 2017, Can anyone explain it the formulas?
	Raj, 2018, Please explain with detail steps and formula.
	Nikhil Parbat, 2018, The Weight of footing 10% of total load =W'.Area of footing =(W1+W2+W')/q. q is safe bearing capacity.The distance of CG from the centre of the small column = (W2.l)/(W1+W).l is centre to centre distance between columns.* centre of gravity of footing area shall coincide with c.g of the combined load in the combined footing.
	Sujit, 2018, Total load = 200 t, self wt of f/n = 10% of total load, And the required area of f/n = 220/20 =11 m^2.
	Gerome, 2018, Please explain how to get the CG and the length of the footing?
	Gerome, 2018, Total load = (120+80)*1.1=220t.Area of footing = 220/20 = 11 sq.m.Dist. Of CG from the center of the small column = 120(5)/(120+80).= 3m.Length of the footing = (3+0.25+0.25) * 2 = 7m.Width of the footing = 11/7 = 1.57m.

If height of the first storey of a building is 3.2 m and riser is 13 cm, the number of treads required, is
	Arif, 2014, First of all covert height in cm so,3.2*100 = 320 after that one riser is 13 cm.320/13 = 24.6.
	Tojum Ete, 2017, What is the exact formula? anyone please explain me.
	Roy, 2017, Height of one flight = 320/2=160 cm.No of rises in one flight=160/13=app. 13,so no of threads in one flight=13-1=12,  so total no of tread in one storey =12*2=24.
	Naveen B, 2017, 3.20/0.13 = 25  (Height of a building/ riser).For each storey, No of threads = 25 - No of storeys (Here it is 1).i.e 25-1 = 24. If it is a 2-storey building, then No of threads equal to 25-2 = 23 and Vice versa.
	Manav Kumar, 2018, If multi-storey building than what to do? Please explain.
	Nanu, 2018, What about for 2 flights?
	Ujjol, 2019, Yes, you are right, thanks @Roy.
	Gopal, 2019, Well said, Thanks @Roy.
	Ajay Kumar Yadav, 2020, Thanks for a helpful answer.
	Kisore, 2020, Thanks @Roy.
	Nitin Salunke, 2020, Thanks for explaining @Roy.
	Rakesh Dev, 2021, No.of riser = (3.2/0.13) = 24.61~~25,No.of tread = 25 - 1 = 24.
	Dawood Khan, 2021, So simple;1m = 100cmSo, 3.2m is equal to 320cm.Now, 320/13 = 24.
	Umesh, 2021, In one flight;Tread = rise - 1So Rise = 320÷13 = 24.61 ~25.Tread = 25 - 1 = 24.
	Gurjant Singh, 2021, 24 is the right answer.

The sound which continues even after its source is cut off, is called
	Rampras, 2015, Why is it not echo?
	Dilawar Khan, 2016, The correct answer is echo.
	Er. Dev Chaudhary, 2018, If we shout or clap, near a tall building or a hill, we will hear the same sound back in a little time. This is called an echo.A sound created in a big hall will persist by repeated reflections from the walls until it's reduced to a value where it is no longer in the audible range of human hearing. This phenomenon is called as reverberation.
	Utsav, 2018, Good explanation, Thanks @Dev.
	Uday, 2019, Reverberation time is measured by recording a sound level as it decreases after the source of the sound has been cut off.
	Arshad Ali, 2020, The sound caused by the reflection of sound waves from a surface back to the listener is called Echo.A reverberation, or reverb, is created when a sound or signal is reflected causing numerous reflections to build up and then decay as the sound is absorbed by the surfaces of objects in the space " which could include furniture, people, and air.

If 1500 g of water is required to have a cement paste 1875 g of normal consistency, the percentage of water is,
	B.Arun Kumar, 2013, Percentage of water = wt of cement/weight of water = (1875-1500)/[1500]*100 = 25%.
	Prof. P. Rajayogan, 2013, Percentage of water = (wt.of water/wt.of cement)100.Question should be revised as followsIf 375 ml or g of water is required to have a cement paste 1875 g of normal consistency, the percentage of water is,Wt.of water = 375 g.Wt.of  cement = 1875-375 = 1500 g.Normal  Consistency = (375/1500)*100.                                        = 25%.
	Malavika, 2013, Which one of the answer is correct. According to the question the weight of the water is 1500g why should we take 375g.
	Uma, 2013, Weight of water = 1500g.Weight of cement paste = 1875g.Weight of cement = 1875-1500. = 375g.% of water = wt.of cement/wt.of  water.= (375/1500)*100.= 25%.
	Usha Rani, 2013, @Malavika.As normal consistency test is conducted by mixing water and cement. The value 1875 is for both cement and water. When we subtract water value i.e. 1500g from whole 1875 (cement water paste) we get the weight of the cement.
	Jay, 2013, @B.Arun Kumar,  @Malavika.As per the question,The percentage of water = (weight of water/ weight of whole mixture)*100. = (1500/1875)*100.
	Nitin, 2013, If we will add 1500 gm of water in 375 gm of cement then will it be a cement paste or cement solution ?  I am satisfied with Prof. P. Rajayogan's comment.
	Shamim Alam, 2013, weight of cement -  weight of water/weight of tare wt*100 a - b/b*100.So, 1875-1500 = 375/1500*100 = 25%.
	Vr Chowdary, 2013, Percentage of water = wt of cement/weight of water = (1875-1500)/[1500]*100 = 25%.
	Rakesh, 2013, Simply p of water = (375/1500)*100 = 25%.
	Sanjay Rakholiya, 2013, Percentage of water = 1875-1500/1500 = 25%.
	Subhro Moitra, 2013, If we take 1500 gm of water then.Weight of water=400% of weight of cement. Would it be of standard consistency?Prof. @Rajayogan and Mr @Nitin are right.
	Saboor, 2014, @Malavika.The water cement ratio should not increase or should not exceed 50% weight of cement. The question shall be recapitulated as sir has said.
	Nile, 2014, What would be the standard consistency to know good quality of cement?
	Jairaj, 2014, The standard normal consistency of cement is 22% to 30% of weight of dry cement. If satisfied my answer then give a comment on this.
	Bangada Mangisto Livino, 2014, Given: Mass of water 1500g, Mass of paste 1875g and Mass of cement is given by,1875g - 1500g = 375g.%Mass of water = (Mass of cement/Mass of water).100%.             = (375/1500).100%.             = (0.25).100%.             = 25% Ans. required.
	Vishal Dave, 2014, The percentage = 1875-1500/1500 = 0.25*100 = 25%.
	Afroz, 2014, Total cement past weight 1875-1500 = 375g.Weight of cement = 1500g.Normal consistancy is the the amount of water which mixed in neat cement so that gauge of vicat appartes should be insert in cement past 35mm to 37mm. It takes as a percentage.So,Normal consistency of cement or percentage og water is.375/1500*100 = 25%.

Wp and Wf are the weights of a cylinder containing partially compacted and fully compacted concrete. If the compaction factor  is 0.95, the workability of concrete is
	Amal Ranjan, 2013, Higher compaction factor implies higher workability.A mix with good workability compacts easily.
	Sudhansu Bhagat, 2013, Extremely low - 0.65 to 0.68.Very low - 0.75.low - 0.83 to 0.85.Medium - 0.90 to 0.92.High - 0.95.
	Ghassan Kharouf, 2014, More compaction, means more dense material, otherwise (lower workability).For a good workability, compaction factor must be between (0.7 - 0.95).
	Vishal Dave, 2014, The more compaction means more denser concrete and as 1% of air voids reduce strength by 5%, more compaction provides lesser voids which provides good strength.
	Mohamed Ahmed Ibrahim, 2014, In fact partial compaction happens during pouring & when we use vibration or other method of compaction we will get full compaction.And since the sample obtains 95% i.e. it has high work ability.
	Amresh, 2014, As it goes towards 1 its work ability increases.
	Md Saleem Ansari, 2015, As the ratio between partially compacted and fully compacted concrete increases, the work ability of concrete also increases upto the maximum.
	Antony, 2016, What is meant by wf and wp?
	Sushan Bastola, 2016, wp = wt of partially compacted concrete and wf = wt of fully compacted concrete.
	Vishal, 2016, Agree @MD Saleem Ansari.
	Muddasser, 2016, 0.95 is high.
	Dhiraj P Chaudhari, 2017, For a good workability, compaction factor must be between (0.7 - 0.95).Extremely low - 0.65 to 0.68.Very low - 0.75.Low - 0.83 to 0.85.Medium - 0.90 to 0.92.High - 0.95.

For given water content, workability decreases if the concrete aggregates contain an excess of
	Hari, 2013, All are the particles have insufficient capability to increase workability.
	Nwaeze, Humphrey, 2014, The answer E because all of them are debris i.e unwanted particles, which absorb water thereby decreasing the water content.
	Sanj, 2014, Rounded shape of the aggregate is best for high workability.
	Jairaj, 2014, Rounded shape of the agrt are not suitable for any kind of concrete structure. Because there will not have grip between the concrete constituents.
	Ruturaj Kurade, 2014, This type of particle is lead to reduce workability because surface area is more of these particle.
	Srinu, 2014, Triangular shape of aggregate is best for high workability.
	Ahir Ashok R, 2014, Rounded shape particles are the most suitable for the high workability.
	Soni Kumari, 2014, Rounded shape particles are the most suitable for the high workability.
	Shubham, 2014, Rounded shape particles are the most suitable for the high work-ability, but Triangular shape of aggregate is best for high strength b/c of good bonding between cement paste and aggregates.
	Dini, 2015, How surface area is depended on work ability?
	Devidas Bedre, 2015, This type of particle lead to reduce work ability because there is formation of voids between them therefore there will not have grip between the concrete constituents.
	Pradeep, 2015, Angular shape particle is use in concrete because this shape is good for aggregate.
	Mike, 2015, Because the surface is dependent on work ability.
	Deepak, 2015, @ Dini.I think more surface area absorb more water at given water content so reduces workability.
	B Bikshapathi, 2015, I think angular aggregate are more useful for construction because it gives more work ability.
	Tony, 2015, The more the surface are the more cement paste required for enveloping them. So as flaky, elongated particles have more surface area less cement available, less cohesion, more segregation.
	Satani Mohit, 2015, I think elongated aggregate are more than useful for construction. Because is more than strength and work ability and structure life is many more years.
	Pallabi Rath, 2015, Surface area as decreases strength decreases but work ability increases. So round gives more work ability but less strength and flaky elongated material gives less work ability and more strength.
	Irfan, 2016, Yes @Deepak. The more surface area absorbs more water at the given water content so, it reduces the workability.
	Adil Hussain, 2016, Surface area is more for all types of aggregate in this. So the answer is E.
	Suraj, 2016, Rounded shape particles are the suitable for the high workability.
	Hitendra, 2016, The aggregate with rounded shape has the minimum percentage of voids ranging from 32 to 33%. It gives the minimum ratio of surface area to given volume and hence requires minimum water for lubrication. It gives good workability for the given amount of water and hence needs less cement for a given water cement ratio. The only disadvantages are that the interlocking between its particles is less and hence the development of bond is poor. This is why rounded aggregate is not suitable for high strength concrete and for pavements subjected to tension.
	Vipin, 2016, As per IS - 383, The aggregate should be angular.
	Naren, 2017, Workability decreases if the concrete aggregate contain above options bcoz, surface area is high.Rounded and semi-rounded aggregates increases workability because for a given volume it has less surface and less void, so excess paste is available to give better lubricating effect and rounded aggregates has less friction resistance.Important nature of workable concrete is its lubricating nature.
	Chinny, 2017, I think the angular shaped aggregates are more used construction & it gives more strength, w workability.
	Duraisamy, 2017, Water cement ratio indirect proportion to the size of practical and workability.
	Ajay Desai, 2018, Rounded shape particles are the most suitable for the high work-ability, but angular shape of aggregate is best for high strength b/c of good bonding between cement paste and aggregates.
	Lal Chand Godara, 2020, Only on rounded aggregate workability is increased.
	Sandeep Rana, 2021, Smooth surface texture of aggregates give better workability of concrete.
	Sandeep Rana, 2021, Flaky & elongated aggregates provide higher surface area.

	Mahesh, 2017, What is the M in M10 mix?
	Saket, 2017, IS 456-2000 has designated the concrete mixes into a number of grades as M10, M15, M20, M25, M30, M35 and M40. In this designation the letter M refers to the mix and the number to the specified 28 day cube strength of mix in N/mm2. The mixes of grades M10, M15, M20 and M25 correspond approximately to the mix proportions (1:3:6), (1:2:4), (1:1.5:3) and (1:1:2) respectively.

For ensuring quality of concrete, use

After casting, an ordinary cement concrete on drying
	Bhagyashree, 2013, I guess its because water is evaporated and the concrete becomes compact.
	Bimal Lamsal, 2013, What is the process of that ordinary cement concrete will shrinks after casting on drying?
	Jairaj, 2014, @Bhagyashree. It is not so that the water will evaporate and concrete will get compact.Only thing is that the chemical process will going on inside the concrete structure thats why it get shrinks.Curing is done to protect the inside chemical action against evaporate to get strengthen the structure.If the curing is not done within the time then the structure will definitely cracks, and may chance to collapse.
	S.R, 2014, It shrink because of capillary pressure.
	Abhi, 2015, Concrete shrinks after drying due to loss of water by evaporation which results in shrinkage cracks.Proper curing is hence required to contain the shrinkage cracks.
	Darpan Gautam, 2015, Drying shrinkage is defined as the contracting of a hardened concrete mixture due to the loss of capillary water. This shrinkage causes an increase in tensile stress, which may lead to cracking, internal warping, and external deflection, before the concrete is subjected to any kind of loading.
	Lokesh, 2015, On drying there is a decrease in volume of the concrete.
	Najrul, 2015, Concrete shrinks after casting due to the chemical reaction known as HEAT OF HYDRATION, due to this action the water present in concrete will evaporate as per the required period. Finally the loss of water in concrete cause shrinkage in concrete.
	Balabhadra Sena, 2016, Concrete shrinks after drying due to the voids are filled by air after drying. It is considerably filled by water. Water will evaporate from that place by drying. . Thus it shrinks.
	Saudan Singh, 2017, Give me the answer if Shrinkage in concrete is increased.
	Duraisamy, 2017, Water will be dry and heat of hydration process start so concrete shrink.
	Sandy, 2017, This is because the water content in the concrete will evaporate and it will shrink.
	Kishan, 2018, It is Due to the heat of hydration.
	Gaurav, 2019, Heat of hydration happen there.



Hydration of cement is due to chemical action of water with
	Gupta Karan, 2013, Hydration reaction is carried out in presence of 40%of tri calcium silicate, 30% of dicalcium silicate, 11% of tricalcium aluminate and 11% tricalcium alumino ferrite each.
	Raghavendra, 2014, These compounds are called, "Bogue's compounds".
	Anonymous, 2014, Made a confusion because of C4AF. Tetra Calcium Alumino Ferrite.
	Pankaj, 2014, 1) Tricalcium silicate (CaO) SiO or C S.3 2 3.2) Dicalcium silicate (CaO) SiO or C S.2 2 2.3) Tricalcium Aluminate (CaO) Al O or C A.3 2 3 3.4) Tetracalcium Aluminoferrite (CaO) Al O Fe O or C AF.4 2 3 2 3 4.
	Rustam Tomar, 2016, Cement Compound - Chemical Formula1) Tricalcium silicate - Ca3SiO5 or 3CaO.SiO22) Dicalcium silicate - Ca2SiO4 or 2CaO.SiO23) Tricalcium aluminate - Ca3Al2O6 or 3CaO .Al2O34) Tetracalcium aluminoferrite - Ca4Al2Fe2O10 or 4CaO.Al2O3.Fe2O3
	Asharam, 2016, Cement Compound - Chemical Formula.1) Tricalcium silicate - Ca3SiO5 or 3CaO.SiO2.2) Dicalcium silicate - Ca2SiO4 or 2CaO.SiO2.3) Tricalcium aluminate - Ca3Al2O6 or 3CaO .Al2O3.4) Tetracalcium aluminoferrite - Ca4Al2Fe2O10 or 4CaO.Al2O3.Fe2O.

The bulk density of aggregates does not depend upon :
	Saboor, 2014, What is bulk density. Could anyone please explain ?
	Kishori Pant, 2014, Bluk density is a measurement of soil mass per unit volume of soil.
	Sonu Jangra, 2014, Bulk density is defined as the dry weight of soil per unit volume of soil. Bulk density considers both the solids and the pore space; whereas, particle density considers only the mineral solids.
	Najimul Mondal, 2015, The bulk density is the total mass of the soil per unit of its total volume.
	Tony, 2015, The shape and size of container does affect the calculation of bulk density practically, thus Indian standards have specific containers used, on the basis of trial and error methods.
	Chiranjeev Mcet, 2015, Bulk density means change in volume of any material.
	Md Khalid, 2016, Bulk density is defined as weight of materials per unit volume.
	Aneesha, 2016, According to the size of the container, the volume will change. Bulk density means the weight of soil to the unit volume. In this unit volume means container volume. If we change the volume bulk density will change. Why the size and shape of container do not depend on bulk density?
	Pankaj Raut, 2016, Bulk density of aggregate = weight of aggregate in a container/weight of water in same container.
	Kasturi, 2017, Please give me the right answer. If the volume of the container is used, then why option (D) correct?
	Dineshkumar, 2017, Bulk density is defined as the mass required to fill a container of unit volume.

An aggregate is said to be flaky if its least dimension is less than
	Buddhi, 2013, And For Elongated Particles, their Maximum dimension should be larger than 1.8 times of mean size.
	Md Khalid, 2016, In other words we can say that an aggregate is said to be flaky if its least dimension is less than 0.6 times of mean dimension.
	Hassan Choudhury, 2018, 1. Flaky Particles: The particles whose least dimension is less than 0.6 times the mean size dimensions.2. Elongated particles: The particles whose greatest dimension is greater than 1.8 times the mean size dimension.3. Mean size of aggregates: Mean size of aggregates is defined as the mean of two seive sizes, between which the particle is retained i.e. Mean size = (Passing sieve size + Retaining sieve size)/2.

The increased cohesiveness of concrete, makes it
	Chaitu, 2013, When the cohesive property of concrete increases, then there will be chance of generation of bonding between each particle in concrete, so there will be no chance of segregation (Separation of particles).
	Yawar, 2015, Cohesive means bonding force and segregation means separation, when boding is increased between ingredients of concrete then segregation will be less.
	Jitendra, 2015, Can anyone tell me what is bleeding of concrete?
	Chiranjeevi Mcet, 2015, Bleeding of concrete is nothing but addition of more amount of water in concrete mix it happens bleeding.
	Prabhakaran, 2016, If cohesive is more then it get a chance of bleeding right?
	Vikram, 2016, @Jitendra.Bleeding is defined as water occur from the concrete mix onto the top surface.
	Abhimanyu, 2016, Cohesion means bonding b/w molecules, by this property b/w concrete particles it is less liable to segregation.
	Jack, 2016, Suppose you are filling a formwork with concrete, if the water or cement slurry runs out from formwork joints/gaps it is termed as bleeding.
	T Stark, 2017, Cohesion means molecular attraction between different types of materials.
	Rahul Choudhary, 2018, Cohesion means molecular attraction between same type of material.In different type of material molecular attraction is adhesion.
	Abhishek, 2018, What is surface scaling in frosty weather?
	Subash, 2018, The cohesiveness of concrete is given, So will segregation increased or decreased.
	Ram Chaudhary, 2018, Bleeding is a form of segregation. And from above points, cohesiveness means attraction or gathering or binding of ingredients together but segregation is the result of coming out of semi-liquid water cement from its paste. So, according to it cohesiveness means less liable to segregation.
	Basu, 2019, Bleeding - Drops of water by the side of formwork.Latience- Water found to the top surface.
	Heera, 2019, Cohesiveness is how well concrete HOLDS TOGETHER when plastic.
	Bairagi, 2021, Increased cohesiveness of concrete.Binding properties Increased then segregation less.

If 20 kg of coarse aggregate is sieved through 80 mm, 40 mm, 20 mm, 10 mm, 4.75 mm, 2.36 mm, 1.18 mm, 600 micron, 300 micron and 150 micron standard sieves and the weights retained are 0 kg, 2 kg, 8 kg, 6 kg, 4 kg respectively, the fineness modulus of the aggregate, is
	Kishore, 2013, % RETAINED   CUMULATIVE % RETAINED80 MM   0           0                 040     2           10                1020      8          40                5010      6          30                804.75    4          20                1002.36   0           0                 1001.18   0           0                 100600     0          0                 100300    0           0                 100 150    0           0                 100TOTAL   740.FINENESS MODULUS = 740/100 = 7.4.
	Turab, 2013, Please explain how the last column how it comes to b 10 50 80 and 100?
	Maha, 2013, 2 out of 20 kg retained in that particular sieve, so for 100 how much is retained give % retained hence.(2/20) * 100 = 10(8/20) * 100 = 40(6/20)* 100 = 30(4/20)*100 = 20. And so on, if u have to find cumulative % retained u have to add the presently available value with all previous values hence first value is 10.Second value is 10 + 40 = 50.Third value is 10+ 40 +30 = 80.Fourth value is 10+40+30+20 = 100 and so on.
	Rhythm, 2015, But the answer is in 7.35 and your answer is 7.4 which is correct?
	Rohan, 2015, But the answer is nearly to correct so answer is true.
	Tanu, 2015, But here both 7.35 and 7.40 options are given. Finally what will we do?
	Nitin, 2015, Yes the answer should be 7.40 means option C is correct.
	Nikita, 2015, Please tell me how you get the sum 740?
	Snehal Nikam, 2015, How it is possible?
	Hardik, 2015, Approximately maximum near by value is taken as A answer.
	Nirav Bhatt, 2015, Pass Wt Cum Wt. %Cum %cum.Retained % passing.0 0 0 100.2 2 10 90.8 10 40 50.6 16 30 20.4 20 20 0.--- --- --- ---.20 48 100 260.Sieves used are 10.Hence FM = (10*100-260)/100 = 7.4.In method suggested by @Kishor, the total is done individually for each sieve where as here it is done by using the nos. sieve.Both the methods are correct. So the Answer is 7.4.
	Jagadeesh, 2015, Sieve weight % weight cumulative.80 0 0 0.40 2 10 10.20 8 40 50.10 6 30 80.7.5 4 20 100.4.75 0 0 100.2.36 0 0 100.1.18 0 0 100.600 0 0 100.300 0 0 100.150 0 0 100.-----------------Total = 740.-----------------Fineness modulus of given course aggregate is 740/100 = 7.4.
	Sanu Sonkamble, 2016, Thanks Kishorn & Jagadeesh.
	Vinod, 2016, Fineness modulus of given course aggregate is 740/100 =7.4.
	Bapureddy, 2016, I didn't get it. Please, Can anyone explain it clearly?
	Naman, 2016, I didn't get this, Please explain briefly.
	Sanjana, 2016, Can anyone explain last column?
	Sudip, 2016, Sieve.size  wt. retained(kg)  %wt.retained(%)  cumulative%80 0 0 0.40 2 10 10.20 8 40 50.10 6 30 80.7.5 4 20 100.4.75 0 0 100.2.36 0 0 100.1.18 0 0 100.600 0 0 100.300 0 0 100.150 0 0 100.-----------------Total = 740.-----------------Fineness modulus of given course aggregate is 740/100 = 7.4.For wt. retained in %, for 40mm sieve size, (2/20)*100% where 20 is the total wt.
	Abi, 2016, Can't understand. Please explain this.
	Umang, 2016, Thanks for the explanation @Maha.
	Moreri, 2017, Why do we divide 740 by 100?
	Dangerous, 2017, Sieve  Retained	 Reained perce	Cumaltive size80	0	0	040	2	10	1020	8	40	5010	6	30	804.75	4	20	1002.36	0	0	1001.18	0	0	100600	0	0	100300	0	0	100150	0	0	100			740Fineness modulus =740/100,	= 7.4.
	Harsha G, 2017, SIEVE   WEIGHT RET CUMULATIVE WT CUM.%SIZE             80          0         0                          040          2         2                         1020          8         10                        5010          6         16                        804.75       4         20                       1002.36       0         20                       1001.18       0         20                       100                    600M      0         20                       100300M      0         20                       100                  150M      0         20                       100ADD CUM % RET AND DIVIDE BY 100  740/100 = 7.4.Fineness modulus is an empirical factor obtained by adding the cumulative percentages of aggregate retained on each of the standard sieves ranging from 80 mm to 150 micron and dividing this sum by 100.CUM% = (CUM WT / TOTAL WT )* 100.
	Virendra Kumar, 2017, How to total 740 and what is 740? please explain.

Retained80.          0.                   0.                         040.          2                   10.                      1020.          8                    40.                     5010.          6                     30.                    804.75.       4                     20.                 1002.36.        0.                   0.                   1001.18.        0                     0                   100600.          0                    0                     100300.          0                     0                    100150.          0.                    0                    100Total cumulative % weight retained= 740.Fineness modulus = total cumulative % weight  retained/ 100.FM=740/100.7.40 is the correct answer.

The process of proper and accurate measurement of concrete ingredients for uniformity of proportion, is known
	Sapna, 2015, The major steps involved in the process of concreting are as follows:1. Batching.2. Mixing.3. Transporting and placing of concrete.4. Compacting.> Batching: The process of measurement of the different materials for the making of concrete is known as batching. Batching is usually done in two ways: volume batching and weight batching. In case of volume batching the measurement is done in the form of volume whereas in the case of weight batching it is done by the weight.> Mixing: In order to create good concrete the mixing of the materials should be first done in dry condition and after it wet condition. The two general methods of mixing are: hand mixing and machine mixing.> Transportation and placing of concrete: Once the concrete mixture is created it must be transported to its final location. The concrete is placed on form works and should always be dropped on its final location as closely as possible.> Compaction of concrete: When concrete is placed it can have air bubbles entrapped in it which can lead to the reduction of the strength by 30%. In order to reduce the air bubbles the process of compaction is performed. Compaction is generally performed in two ways: by hand or by the use of vibrators.
	Satish 57, 2016, Thanks @Sapna.
	Subhajit Kanjilal, 2017, Thanks for the given information @Sapna.
	Uday, 2017, Excellent @Sapna.
	A Ghaffar, 2018, Thanks for the proper information @Sapna.
	Suraj Chaurasiya, 2019, The major source is involved in concreting are as follows;1) batching.2) transportation.3) mixing.4) compaction.As we know that, Batching of aggregate play a very important role in the preparation of cement concrete.Batching of aggregate directly affect the strength of cement concrete.
	Bibek Anand, 2019, Thanks for explaining @Sapna.
	Archana, 2020, Thank you @Sapna.
	Mahesh, 2020, Thanks for the description @Sapana.

The preliminary test is repeated if the difference of compressive strength of three test specimens, exceeds
	Amitgoel, 2014, Please explain your thought. ! As per IS-456, allowable % age is 15% between three adjacent cubes.

	Jinx, 2015, I didn't get the specimens details it is a cement mortar or concrete cubes.

Transport of concrete by pumps, is done for a distance of
	Manoj Kumar, 2014, It is the maximum limit or minimum limit?

	Amar, 2016, 50 m only is the right answer. But it is not in the given option.
	Abhishek Paliwal, 2016, Two types of concrete pumps are used:Direct-acting, horizontal piston type with semi-rotary valves set to permit always the passage of the largest aggregate particles. This type of pump can cover a horizontal distance of 1000 m and a vertical distance of 120 m. The concrete is fed in by gravity and is also partially sucked in during the suction stroke. The valves open and close with definite pauses so that concrete moves in a series of impulses, but the pipe always remains full. These pumps are capable of pumping 130 m3 of concrete per hour with 8-inch pipes.
	Dilawar Khan, 2016, I totally agree with your statement @Abhishek Paliwal.
	Arijit Sanyal, 2017, It will be maximum vertical distance of 50m.

If X, Y and Z are the fineness modulli of coarse, fine and combined aggregates, the percentage (P) of fine aggregates to combined aggregates, is
	Shivam, 2014, How we got the answer?
	Ranjith, 2015, How to work to find answer?
	Nirav Bhatt, 2015, The fineness Modulus (FM) of Coarse aggregate is higher than combined and fine aggregate. The FM of fine aggregates is lowest. And FM of combined aggregate is in between.Here, X = Coarse aggregate = Largest value.Y = Fine aggregate = Lowest value.Z = Combined aggregate = Intermediate value.Compare it with the answer. You will get the required information.
	Dimpi, 2015, Z- Combined Aggregate. (Both fine & Coarse), So value should be highest?
	Darshan H A, 2016, Question should be percentage of fine aggregate to coarse aggregate not combined aggregate.
	Nayandeep, 2016, B and E options are same if we take sign common.
	Faisal, 2016, According to me, It is option D.
	Rohit Negi, 2017, According to me, the Answer is D.
	Sheela, 2017, B is correct. I agree.
	Chetan, 2017, No, answer is correct, according to question. @Darshan H A 100%correct.Let FA/CA=m then the ratio of FA with combined aggregate is m/ (m+1).Now solve you get the desired answer.
	Garry, 2017, None of the above is correct.Correct is, (X-Z)/(X-Y).
	S.Bhakta, 2018, Answer is obviously D.
	Virender Paul, 2018, Answer is correct.Because coaser particle will be greater than combined or finer so answer should be coarser-combined/combinef-fine.
	Manohar, 2018, pY+X(1-p)=1 * Z.It is p = (X-Z) &div; (X-Y).
	Vivek Mishra, 2019, You are right, thanks @Manohar.
	Prasenjit, 2019, (CALCULATING RATIO OF FINE AGGREGATE TO COARSE AGGREGATE).Let "x1" is the percentage of fine aggregate to be combined with coarse aggregate. x can be calculated using the formula given below.x = [(F2-F)/(F-F1)] * 100.Where,F1 = Fineness modulus value of the fine aggregate.F2 = Fineness modulus value of coarse aggregate.F = Fineness modulus value of the combined aggregate.After finding the percentage of fine aggregate to be combined with the coarse aggregate by the above formula, their proportion can be calculated.
	Rishabh, 2020, It should be p={(X-Z)/(X-Y)}*100%.
	Pratusha Shankar Arepelli, 2021, How to calculate it? Please explain in detail.

The high strength of rapid hardening cement at early stage, is due to its
	M.Pagavandoss, 2014, For rapid hardening cement -cement has to grind finer its surface area not less than 3,25000 sqmm. So answer A is correct.If we increased the lime content the initial setting time also increase.
	Anil Choudhary, 2014, The rapid rate of developing of strength in RHC is attributed to.1. Higher fineness of grinding.2. Higher content of tricalcium (C3S) and lower content of dicalcium (C2S).So A and D is the right answer!
	Nikhil Tank, 2014, Contains more tri-calcium silicate (C3S) and finely ground.
	Shankar Khanal, 2015, As we increase the lime content, the amount of C3S increases. Hence the option C is correct.

	Rajendran P, 2015, Dear sir,The early strength of RHC is achieved by the cement is grinding finer than the OPC. So surface area of cement will more and the finer cement will quick action with water and getting early strength. If adding more cement, cement will unsound.
	Nirav Bhatt, 2015, All the options are participating in the high strength at early stage. To make RHC they are burnt at higher temperature and grinded fine. Also increasing the lime content increases the amount of C3S. Hence all the options are correct. The answer is all of above.
	Mike, 2015, RHC develops the strength rapidly at the age of 3 days, the same at that expected in opc in 7 days. The rapid rate development of strength is due to higher fine grinding and higher of C3S and lower of C2S, but I don't know how the answer is justified to Increase in lime content.

	Smily, 2015, Increase in lime content will increase the strength and hardening.
	Darshan H A, 2016, Clearly given in books. The answer is both A and D.
	Manish Bairagi, 2016, Early strength of RHC is mainly due to fine grinding and more content of C3S but for single purpose. What is the correct option b/w these two?
	Jack, 2016, All the options are correct. Reference M. S. Shetty.
	Nayeem Ahmad Reshi, 2017, During the initial period of hardening, the gain in strength of C2S is small and is about 15% of that of C3S. After 28 days the hydration of C3S comes practically to an end and the hydration of C2S only really begins at that time. Hence when a high strength concrete is required within a short period of time, the cement is made with the high content of C3S. On the other hand if a high strength concrete is required at a later stage as in the case of hydraulic engineering constructions the cement is made with the high content of C2S.So option D is the right one.Option [A].  Finer grinding. The fineness of cement helps to improve some properties of the cement but can not impart any extra property to it and plays a role in early setting but early strength is far different.Option [B]. Burning at high temperature...the main motives of burning are 1) to drive-off the carbon dioxide with combustion gases2)  the raw materials get fused together...(and nothing to do with the initial strength of cement)Option [C]. increased lime cement. Being a civil engineer, one must know this ''the lime in excess makes the cement unsound and causes the cement to expand and disintegrate and if lime is in deficiency the strength of cement is decreased and causes the cement to set quickly. [D]. 	higher content of tricalcium is the correct option.

Separation of coarse aggregates from mortar during transportation, is known
	Devidas Bedre, 2015, At the time of transportation due to movement of concrete containing tank the constituents of concrete are separate from each other this process is called segregation.
	R.K.B., 2015, Segregation is the property of the concrete in plastic stage.
	Jahnavi, 2016, Labour is placed to the concrete in slab some force to be thrown away particles will be separated is called segregation.
	Nma, 2017, Bleeding is the tendency of the water to rise to the surface of the freshly laid concrete. This results from the inability of the solid material of concrete to hold the all the water mixed fro preparation of concrete and during the process of material downward settling. If we resort to surface finish in presence of bleeding it would weaken the top surface. Type of cement used also has to influence on the bleeding process.In the case of segregation, the heavy aggregate particles settle down leaving a sand cement mix on top affecting the quality adversely. Fine aggregate could be used to overcome the problems. However; design mix must be such that required strength could be achieved. Similarly, water ratio may be catered for in design mix to avoid bleeding keeping in view the local material specifications.

Shrinkage in concrete can be reduced by using
	Santosh, 2016, I think it should be only proper concrete mix.
	Sourav, 2016, You are right @Santosh.
	Tanu, 2017, Yes, I think also that only proper concrete mix reduce the shrinkage of concrete.
	Anshu Singh, 2018, Increase of the water content by one percent will approximately increase the drying shrinkage by three percent.
	Ashish, 2019, Proper concrete mix = less cement.Low WC ratio. It's all included in. So the answer E is right.
	Lohith, 2019, Thank you @Ashish.
	Pramit Samanta, 2019, Shrinkage in concrete can also be controlled by adding bigger size aggregates.
	Krishna Pada Bera, 2019, Proper concrete ingredient mix as like stone chips, sand, cement, chemical, water.
	Hardik, 2019, @Ashish. How water cement ratio = less cement??For ex...water-100, cement- 5...then w/c ratio is (100/5) = 20.So if we reduce cement means high w/c ratio...like, Cement is 2 then w/c ratio is (100/2) = 50.So for me 1st and 2nd option are opposite to each other...it may be right...is that is cement/water ratio...Please Correct me if I am wrong.
	Rahul Negi, 2020, @Hardik @Ashish.As the amount of water added to the mix is increased the ratio of water-to-cement is increased which increases the amount of shrinkage.In simple terms water is volume. Increasing the amount of water added to the concrete mix correspondingly increases the wet mix volume.
	Hossayn Fedayi, 2021, Option is correct.Because.The extent of initial shrinkage in cement concrete and cement mortar depends on a number of factors namely : a) Cement content "It increases with the richness of mix. B) Water content " Greater the water quantity used in the mix, greater is the shrinkage. C) Maximum size, grading & quality of aggregate "With the use of largest possible max. Size of aggregate in concrete and with good grading, the requirement of water for the desired workability is reduced, with consequent less shrinkage on drying due to reduction in porosity. Eg: For the same cement aggregate ratio, shrinkage of sand mortar is 2 to 3 times that of concrete using 20 mm maximum size aggregate and 3 to 4 times that of concrete using 40 mm maximum size aggregate. D) Curing "if the proper curing is carried out as soon as the initial set has taken place and is continued for at least 7 to 10 days then the initial shrinkage is comparatively less. E) Presence of excessive fines in aggregates -The presence of fines increases the specific surface area of aggregate & consequently the water requirement for the desired workability, with an increase in initial shrinkage.

You are asked to construct a massive dam, the type of cement you will use, is
	Pramit_Ce_Besus, 2013, Dam construction will involve mass concreting where heat of hydration is the major concern which if not monitored will cause crack in the bulk of the dam which is undesirable, hence low heat cement is preferable in this type of concreting.
	Megha Thilak, 2014, When to use low heat cement - Hydration of cement is an exothermic process that produces large quantity of heat. In mass concrete structures such as dams, retaining walls, bridge abutment, raft etc the rate of dissipation of heat of hydration from the surface is much lower than that generated.It causes rise in temperature inside the concrete mass and may develop thermal and shrinkage cracks. Under this circumstances, the low heat cement or low heat Portland cement can be effectively used.In low heat cement, ingredients are proportioned in such a way that C3A and C3S are formed in less amount but C2S is formed in increased amount so that C2S and C3S collectively impart the same strength as Ordinary Portland Cement (OPC).
	Arumugapandian, 2016, Why? Explain it in detail.
	Ishita Goyal, 2017, Why can't blast furnace slag cement be used?
	Karmveer Singh, 2017, Why quick is setting cement not used?
	Ganesh Mondal, 2017, Why is blast furnace slag cement not used?
	Karan Bali, 2018, Why partland pozzollana cement is not used?
	Piyush, 2020, Blast furnace slag cement should be used for bridge.
	Rahul Khan, 2020, The quick setting is cement generally used in under running water and for construction of massive dams, we will use generally low heat Portland cement.
	Aman, 2020, Blast furnace slag used only M 20 grade.For dam m30 grade used so used low heat cement.
	Hari, 2021, Low heat cement for mass constructions lika dams.

For batching 1:3:6 concrete mix by volume, the ingredients required per bag of 50 kg cement, are:
	Dikshant, 2013, How the quantities are converted into liters?
	Ram, 2013, Volume of one beg cement = 0.035 cum.Hence sand = 0.035*3 = 0.105 cum.1 cum = 1000 lt.So sand = 0.105*1000 = 105 lt.& Aggregates = 105*2 = 210 lt.
	Sim, 2014, How do we convert litre to kg? Multiply by bulk density?
	Suresh, 2015, Kg and liter are equal, one gram = one milliliter.
	Niharika, 2015, How to convert volume to weight?
	Vaibhav S, 2015, Density = Mass/Volume.Weight = Density*Volume*9.81.
	Rohit, 2015, 1:3:6 is the proportion that he has asked.Density = Mass/Volume.Density of concrete = 1400 kg/m3.Mass of cement = 50 kg (given).1440 = 50/v, hence v = 0.035 m3.Water = 0.035*3 = 0.105 m3.Aggregates = 0.035*6 = 0.21 m3.Cement:Sand:Aggregates = 0.035:0.105:0.21 (in m3).Cement:Sand:Aggregates = 35:105:210 (in lts if multiplied above result with 1000 as 1 m3 = 1000 lts).
	Ramesh, 2015, Density = Mass/Volume.Weight = Density*Volume*9.81.Why should we multiply with 9.81?
	Bhavyasree, 2015, What its's bulk density?
	Xai, 2015, @Rohit the density of concrete is 2400 kg/m3. Where did you get 1400 kg/m3?
	Shrikant, 2015, Its a density of cement.
	Antil, 2016, This is not a density of concrete. It is density of cement = 1440 kg/m3.
	Dhanashree, 2016, Thank you all for providing the clear solution.
	Baloch, 2016, Clear cut solution:A cement bag contains 35 liters of cement,35 x 1 : 35 x 3 : 35 x 6.= 35 :  105 : 210.
	Bhavesh Panker, 2017, 50 kg of cement - 35 lit volume.1:3:635 lit cement : 105 lit sand : 210 lit c.a.
	Basudeb, 2017, Thanks to all.
	Pankoj, 2017, How to calculate quantity of water for this question?
	Amal Zad, 2017, Thank you @Ram and @Bhavesh Pankar.
	Roy, 2018, Thanks @Baloch.Your explanation is very simple & easy to understand.
	Anil Mahato, 2019, 1kg = 0.7 liters.50kg cement - 35liters.3*50kgsand-150 * .7 = 105liter.300kg aggregate-300*.7 = 210 liters.
	Kapfoie, 2019, The Volume of 50kg bag of cement =35 litre,Therefore; Sand 35x3=105.Aggregate 35x6=210.

If the slump increases, workability decreases----> Incorrect (more slump less consistency more workable).D]. If the concrete mix is dry, the slump is maximum----> Incorrect (Dry mix has no water thus slump zero).So, None of these is the correct answer.

Pick up the incorrect statement from the following:
	Darpan Gautam, 2015, Cement mixtures.Mixtures are always listed as parts Cement to Sand to Aggregate. Following are typical cement mixtures description:Rich: 1 part cement, 2 parts sand, 3 parts coarse aggregate. A rich mix is used for concrete roads and waterproof structures.Standard: 1 part cement, 2 parts sand, 4 parts coarse aggregate. A standard mix is used for reinforced work floors, roofs, columns, arches, tanks, sewers, conduits, etc.Medium: 1 part cement, 2 1/2 parts sand, 5 parts coarse aggregate. A medium mix is used for foundations, walls, abutments, piers, etc.Lean: 1 part cement, 3 parts sand, 6 parts coarse aggregate. A lean mix is used for all mass concrete work, large foundations, backing for stone masonry, etc.

The percentage of the aggregate of F.M. 2.6 to be combined with coarse aggregate of F.M. 6.8 for obtaining the aggregates of F.M. 5.4, is
	Pratik Basak, 2015, Formula is (X-Z)/(Z-Y)*100. Here X - 6.8, Y - 2.6, Z - 5.4.
	Agar, 2015, FM mean?
	Rajesh, 2016, Fineness Modulus-FM.
	Nabin Chandra Das From Gimt, 2016, {(6.8-5.4)/(5.4-2.6)}*100 = 50%x = 6.8, y = 2.6, z = 5.4.C is correct answer.
	Amrit Raj, 2017, How can we determine the value of X, Y and Z?
	Vishvas, 2017, X = % coarse aggregates.Y = % fine aggregates.Z = % combined aggregates.
	Pritam, 2017, When it is given "to be combined", the solution is given as  (6.8-5.4)/(6.8-2.6).
	Naveen Kallan, 2017, If it is the ratio of fine to coarse then only ans is 50%. If it is percentage then ans is X-Z/X-Y = 33.33%.
	Rabindra, 2018, Nice @Pratik.
	Vikrantsinghchauhan, 2018, I am not understanding. Please explain the correct answer of this question.
	Vivek Yadav, 2018, 50% is correct.
	Mrityunjoy Mete, 2018, the fine aggregate of finesse modulus 2.6 is mix with a coarse aggregate of finesse modulus 6.8 for obtaining the aggregate of finesse modulus 5.4 then find our the percentage of coarse aggregate in the mix?Can anybody answer this?
	Gopal, 2019, Yes, you are right, Thanks @Naveen Kallan.
	Chintu, 2019, Agree @Naveen Kallan.The Answer is 33.33%.
	Chintu, 2019, Let the proportion of FA is =p.Then, proportion of CA will be =1-p , as sum of proportion is always 1.So, 2.6p+6.8(1-p)=5.4 , by solving it we get,  p=1/3=0.33, i.e in percentage it is 33.33% .But in option appropriate answer will be 30%.
	Anoms, 2019, (coarse -Combine)/(Combine-Fine) * 100.So, (6.8-5.4)/(5.4-2.6)*100 = 50%.
	Anand Ratan, 2019, X = course aggregates.Y = fine aggregates.Z = combined aggregates.Formula ,Fine modules ( % )= ( x - z / z - y ) x 100                                =( 6.8 - 5.4 / 5.2 - 2.6 ) x 100                               = 50% Correct answer is C .
	Khongthaw, 2020, Let Fine aggregates be P1.Course aggregates be P2.Combined aggregates be P.Given: P1=2.6, P2=6.8 and P=5.4.Fineness modulus = (P2 - P/P-P1) * 100.= 50%.
	Rahul, 2020, @Mrityunjoy mete.First, find the percentage of fine aggregate by FMc-FMcom/FMcm-FMf.Then the percentage of coarse aggregate will be 100- the percentage of fine aggregate.
	Dawood Khan, 2021, 5O is the correct answer.FM= x-z/z-y x100.X= coarse agg which is 6.8.Y = fine agg which is 2.6.Z= combined or obtained agg which is= 5.4.Now put the values in the above formula,= 6.8-5.4/5.4-2.6x100.=1.4/2.8x100,= 0.5x100,= 50.
	Khaled Mahmoud, 2021, Can anyone give me feedback about the previous percent 50%, Is this percent is relative to the total mix volume or related to the total volume of aggregates (coarse and fine)?  Please explain me.
	Pankaj Nagar, 2021, Here, The percentage of the aggregate of F.M. 2.6 to be combined.i.e  If the total amount of F.M 2.6 is 100% now then how much % of Aggregate will be required to obtain an F.M 5.4 with 100% amount of F. M. 6.8 aggregates.

The internal dimensions of a ware house are 15 m x 5.6 m, and the maximum height of piles is 2.70 m, the maximum number of bags to be stored in two piles, are
	Sharad, 2014, How this answer is possible please explain with size of cement bag or volume of cement bag?
	Prasanta Naskar, 2014, We know, surface area and height of pile required for storing of 1 bag of cement are 0.3 sq.m. and 0.18 m respectively.Therefore, maximum nos of bags can be stored into the given stored house = (15 x 5.6 x 2.7)/(0.3 x 0.18) = 4200.Therefore, options were wrong.
	Er. Soumya Choudhury, 2015, But volume of one cement bag is 0.035 cum.Volume of ware house is 15*5.6*2.7 = 226.8 cum.No of bags = 226.8/0.035 = 6480 bags. In two rows means 3240 bags.

	Ayon Som, 2016, The answer should be 3600 bags.Internal dimension of warehouse = 15m x 5.6mEffective storage floor area which is available: => (15m - 300mm - 300mm) x (5.6m - 300 mm - 300 mm) = 14.4m x 5m(300mm distance should be maintained from each exterior wall)Therefore, No. of bag in one layer = Available area / Area occupied by one bag:=> 14.4m x 5m / 0.3 sq.m = 240 bags in one layerNo. of layer of bags = Max. height of pile allowed / Height of one bag:=> 2.7m / 0.18 m = 15 layers.Therefore, total no. bags can be stored = Total bag in one layer X Total no. of layers:=> 240 X 15 = 3600 Bags of cementHence, The answer should be 3600 bags.

The datum temperature for maturity by Plowman, is
	Vikram, 2016, Please explain it.
	Manisha, 2016, Please explain this answer.
	Aniket, 2016, Plowman (1958) proposed a single strength-maturity equation based on past studies performed by various authors, with various mixtures, water-to-cement ratios, and curing temperatures between 11°F and 105°F (-12°C to 41°C). Using the logarithmic strength-maturity function, he proposed that the constants, a and b have specific values based on four strength ranges up to 10,000 psi. With this equation, Plowman found that any concrete strength could be estimated based on a given maturity, regardless of water-to-cement ratio, the curing temperature under 100°F, or aggregate-to-cement ratio, with an average error of 3%. Plowman's equation seemed to be valid, although his equation was only based on 26 different compressive strength values.
	Uma, 2016, Thanks @Aniket.
	Nikku, 2019, Thanks @Aniket.(11°F-32)*5/9 = -11.67°C.
	Nick Dongre, 2021, @Nikku.How did this come out?  Explain please.
	Komal, 2021, Maturity =summation of (time x temperature).Hydration of concrete continue to takes place up to -11 degree Celsius.Therefore, -11deg celsius is taken as a datum temperature for computing maturity.That means hydration calculated with respect to -11deg Celcius.

The shrinkage of concrete
	Apcivilian, 2016, No, it's wrong.Because shrinkage decreases with the age of concrete!
	Rosy Angelo, 2016, Please explain the Effect of shrinkage on the age of concrete.
	Narendra, 2018, Shrinkage decreases with time.
	Laksh, 2018, Shrinkage increases with age of concrete is true but its rate of shrinkage slows down starting from fresh concrete to older one. And its overall effect is the found to be increased with time (here we are not talking about rate but the changes in concrete due to shrinkage will be more as earlier because shrinkage, hydration, strengthening of concrete are continuous process that takes place throughout itself).
	Laksh, 2018, Drying shrinkage is defined as the contracting of a hardened concrete mixture due to the loss of capillary water. This shrinkage causes an increase in tensile stress, which may lead to cracking, internal warping, and external deflection, before the concrete is subjected to any kind of loading. All portland cement concrete undergoes drying shrinkage or hydral volume change as the concrete ages. The hydral volume change in concrete is very important to the engineer in the design of a structure. Drying shrinkage can occur in slabs, beams, columns, bearing walls, prestressed members, tanks, and foundations.Drying shrinkage is dependent upon several factors. These factors include the properties of the components, proportions of the components, mixing manner, amount of moisture while curing, dry environment, and member size. Concrete cured under normal conditions will undergo some volumetric change. Drying shrinkage happens mostly because of the reduction of capillary water by evaporation and the water in the cement paste. The higher amount of water in the fresh concrete, the greater the drying shrinkage affects. The shrinkage potential of a particular concrete is influenced by the amount of mixing, the elapsed time after the addition of water, temperature fluctuation, slumping, placement, and curing. The makeup of concrete is also very important. Each aggregate and cement type has distinctive characteristics, each contributing to concrete shrinkage. The amounts of water and admixtures used during mixing also have direct and indirect effects on drying shrinkage of concrete. Concrete shrinkage occurs mostly due to the evaporation of the mixing capillary water. The severity of this shrinkage depends on the physical properties of the concrete including size of the structure, location of the structure, and the surrounding temperature.Properties and Proportions of Components.The compositional makeup of concrete contributes directly to the drying shrinkage of concrete. Loss of moisture in the hydrated cement paste results in shrinkage. Different compositions and fineness of cement have variable effects on the shrinkage of cement paste. The difference in shrinkage is reduced significantly due to the adjustment of the amount of gypsum added to the different cement compositions. The size of aggregate is not as important but has an indirect influence on the water content of concrete. Shrinkage decreases with the volumetric increase of aggregate concentration causing a linear relationship between free shrinkage and cracks width. High-density aggregates and high modulus of elasticity of aggregates will decrease the compressibility and increase the shrinkage of concrete. The use of admixtures may alter the hydration reaction, which results directly in a high increase of drying shrinkage.Moisture:The concrete properties influence on drying shrinkage depends on the ratio of water to cementitious materials content, aggregate content, and total water content. The total water content is the most important of these. The relationship between the amount of water content of fresh concrete and the drying shrinkage is linear. The increase of the water content by one percent will approximately increase the drying shrinkage by three percent. Constant water to cementitious materials ratio coincides with changes in the amount of aggregate used.Dry Environment:The amount of drying shrinkage depends on the environmental conditions; relative humidity, temperature, and air circulation. Concrete subjected to a dry atmosphere will, in most cases, have a greater drying shrinkage than if subjected to an alternate wetting and drying. Lower temperatures generally produce a decrease in drying shrinkage because of higher humidity and slower evaporation.

Pick up the incorrect statement applicable to the field test of good cement.
	Shaurya, 2013, 1. We feel a cool feeling when we put our hand inside.2. We should feel a smooth feeling when we rub cement between our fingers.
	Dinesh Adhikaree, 2014, Answer E is wrong because the cement should be smooth and feel warm.Hence option D is correct.
	Mannjeet, 2015, We feel a cool feeling when we put our hand inside.We should feel a smooth feeling when we rub cement between our fingers.The color of the cement is grayish.A handful of cement thrown into a bucket of water should not sink immediately, it floats for few minutes.
	Vikas Sharma, 2016, I think it is C only.
	Shailendra, 2017, Option E is correct. I agree with the given answer.
	Premchand, 2017, FIELD TESTS ON CEMENT:1. DATE OF PACKING.Date of manufacture should be seen on the bag. It is important because the strength of cement reduces with age.2. COLOUR.The cement should be uniform in colour. In general the colour of cement is grey with a light greenish shade. The colour of cement gives an indication of excess lime or clay and the degree of burning.3. RUBBING.Take a pinch of cement between fingers and rub it. It should feel smooth while rubbing. If it is rough, that means adulteration with sand.4. HAND INSERTION.Thrust your hand into the cement bag and it should give cool feeling. It indicates that no hydration reaction is taking place in the bag.5. FLOAT TEST.Throw a small quantity of cement in a bucket of water. It should sink and should not float on the surface.6. SMELL TEST.Take a pinch of cement and smell it. If the cement contains too much of pounded clay and silt as an adulterant, the paste will give an earthy smell.7. PRESENCE OF LUMPS.Open the bag and see that lumps should not be present in the bag. It will ensure that no setting has taken place.8. SHAPE TEST.Take 100g of cement and make a stiff paste. Prepare a cake with sharp edges and put on the glass plate. Immerse this plate in water. Observe that the shape shouldn't get disturbed while settling. It should be able to set and attain strength. Cement is capable of setting under water also and that is why it is also called 'Hydraulic Cement'.9. STRENGTH TEST.A block of cement 25 mm*25 mm and 200 mm long is prepared and it is immersed for 7 days in water. It is then placed on supports 15000 mm apart and it is loaded with a weight of 340 N. the block should not show any sign of failure.
	Priya, 2018, As cement's SP gravity is more than that of water hence it should sink in water when thrown into a bucket of water.
	Nani, 2018, Thank you @Premchand.
	Tamanna, 2018, Option E is correct. I agree with the given answer.
	Balbonder Singh, 2019, Only option C is right.
	Navi, 2019, I think it is only option D.

If the effective working time is 7 hours and per batch time of concrete is 3 minutes, the output of a concrete mixer of 150 litre capacity, is
	Ajay, 2014, Can any solve this problem how this answer come?
	Bina, 2014, How do this answer came. Please explain ?
	Mohd Parvez, 2014, Effective working time= 7 hours = 7*60 = 420 minute.Per batch time = 3 minute.Number of mixing batch = 420/3 = 140. Output concrete of per batch = 150 litre.Output concrete 140 batch = 140*150 = 21000 litre.
	Amit, 2014, Effective working time= 7 hours = 7*60 = 420 minute.Per batch time = 3 minute.Number of mixing batch = 420/3 = 140. Output concrete of per batch = 150 litre.Output concrete 140 batch = 140*150 = 21000 litre.Assuming the efficiency = 90%.21000* 0.9 = 18900.
	Jay Shah, 2015, There is any clues for assuming the efficiency = 90%?

The condition not applicable to water cement ratio law, is

The most useless aggregate is one whose surface texture is
	Puja, 2016, Why? Give explanation.
	Prithvendra Singh, 2016, I think glassy aggregates will not form bonds easily with other components of concrete mixture.
	Sanket Gaikwad, 2016, Why only glassy rather than smooth? Smooth texture also not make good bond and fais in shear and compresive stress.
	Midhun Madhav, 2017, Glassy and smooth are different. A smooth aggregate for convenience can be a rounded aggregate also. It will have microscopic pores even though it may appear smooth to us. The bonding between the cement and aggregate would happen at this level rather than on the macro scale. Now, in the case of glassy, the surface lacks this microscopic rough surface. Hence Glassy is the correct answer.
	Ankur Bhatt, 2017, Thanks for the fabulous explanation @Midhun.
	Hir, 2017, How it can not be honey comb and porous?
	Abishkar, 2017, Agree @Midhun Madhav.The surface lacks this microscopic rough surface, that's why it is very suitable for concrete mix.D) honey combed and porous will be the answer.
	Amit, 2018, Agree @Midhun Madhav.
	Karan, 2018, Why not the correct answer is D?I agree @Midhun.
	Anjali, 2019, Honey combed and porous is the correct answer.
	Dillip, 2021, Agree, Thanks @ Anjali.
	Aman, 2021, Honeycomb and porous aggregates can be used in light weight structures so glassy will be correct.

The type of aggregates not suitable for high strength concrete and for pavements subjected to tension, is
	Chinu Singla, 2015, Why not flaky? Flaky also unsuitable for any type of construction.
	Sonu, 2017, I think it's Option E.
	Thara, 2017, The aggregate with rounded shape has the minimum percentage of voids ranging from 32 to 33%. It gives minimum ratio of surface area to given volume and hence requires minimum water for lubrication. It gives good workability for the given amount of water and hence needs less cement for a given water cement ratio. The only disadvantages is that the interlocking between its particles is less and hence the development of bond is poor. This is why rounded aggregate is not suitable for high strength concrete and for pavements subjected to tension.
	Sonu, 2017, I agree @Thara.
	Rabindra, 2018, Nice, Thanks @Thara.
	Riya, 2018, If void is less then surface area should be maximum.
	Anirban Roy, 2019, Flaky aggregate is also not suitable and so it is irregular.
	Rehaan, 2020, It should be Angular.
	Ashish Sharma, 2020, Rounded aggregate is very good for workability in a minimum quantity of water and cement. But very poor bouncing between ingredient. So according to question, it is poor for tension. But in the compression side, it is very good. So the answer is rounded aggregate.

This ratio cement concrete has good strength and is recommended for the following type of buildings and works:-- Used in footings of columns and raft foundation, used in beams, slabs, columns, stairs and walls of ordinary, single story and temporary buildings and used in retaining walls, pavements, floors and bedplates etc.- Where cement concrete 1:1:5:3 ratio is used it means 1 part cement 1.5 parts of fine aggregate or coarse sand and 3 parts of coarse aggregates. This ratio cement concrete has very good strength and is recommended for the following type of buildings and works:-- Important RCC structures, piles, arches, impermeable construction against water heads. This ratio of cement concrete is safe against earth quake.

The aggregate containing moisture in pores and having its suface dry, is known as
	Shailesh, 2016, I think the correct answer is C.
	Palash Puri, 2017, Yes, c is correct answer.
	Shri, 2017, Given answer D is correct.I agree with the given answer.
	Pramod, 2017, It is Dry aggregate.
	Shivraj Nath, 2017, C is the correct answer.
	Dineshkumar, 2017, Yes, C is correct answer because,1. The aggregate in which pores are filled with some moisture and having surface dry is called Dry Aggregate.2. The aggregate in which pores are filled with no moisture and having surface dry is called Very Dry Aggregate.3. The aggregate in which pores are filled with full moisture and having surface just wet is called Moist Aggregate.4. The aggregate in which pores are filled with full moisture and having surface just dry is called Aggregate.
	Maya Manu, 2018, Saturated surface dry (SSD) is defined as the condition of an aggregate in which the surfaces of the particles are "dry" (i.e,  surface adsorption would no longer take place), but the inter-particle voids are saturated with water.So the given answer is right.
	Mano, 2018, It's dry aggregate because when the press are completely filled with water it is saturated surface dry but here the just gave the pores have moisture so I think it's dry aggregate.
	Govinda, 2019, @All.Given option is right. Moisture is some pores called Dry aggregate & in all pores called saturated surface dry aggregate. In question it said moisture in all pores.
	Deepak Aryal, 2021, Answer is dry aggregate. In saturated surface dry aggregate all the pores are completely filled with water but having their surface dry.
	Pawan Yadav, 2021, In my opinion, the correct answer is C.

To hydrate 500kg of cement full water needed, is
	Aravind Gottumukkala, 2014, % wt of water required for full hydration is 38% by wt of cement.i.e., 38*150/100 = 57 kg.
	Amar Deep Tiwari, 2014, % wt of water required for full hydration is 26% by wt of cement.i.e., 26*500/100 = 130 kg.
	Soumya Choudhury, 2015, Actually it is 25% of corresponding water of cement.So 500*0.25 = 125 kg. But according to option given answer will be 130 kg.
	Sudhansu Bhagat, 2016, 24%for Hydration of cement  + 15% Jel of water. So 24 * 500 :- 120liter.
	Nazira, 2016, For complete reaction, 38% of water by weight of cement is required.0.38 * 500 = 190 kg.But the answer given in this option is 130kg.How?Can anybody explain?
	Swapnil Pokulwar, 2016, For 50kg cement, 35 kg water is needed.So, for 500kg its 135 = 130kg.
	Apcivilian, 2016, For chemical reaction 23% water needed. For complete reaction it is 38%.
	Suresh, 2016, 23% for hydration and 15% to fill gel pores.
	Saifalikhan, 2017, 25% for hyderation and 20% to fill gel pores.
	Vijay, 2017, 23+15=bound water +gel water = full hydration=0.38. So w/c=0.38=w/500 so w=500 * 0.38=190.
	Minesh, 2018, 25% water need to full hydrate of cement.
	Jayrajsinh, 2018, Cement 500= 0.35 m3   (   50 kg cement =0.035 m3 cement )Water Req for cement  38 %.= 0.35 * 38/woo.= 0.133 m3   Water.From Water Density.1000 kg/m3  =  (kg/0.133 m3).Water = 133 kg.
	Shahbaz, 2018, Thanks for the answer @Jayrasinhi.
	Uday Singh, 2018, Cement 500= 0.33 m3 ( 50 kg cement =0.033 m3 cement).Water Req for cement 38 %.= 0.33 * 38/woo.= 0.127 m3 Water.From Water Density.1000 kg/m3 = (kg/0.127 m3).Water = 127 kg.

	Ihsanullah, 2018, Water required from 26 to 35 percent.
	Vineet, 2019, 0.35*0.38  = 0.133.0.133 * 1000 = 133kg.
	Ali Zafar, 2019, It has been further estimated that about 15% by weight of cement is required to fill up the gel pores and 23% is used for hydation of cement process. So total 38% is required.
	Soumya, 2020, For hydration of cement 23% of water is required.So, for 500 kg cement, the answer should be 115kg.
	Gurjant Singh Sawna, 2020, For complete hydration, 38% water is needed.So 500*38/100 = 190 then how the answer is 130?
	Vishal, 2020, Total water requirements for Complete the chemical reaction Is 38 %, So, 38 * 500 = 19.Because 23% of the water required for hydration process & 15 water is required for filling the gell pores.So, total (23+15) = 38% water is required to complete the total chemical reaction.
	Rahul Rana, 2020, @Gurjant Singh Sawna.For Hydration of cement only it requires *26% of water by Mass* for 500kg of cement it will be 130 kg of water.Those who are saying 38% or 40% it is for (*Hydration+ gel pores filling*).It is NOT necessary that for the process of "Hydration" the gel pores should be "saturated".We think that fully hydration means for "chemical reaction & pores filling" both but Hydration means chemical reaction btwn cement and water only.
	Lund Chut, 2020, @ Rahul Rana.For hydration, it needs 23 per cent for hydration and 15 per cent for filling gel space.

If aggregates completely pass through a sieve of size 75 mm and are retained on a sieve of size 60 mm, the particular aggregate will be flaky if its minimum dimension is less than
	Reddy, 2013, 60+75 = 135.135/2 = 67.5.3/5*67.5 = 40.5.
	Aruna, 2014, (75+60)/2 = 67.5.                                                                      67.5*3/5 = 40.5.
	Prahumac, 2014, I didn't get it why did you multiply by 3.
	Itaf Hussain, 2014, @prahumac we multiply it by 3/5 of its mean dimension
	G.V.S. Bhargav, 2015, Flaky aggregate is a aggregate having a size less than 60% of average size of the two aggregate. So now (75+60)/2 = 67.5.Now 60% of 67.5 is,That is 67.5*(60/100) = 40.5.
	Sankalp Kadam, 2016, If avg has be taken into account then according to me, as agg are retained on 60mm sieve size so it means that most of the agg are retained at 60 so we have to consider that value only rather than taking commutative of two sieve sizes isn't it? Means (3/5) * 60 that comes 36mm.
	Abhi, 2017, Flakiness means 3/5 of mean size of aggregate, therefore it is 40.5.
	Sandeep Kumar, 2017, Since we know.Flakiness index= .6(3/5)* average size of aggregate((passing size+retained size)/2).So it is 40.5mm.
	Avijit Mirdda, 2017, Average of 75mm and 60 mm sieve is (75+60)/2=67.5.For flaky if its minimum dimension is less than 3/5 of 67.5 i.e. 67.5*(3/5) = 40.5mm.
	Yasir, 2018, Flakiness means 60%(3/5) of avg size of particles.
	Lalit, 2019, Flaky aggregate = 3/5 of there's mean dimension.                             = (3/5) *{(75+60) /2}.                            = (3/5) *(67.5).                             = 40.5mm.
	Anurag, 2020, An aggregate is said to be flaky if its least dimension is less that 3-5. That's why we use 3÷5.
	Abbas Khan, 2021, If an aggregate is rounded or angular then what value will be use about dimension? Please explain.

If the slump of a concrete mix is 60 mm, its workability is
	Nawal Kishor Dwivedi, 2015, The degree of workability of concrete and its slump values are given as follows:Very Low 0-25 mm slump.Low 25-50 mm slump.Medium 50-100 mm slump.High 100-150 mm slump.
	Suman Mitra, 2016, Very low 0-25 mm,Low 25-75mm,Medium 75-100mm,High 100-150mm,In that sense 60 mm slump must be medium.
	Papai Ghosh, 2017, <25mm-very low workability.25-50mm-low workability.50-75mm-medium workability.75-125mm-high workability.
	Pakkirappa, 2017, 1) Very dry mixes having slump 0 " 25 mm are typically used in road making,2) low workability mixes having slump 10 " 40 mm are typically used for foundations with light reinforcement,3) medium workability mixes with slump 50 " 90 mm, are typically used for normal reinforced concrete placed with vibration,4) high workability concrete with slump > 100 mm is typically used where reinforcing has tight spacing, and/or the concrete has to flow a great distance.
	Mano, 2018, You are correct, agree @Papai Ghosh.
	Mohammed, 2018, Why we use very dry mix in road making?
	Niranjan, 2019, Medium is the correct answer according to IS:456-2000 Clause no-7.1.
	Safiur Rahaman Molla, 2020, The degree of workability of concrete and its slump values are;(1) Very Low 0 - 25 mm slump(2) Low 25-50 mm slump.(3) Medium 50-100 mm slump.(4) High 100-150 mm slump.

A flaky aggregate is said to be elongated if its length is
	Sam, 2015, It should be thrice as 3/5*3= 9/5.
	Rupesh, 2015, Flaky = 3/5*mean, i.e mean = 5/3*flaky.Elongated = 9/5*mean.= (9/5)*(5/3)*flaky = 3*flaky.Hence, answer is thrice.
	Darshan H A, 2016, Aggregate is said to be flaky if its size is more than 9/5 of their mean size. i.e 1.8 times of their mean size which is approximately taken as 2 times in this problem.
	Sohan Jangra, 2017, I agree, the right answer is B.
	Shweta Puri, 2017, The size of flaky aggregate is 3/5 =0.6 as well the size of an elongated particle is 9/5=1.8.When we multiply 0.6 with 3 then the answer is 1.8 which is the size of an elongated particle as mention in the question. So option C is correct.
	Vikas, 2017, The size of flaky aggregate is 3/5 =0.6 as well the size of an elongated particle is 9/5 = 1.8.When we multiply 0.6 with 3 then the answer is 1.8 which is the size of an elongated particle as mention in the question. So option C is correct.
	Minesh, 2017, I think C is the correct answer.
	Rajesh Meena, 2017, Yes, the correct answer is C.
	Roy, 2017, Given answer is right according to SSC JE 2008 answer key. And the reason behind it according to me, the flaky aggregate is considered to their MEAN dimension and elongated aggregate is considered to their GREATER dimension.
	Jaydip, 2017, 1.8 time mean six size.
	Kanemozhe, 2017, Option B is correct because for flaky aggregates the size is considered for the width, not the length. Only when it comes to elongated aggregates, length is taken into account. And the question is regarding the length.
	Pavan, 2018, 1.8 approximatly twice the mean dimension
	Viru Kapoor, 2018, Option C is absolutely correct.Flaky aggregates 3/5 = 0.6.Elongated aggregates 9/5 = 1.8.Any Flaky aggregate is said to be elongated if (0.6*3 = 1.8)Which Means thrice.
	Amol, 2018, Option B is correct because the answer will be from mean size.9/5 is the mean size.
	Neelkanth, 2018, Option C is correct according to IS456.
	Sandeep Suthar Hisar, 2018, Twice is the Correct answer because mean size is not 0.6 it is the length but we have calculated with respect to mean size so 9/5 is the correct answer.
	Dheeraj, 2018, Can someone explain. Why it's twice?
	Narayan Ch. Saha, 2020, Given answer is correct. Because there is no relation b/w flaky and elongation.So 1.8~2 times.
	Gaurav, 2020, Flaky aggregate .6 times mean dimensions. Elongated aggregate 1.8 times mean dimensions. So, Flaky aggregate is 3 times of elongated aggregate.
	Guddi Meena, 2020, It shoul be thrice.
	Ablesh Meena, 2020, C is the right option, The size of flaky aggregate is 3/5 =0.6 as well the size of an elongated particle is 9/5 = 1.8.When we multiply 0.6 with 3 then the answer is 1.8 which is the size of an elongated particle as mention in the question. So option C is correct.
	Suraj, 2021, C is the correct answer.
	Ravi Kumar Poodi, 2021, It is clearly mentioned that the flaky aggregate, not the FLAKINESS INDEX is equating to the ELONGATION INDEX. For a flaky aggregate, there should be a FI and EI. Hence, FI= 3/5 of mean size and EI= 9/5 of mean size.So the given answer is CORRECT----> option B.
	Abhinay, 2021, 1)The aggregate is said to be elongated if its greater length is greater than 9/5th of its mean dimension.Flaky means have less thickness, elongated means having more length.A flaky aggregate is said to be elongated if its length is twice the mean size.Elongated aggregates are unsuitable for pavement design.Elongated aggregates can be found with the help of a length gauge.Important Points:An aggregate is said to be flaky is its least dimension is less than 3/5 of the mean dimension and the aggregate is said to be elongated if its greatest dimension is greater than 1.8 times the mean dimension.So, the option B is the correct answer.
	Santosh, 2021, Mean of flaky aggregate is (3+5) /2=4.Ellongated agg. 5/8.So elongated length 8 but mean of flaky 4.Then 2 times.



To prevent segregation, the maximum height for placing concrete, is
	Darpan Gautam, 2015, As per IS 456 clause 13 transporting, placing.Compaction and curing height = 150 cm.
	Satishpujari, 2015, 13.2 Placing.The concrete shall be deposited as nearly as practicable in its final position to avoid re-handling. The concrete shall be placed and compacted before initial setting of concrete commences and should not be subsequently disturbed. Methods of placing should be such as to preclude segregation. Care should be taken to avoid displacement of reinforcement or movement of form work. As a general guidance, the maximum permissible free fall of concrete may be taken as 1.5 m.

If the effective plan area of a warehouse is 54 sq. m, and maximum height of piles permitted is 270 cm, the number of cement bags to be stored, is
	Kenmen, 2015, We know, surface area and height of pile required for storing of 1 bag of cement are 0.3 sq. m. and 0.18 m respectively.Therefore, maximum nos of bags can be stored into the given stored house = (5.4 x 2.7)/(0.3 x 0.18) = 2700 bags.
	Sivareddy, 2016, How did you change 54 sq meters in 5.4?Please explain about it.
	Darshan H A, 2016, Surface area and height of pile required for storing of 1 bag of cement is 0.3 sq M and 0.18 m.Therefore maximum number of bags can be stored into the given house = (54 x 2.7) / (0.3 x 0.18) = 2700 bags.
	Vivek Bhardwaj, 2017, One bag of cement occupy 0.034cum vol.Why you take 0.054?
	Anurag Garg, 2017, But as per rules, it is necessary to keep min 30 cm distance between the wall and first column of cement to avoid moisture.
	Chris, 2018, Effective plan area is given. Already 30cm excluded.
	Ravneet, 2018, The total volume of room 54 * 2.7.The volume of 1 cement bag .0347. The Number of cement bags = 54 * 2.7/.0347 = 4200.
	Jas, 2018, It's the volume of cement bag not of cement;Height of bag - 0.18m (or)  18 cm.And area - 0.3sqm (or) 3000 sq cm.So the volume  of bag = 0.18 * 0.3 = 0.054 cum,Volume of room - 54 * 2.7= 145.8 cum.No of bags= volume of room (warehouse)/volume of one cement bag.= 54 * 2.7/(0.18 * 0.3),=145.8/0.054, = 2700.
	Deepu Maurya, 2018, 54 * 2.7 &div; (.054) = 2700.
	Sami, 2019, How to find the height and area bag?
	Sikindra Yadav, 2019, Yeah, you are right, agree @Darshan H A.
	Rahul Swarnkar, 2019, 54*2.7/.18*.3 = 2700 bags.
	Yash, 2019, When you take 1 bag of cement and  plan on group its cover 3000 sq cm area.And bag height is 18 cm.That by they use .3 Sqm and .18m  here For a bag.
	Beewaek Mandal, 2020, Simply,No. Of bags = Effective area* Max.ht of piles/ Area coverd by one bag cement is 0.3 sq.m & ht= 0.18.No= 54*2.7/0.3*0.18 = 2700 bags you must get.
	Er. Anshu Kumar, 2020, Here,In first case: (standard):Area required for 1 bag of cement = 0.3 sq.m,Height of pile required for 1 bag = 0.18 m,Volume of 1 bag = 0.3 * 0.18,= 0.054 cubic metre.In second case:(Given): Plan area of warehouse = 54 sq.m, Max.height of piles = 270 cm =2.7 m, Volume of warehouse = 54*2.7.= 145.8 cubic metre.We know that;No.of bags = volume of warehouse/volume of 1 bag of cement.                     = 145.8/0.054.                     = 2700 bags.(Answer).
	Chandan Nandi, 2020, 2.7 How come? Please explain.
	Longti, 2020, 270cm = 2.7m.
	Sk Pratap, 2021, As per rule the distance between two piles is 1.6m and distance from wall is 30cm and 20cm above the ground level is not included in this calculation why?According to rule, it is a bad way to store cement in ware house.
	Jahangir Abbas Sahil, 2021, The total volume of room = 54 * 2.7 = 145.8 cu.m.The volume of 1 cement bag = 0.0347 (This is the wet Volume and We've to calculate at site in dry Volume).Dry Volume= Wet Vol * 1.54 to 1.57,Dry Volume= 0.0347 * 1.5,Dry Vol= 0.053 cu.m.The Number of cement bags = 145.5cum/0.053 = 2745.Hence, 2745 is near to 2700.

The dimensions of a 35 litre forma for measuring aggregates by volume, are :
	Shakeel, 2014, .27 x .27 x.48 = .035 cum.1000 litre = 1 cum.35 litres = 1/1000 x 35 = .035 cum.

	Midhun Madhav, 2017, No, @Devaraj. It is 27 X 27 X 48.35 litres are chosen because, while doing nominal mixing ( like for M15 at 1:2:4) etc, the volume of 1 in cement represents 1 bag of cement which is of 0.035m3 which in turn is 35 Litres. So, the volume of container measuring should also be 35 Litres. Hence, the containers volume  L * B * H.which is 27 * 27 * 48 which gives 35000ml => 35 L. The answer given in key is correct.

If 50 kg of fine aggregates and 100 kg of coarse agregates are mixed in a concrete whose water cement ratio is 0.6, the weight of water required for harsh mix, is
	Mohanad, 2013, How to get this result?
	M.Pagavandoss, 2014, Wt of water = (5% wt of coarse aggregate+30% wt of cement)*w/c ratio.
	Sumit, 2014, 100 (wt.of Coarse sand) *5/100 = 5 kg.50 (wt.of cement)* 30/100 = 15 kg.(5+15)*0.6 = 12 kg.
	Vishal Singh, 2015, Its 30% of coarse aggregate.
	Nungsang, 2015, If the mix is 1:3:6, Can we use same method keeping same wc ratio?
	Nabaratna, 2015, WC ratio is always 0.6.
	Ruhi Varshney, 2016, Water cement ratio are different for different grade of concrete.
	Bahram, 2016, @Sumit, 50 kg is the weight  of fine aggregates, not cement.
	Baloch, 2016, Please read question, it's giving 50kg of FINE aggregates not cement.
	Buet-K, 2016, C = 1/7 * 150 = 21  (1;2;4) = 7,W/C = 0.6,W = 0.6 * 21 = 12.
	Apcivilian, 2016, .3p+.1F+.01C= w/c xpBy using this equation, we can get the answer.
	Concretesukan, 2016, (5% Coarse aggregate + 30% of cement (50kg here))* w/c is correct.
	Prem. Vit Jaipur, 2016, 0.3 * cement + 0.1FA + 0.01CA = w/c * cement.                   0.3 * cement + 0.1 * 50 + .01 * 100 = 0.6*cement.           cement = 20.     W/c = 0.6 water = 0.6 * 20 = 12kg.
	Bivek Pradhanang, 2017, D. w= 5%aggregate + 30%cement and here w/c=0.6. So, here w= .05 * [50+150]+.3 * [w/0.6], so w = 15kg.
	Sanjeev Kr Anad, 2017, W/c *p = 0.3p +0.1F +0.01C.0.6*p =  0.3p + 0.1*50 +0.01*100,0.3p = 5+1,P = 6 &div; 0.3,P = 20.Then, water= w/c*P =0.6 * 20 = 12kg  ans.
	Ram Soni, 2017, Thanks @Apcivilian & @Prem. Vit.
	Pakkirappa, 2017, Thank you very much @Sanjeev Kr Anad.
	Qazi, 2017, Why water cement ratio always used in weight?

	Priya, 2017, W/C*P = 0.3P + 0.1Y + 0.01Z.
	Hussain, 2018, W= 30%cement +4%of total aggregates.W=. 3W/.6+.04*150  (Given- Water/Cement =0.6).W=. 5W+6.W=12kg.
	Kunal, 2018, Thank you so much for explaining it.
	Qasim Afzal, 2018, W/C *P = 0.3P + 0.1F.A +.01C.A0.6*P=0.3P+0.1(50)+0.01(100)0.6P-0.3P=5+1P=6/0.3P=20 => Where P= Cement.Now;W/C=0.6,W=C*0.6,W=20*0.6,W=12Kg.
