If the irrigation efficiency is 80%, conveyance losses are 20% and the actual depth of watering is 16 cm, the depth of water required at the canal outlet, is
	Gshah, 2014, Taking 25 as a flow, loss = 25*.20 = 5.Remain = 20 efficiency = 80%.So .20*.80 = 16.Answer is 25 c.m.
	Priyanka Suryavanshi, 2014, Not understood. Can you elaborate?
	Rana Faheem, 2015, Is there any relation or formula for it?
	Rahul, 2015, Hence, after losses = X-X/5 = 4X/5.= 4X/5*80/100 = 16.X = 25.
	Zemenu, 2015, Crop water requirement ETo = Gross irrigation requirement*Irrigation efficiency. So 16*80% = 10.4.
	Rosh, 2016, Depth of water required considering conveyance losses = 16/0.8 = 20 cm (20% is lost in conveyance means only 80% in reached. So we have to provide more depth of water).Irrigation efficiency is 80% (means if I provide 20 cm depth of water then only 80% land will get irrigated).To make 100 % irrigation, Depth of water from canal outlet = 20/0.8 = 25 cm.I hope its clear.
	Sharyu, 2016, Yes, Thanks @Rosh.
	Rajat, 2017, Yes, now clearly understood thank you @Rosh.
	Tanu, 2017, Thank you so much @Rosh.
	Anil, 2017, Thank you all.
	Jeff, 2017, @Anil.What if efficiency is 80% and conveyance losses is 12%?
	Shreya Doshi, 2017, Thank you @Rosh.
	Amrutha Raveendran, 2017, But @Rosh.Here the water depth is asked for 80% efficiency so 20cm is enough. Don't it is?
	Indranil Karfa, 2018, Depth of water after losses =x -x/5=4x/5.Again Depth of water after losses=16*(100&div;80)=20cm.So, 4x/5=20,  x=25 cm.
	Sachin Solanki, 2018, It is 16/(0.8*0.8).
	Sv Ranganadh, 2018, (100-800)/(100-20) = 0.25*% = 25cm.
	Akash Vardhan, 2018, Thanks all of you for explaining the answer.
	Mayur, 2018, Not understood. Please explain me clearly.
	Amar, 2018, Nice explaination @Rosh.
	Khushi, 2019, Thanks @Sv Ranganadh.
	Nareshsingh, 2019, X* (80/100)-x * (80/100 * 20/100) = 16.So x = 25.
	Bhawesh, 2020, 20/(0.8*0.8)=25.80% Conveyance efficiency.80% Irrigation Efficiency.
	Bhumika Varshney, 2020, Not getting, please anyone  explain.
	Ghulam Mujatab, 2020, 16*.80= 12.8 efficiency,16*.20= 3.2 loss,16+(12.8-3.2)=25.6~25.
	Samim, 2020, 16/(0.8 *0.8) = 25.80% = 0.8,100 - 20 % = 0.8.
	Amit, 2020, Overall efficiency (water application efficiency*conveyance efficiency) = {water actually stored to the root zone/water delivered to the field}.Therefore,0.8 * 0.8 = 16/x.So, x = 25cm..
	Shrikant, 2021, For 80% conveyance efficiency, depth=16cm (means here 20% loss as given in Qs.)For 100% Conveyance efficiency Depth would be = ((16/80)*100) = 20cm.Now for 80% irrigation efficiency( as given in QS.) and for 100% conveyance efficiency(as we have calculated above) is =20cm.=> for 100% irrigation efficiency and 100% conveyance efficiency the required depth will be= ((20/80)*100) = 25cm.
	Bishow, 2021, Let depth at canal be x then 20% loss = x - 20% of x = 0.8x,From 0.8x only 80% taken by soil that equals 16cm in depth,So,80% of 0.8X = 16, Solve x=25.
	Shahid, 2021, 0.8x - (0.8x * 0.2) = 16.Then, x = 25cm.
	Ahmad Mujtaba, 2021, (100-Efficiency) / (100-Loss) = 0.25.0.25 x 100.= 25cm.

If d1 is the depth of cutting, d2 is the height of the bank from bed level r2 : 1 and r1 : 1 are the slopes in filling and cutting respectively, the horizontal distance n between the bed and bank, is
	Dipak, 2017, So the height of bank from the top of the canal is d2-d1. Again, fiilng slope is r2:1 so horizontal length = (d2-d1)*r2.And cutting slope is r1:1, horizontal length= d1*r1.So, horizontal distance between bed and bank x= (d2-d1)*r2+d1*r1=r2d2.

The structure constructed to allow drainage water to flow under pressure through an inverted syphon below a canal, is called
	Chintu, 2017, What is Super Passage?When the canal is in full cutting and there is sufficient head way for the canal water a Super Passage may be proposed. That is if the Full supply level (F.S.L) of canal is below a depth of free board required for canal from bed level of drain crossing, at this point a super passage may be constructed to let the discharge of drain without causing any damage to the canal.What is Aqueduct?Aqueduct is a structure that may be proposed on canal that is running in full embankment and there is sufficient way to pass the discharge of drain. That is if the Maximum Flood Level (M.F.L) of drain is below a depth of free board required in drain from Bed Level Canal, at this point an aqueduct may be proposed to let the discharge of drain below canal without causing any damage to the canal.What is Canal Syphon?When the canal runs partially in cutting, the canal syphon may be adopted. A barrel will be provided below the bed level of drain to pass the discharge of canal by depressing canal Full supply level (F.S.L) before the drain crossing and again lift it back after crossing the drain. Sufficient head may be provided to force the water from up stream of canal to down stream of canal. This is often provided for drain crossing that have huge discharge and an Under Tunnel may not be economic.
	Jeevan, 2017, I agree with the given answer.
	Ajit Ranjan, 2017, Syphan aqueduct?
	Jam, 2018, As far as I know, Its moves under pressure. If its moves freely then its aqueduct.
	Dheeraj, 2020, Yes @jam, you are correct. Answer is aqueduct.Because if there is well define vertical clearance between the F. S. L of drain and bed slope of canal so it is called Aqueduct.
	Bread, 2021, @Dheeraj.Jam is saying under pressure is syphon aqueduct so here the answer should be syphon aqueduct only. If it moves freely then it will be aqueduct.

A hydraulic jump is generally formed when a stream moving with
	Subhajit Ghosh, 2018, HYDRAULIC JUMP: When a stream of water with high velocity and low depth (i. e Supercritical flow) strikes another stream of water moving with a low velocity and high depth (i.e Sub-critical flow), a sudden rise in the surface of the former takes place.

The field capacity of a soil is 25%, its permanent wilting point is 15% and specific dry unity weight is 1.5. If the depth of root zone of a crop, is 80 cm, the storage capacity of the soil, is
	Fazeelat, 2014, Would you please give me the solution step?
	Badal Shirbhayye, 2014, How to solve?
	Badal Shirbhayye, 2014, dw = sgd[Fc- PWP].= 1.5x80x[0.25-0.15].= 12.
	Ipsita Sahoo, 2015, Explain dw, sgd, [Fc-PWP]?
	Anitha Chetti, 2016, Here, s = Specific gravity of soil,d = Depth of root zone,Fc = Field capacity,PWP = Permanent wilting point.
	Mariya, 2017, dw mean?Explain @Badal.
	Animesh Mahanty, 2017, Thanks for the explanation.
	Subha, 2017, @Mariya.Here, dw means the depth of water zone. i.e., storage capacity of soil.
	Kalu Charan Behera, 2018, For formula is .....(Yd/Yw)*d*(field capacity-permanent welting point)Given data:-Yd=1.5,Yw=1,Depth zone=80 cm,Field capacity=35%=35/100=0.35,Permanent wilting point=15%=15/100=0.15,So,  (1.5/1)*80*(0.35-0.15)=12 cm,Correct answer is 12 cm.
	Prasenjt, 2018, Here; Sp *d(fc-pwp).
	Goora, 2019, @Kalu.FC is 25%.
	Tushar, 2019, The field capacity and moisture content at the time of irrigation are 27% and 19%. The apparent specific gravity 1.3 and root zone depth 100cm. Determine the time required to irrigate 2ha with a flow of 60 if water loss is 20%. Can anyone answer this?
	Khushi, 2019, Thanks for explaining @Badal.
	Anil, 2019, For formula is .....(Yd/Yw)*d*(field capacity-permanent welting point).Given data:-Yd=1.5, Yw=1,Depth zone=80 cm,Field capacity=25%=25/100=0.25,Permanent wilting point=15%=15/100=0.15,So, (1.5/1)*80*(0.35-0.15)=12 cm,Correct answer is 12 cm.

If the optimum depth of kor watering for a crop is 15.12 cm, the outlet factor for the crop for four week period in hectares per cumec, is
	Keerthana, 2014, Delta = 15.12 cm = 0.1512 m.B = 4 week = 28 days.Delta(met) = 8.64*28(days)/D(hect/cumec).D = 1600 hect/met.
	Iklak Husain, 2016, Delta= 15.12 cm = 0.1512 m.B = 4 week = 28 days.Delta = (8.64 * 28)/0.1512.          =  1600 hect/cumec.
	Pritesh Dabhade, 2017, D=[864B/Δ].   = [864 * 28]/15.12,   = 1600 hac/cum.
	S.K.M., 2017, Δ=15.12cm=.1512m. B=4*7=28days.Duty(D)=8.64B/Δ=8.64*28/.1512=1600 ha/cumec.
	Shankar Samad, 2019, Given is ;δ = 15.12cm = 0.1512mm.b = 4 * 7 = 28 days.We know that the relation of δ, base period and duty is;δ =8.64*b/duty.so, duty=8.64*b/δ.= 8.64 * 28/0.1512 = 1600 is the answer.

Lacy's regime condition is obtained if
	Imdad Gichki, 2021, Please explain Lacy's regime condition.
	Geethu, 2021, For true regime condition;Discharge, silt charge, silt grade, and flow must be constant.Also, the flow should occur through a material that can easily be scoured and deposited, say incoherent alluvium.These are the conditions for a true regime.Apart from that, there is the initial and final regime.Lacy's theory is not applicable for the true regimes since the proposed criteria won't occur together in a real situation.

A district road with a bituminous pavement has a horizontal curve of 1000 m for a design speed of 75 km ph. The super-elevation is

	Kara Lala, 2019, GSB - Granular Sab Base.WMM - Wet Mix Macadam.
	Arjun, 2019, Design superelevation is always taken as 75% of the Design Speed hence it is e = v^2/225R.
	Mohammed, 2019, @Azanaw.You will find a clear explanation in IRC 38 codebook. However, I will try to briefly summarise it to you.Basically superelevation is provided to counteract the centrifugal force experienced by a vehicle when it's negotiating a curve. Now counteracting the full centrifugal force developed for a given design speed would require us to provide superelevation of more than 7%. But IRC restricts the maximum value of e= 7%.So in practice for mixed traffic, most vehicles travel at less than design speed (where we have both slow and fast-moving vehicles) so as a compromise since we can\'t provide the actual value we design superelevation to resist the full centrifugal force developed at 75% design speed.Eg: If the design speed of a National Highway is 80kmph. We design the superelevation to fully resist the centrifugal force developed due to 75% of 80kmph = 60 kmph.This method of giving superelevation will not be inconvenient to fast-moving vehicles but would be of aid to slow-moving vehicles.
	Ramkishor Sahu, 2019, Superelevation is always.e+f=v^2/127R.But for the design of the superelevation we take,e=V^2/225R.So, Here velocity 75 kmph and horizontal curve 1000 meter given.That means it is the radius of the horizontal curve.Note:- Horizontal curve always given in the form of the radius.Put these values to find the horizontal curve,e = 75^2 / (225*1000).e = 1/40.Thank you.
	Jeet, 2020, Super elevation = V^2 / 225 R.= 75x75 / 225x1000.= 0.025 = 1 in 40.
	Just, 2020, 75*75/(225*1000) = 75/(3*1000) = 25/1000  =  5/200 = 1/40.
	Pramit Samanta, 2020, If mixed traffic or maximum value was mentioned then it has to be e + f = V2/225R. Otherwise for design purpose e + f = V2/127R.
	Sushil, 2020, Super elevation = V^2 / 225 R.= 75x75 / 225x1000.= 0.025 = 1 in 40.
	Umesh, 2020, as per IRC  e=v^/225.R.Here, In question, it should be 1000m radius
	Elavarasy R, 2021, v^2/225R. = 75 x 75/227 x 1000.= 0.025.=1in40.
	Dr. Anuj, 2021, This will as per the design steps given by IRC for heterogeneous traffic. Step one is, you have to find the value of e (superelevation) neglecting friction for 75% of design speed. If the value so obtained is less than 0.07, the design is over and that value is taken as the designed value of superelevation.

Thickness of a pavement may be reduced considerably by
	Sreenivas, 2013, The major factors which governs the thickness of the pavement are 'Traffic' and 'subgrade soil strength' apart from climatic conditions ( frost action etc.), material characteristics etc. However, there were no options related to the above discussed. Option (A) affects the thickness because the subgrade soil strength (measured in CBR(%)) changes with the density of the soil. Option (B) also affects the thickness because the stabilized soils do possess higher soil strength/desired properties than the unstabilized. Option (C) deals with the drainage of the soils. Drainage affects the performance of the pavement. If the pavement results in a poor performance under drainage condition, an additional "drainage layer" should be provided (IRC 37:2012) and also while strengthening the roads, it is expected to get more characteristic deflection results in providing more thickness than the properly drained pavement.THEREFORE, all the options ( compaction, stabilization, drainage ) do affects the pavement thickness. Hence, OPTION (D) is the ANSWER.
	Sanjay, 2014, The pavement mainly depends upon the compaction, stabilization and drainage of soil for his reduction.
	Jahangeer, 2016, Thickness of pavement can be decrease by the soil compaction hence the voids are removed.
	Dakpo Saviour Dela Doyen, 2016, @Sreenivas.Your explanation is adequate in content and reference. I think drainage is important because if the pavement is poorly drained, the particles in the layer tend to loose their compressive strength as a result of reduced inter-particle friction. This can cause the pavement layer to deform excessively and fail.
	Prince Kumar, 2019, Thickness of pavement with compaction or without compaction.Aggregate size for pavement, sub base, base course.
	Gowtham, 2019, The amount of compaction is done by considering the loads applied on the pavement so the pavement thickness depends on the amount of loads applied on the pavement surface but not by the compaction.



The total length of a valley formed by two gradients - 3% and + 2% curve between the two tangent points to provide a rate of change of centrifugal acceleration 0.6 m/sec2, for a design speed 100 km ph, is
	Venkatesh, 2013, Here N = 0.03+0.02 = 0.05.L = 0.38(NV^3)^0.5.L = 84.76MTS APPROX.

For the movement of vehicles at an intersection of two roads, without any interference, the type of grade separator generally preferred to, is
	Madan Gorathoki, 2013, Clover leaf is used when two high volume and high speed facilities intersect each other. It requires only one structure and is very popular where as Diamond interchange involves a major-minor crossing and is designed for a narrow right of way of major road.
	Neeraj Maurya, 2015, What is the clover leaf?
	Madan Gorathoki, 2016, Cover leaf is a two-level interchange in which left turns (reverse directions in left-driving regions), are handled by ramp roads. The objective of a cloverleaf is to allow two highways to cross without the need for any traffic to be stopped by red lights, even for left and right turns.
	Vishwajeet Keshri, 2016, Please, explain the other grades.
	Shilpa, 2016, What is grade separator?
	Amit, 2016, Grade separator means changing in the gradient i.e. rise or fall of road surface.
	Haylee, 2017, @ Shilpa.Grade separation is the name given to a method of aligning a junction of two or more surface transport axes at different heights (grades) so that they will not disrupt the traffic flow on other transit routes when they cross each other.
	Abhishek, 2017, What is diamond interchange?
	Hari Hajare, 2018, Traffic is moving at different level at the intersection such intersection is called as grade separated intersection.Ex. Clover leaf intersection.
	Wubrst, 2018, Please, explain about other grade separators.
	Janhavi, 2019, Please explain other type of seperators.
	Shuvam Thakur, 2019, @Janhavi.Grade separator means changing in the gradient i.e. rise or fall of road surface.
	Sapan Kumar, 2019, A cloverleaf interchange is a two-level interchange in which left turns are handled by ramp roads. To go left, vehicles first continue as one road passes over or under the other, then exit right onto a one-way three-fourths loop ramp and merge onto the intersecting road.

The absolute minimum radius of horizontal curve for a design speed 60 km ph is
	Gurdeep Singh, 2013, R = 60*60/127(.07+.15) = 128.8.
	Jagu, 2014, e+f = V*V / 127 R.R = V*V/ 127*(0.07+.15).
	Sohan Lal Jangra, 2014, Please tell me formula for this?
	Mukesh, 2014, R = v*v/(e+f)*g.
	Ihsan Baloch, 2015, R = V^2/(e+f)g.
	Rajnish, 2016, Guys can you tell me where we have to use 127 and where 225?
	Neeraj Yadav, 2016, If we calculate for signal traffic, we will take 225.
	Bhavya Jagyasi, 2016, If we have to calculate the superelevation for only single vehicle then we use 127 and when we have to calculate superelevation for design speed (for all vehicle which are going to move on curve) we use 225.
	Rajat, 2016, R = V * V/27.5 = 131 mSo, the answer is A.
	Snehal, 2016, You cannot use (e + f) = v^2/127R. It is for super-elevated curves not for horizontal curves.

	Tina, 2019, e= 0.07 & f= 0.15 (both are constant).e + f= V^2/127R.Where: R= radius of horizontal Curve.R= 60^2/127(0.07+0.15).R= 128.84m,Note: here V is in kmph.

If x% is the gradient of an alignment and y% is the gradient after proper superelevation along a curved portion of a highway, the differential grade along the curve, is
	Samm, 2016, Why not (x-y) %? Please explain.
	Ng Reddy, 2016, That is called grade compensation.At the time of horizontal curves, we reduce the gradient 75/r or (30 + r) /r.
	Andal, 2017, The normal gradient ( slope of the ground ) is x% which is the same value at the point of Transition curve. The super elevation is starting from this Transition point and ending at the mid of curvature of the horizontal curve.Hence, after super elevation attained, the difference between gradient is (y-x)%.
	Bb Bande, 2017, Please explain this.
	Garry, 2018, The Answer is correct because Y% is the gradient after attaining full superelevation.Previously it has X%. So the difference will be {Y-X}%.
	Ankush, 2018, y-x is the right answer.
	Anom, 2018, How the gradient is increased after attaining super elevation?Please suggest.
	Hira, 2020, The grade is decreased in a curve, not increased. It is called grade compensation. Y-X % is differential grade. Although, y% is lesser or flatter than x%.

The absolute minimum sight distance required for stopping a vehicle moving with a speed of 80 km ph, is
	Dinesh, 2014, SSD = lag distance+braking distance.    = v.t   +   v^2/2gf.    = 22.22*2.5  +  (22.2)^2/(2*9.81*0.35).    =       126.5 (near to 120) So answer is (a)120m.Where v is in m/s and t = reaction time(2.5sec acc. to IRC).      f is longitudinal coefficient of friction.
	Sarang Mote, 2016, What is the lag distance?
	Jitendra Singh, 2016, Lag distance = vt.V = m/ s.T = sec.
	Salman Mohammed, 2016, SSD = 0.27vt + v.v/254(f +-g/100).Where t = 2.5.f = b/n 0.43 and 0.4 interpolate. So to answer the question in meter we must use this equation.
	Navi, 2017, Actually its from the direct values given in the books (for 20kmph = 20). So as (25=25) (30=30) (40=45) (50=60)  (60=80) (80=120) & (100=180).
	Ankur, 2017, How to predict the value of f in such cases?
	Asita, 2017, Yes, right @Navi.
	Ankur, 2017, We can predict because we have to calculate min SSD if take f= .15 we get more value but if we take f= .35 we get min value so we take f=.35.
	Pakkirappa, 2017, V is given in kmph convert to m I.e  1000/60*60=0.278Ssd=vt+{v2/2gf}Where t is time required for understanding the situation this is a constant value.Ssd=0.27*80*2.5+{(0.27*80)^2/(2*9.81*0.35)}Ssd=121.6m.
	Shubham, 2018, Right @Navi.
	Dinesh, 2019, 0.28 * V.t + 0.01V^2/brake efficiency.V = 80 t = 2.5And We get exact ans 120.
	Lucky Kumar, 2020, SSD = 0.278Vt+V^2/254(f+S%).        = 0.278*80*2.5+80*80/254(.35).        = 127.6 mtr.Cofficent of friction (f) = 0.35.

An upgrade g1% is followed by a downgrade g2%. The equation of the parabolic curve of length L to be introduced, is given by
	Pranav Bhatkar, 2018, Give me the explanation in detail.
	Chandru, 2019, Equation of parabolic curve y=(NX2)/(2L).Where N is gradient N=n1-n2.n1 is positive for downward gradient.n2 is negative for upward gradient.So N=g1-(-g2).N=g1+g2.Finally y=(g1+g2)X2/(2L).

	Bsd, 2015, = (6300/1400)*20 = 90 kg/sq cm.

	Maggie, 2016, What is the formula? Please explain the solution.
	Harshad, 2016, Stress = load/area.So, area = load/stress.
	Babar, 2017, At = ( load/stress)*length.      At= (6300/1400)*20.     At = 90 cm2.

	Kit, 2018, According to me, it is 90msq.Cm.
	Tanu, 2020, We know,Area= (force or load)/stress.                       =6300/1400.                       = 4.5 cm2 , per metre width.For 20 m length it will be = 4.5x20 = 90 kg/cm2.
	Sabyasachi, 2020, We know that, As=LfW/2s.As=Area of steel. L=Length.f=Co-efficiant of friction, here f=2.W=Weight. s=Working stress in steel in kg/cm2.As=(20X2X6300) /(2X1400).     = 90 Kg/cm2.

	Alex, 2018, Design of horizontal curves on highways is based on the following points.* design speed of vehicles.* permissible friction on the road surface.* permissible centrifugal ratio.* permissible maximum super-elevation.



If no super elevation is provided on a road along curves, pot holes may develop at
	Kartik, 2017, Why in outer edge?
	Naresh Dharam, 2017, I think it is INNER edge. Because while overturning (lack of super elevation), outer edge of vehicles will rise and load transferred by an inner edge. So pot holes appear on inner edge.
	Kvd, 2017, If super elevation is provided on horizontal curves, then more pressure applied on outer edge only while taking a curve so if not provided super elevation where pr is more their is no any resistance, so pot holes developed on outer edge.
	Ekansh Gupta, 2017, I think that it may be the center because potholes are generated when the traffic load is more and for 2 lanes 2-way traffic maximum traffic density is the center.
	Hardik Dabhi, 2018, Effect of centrifugal force when there is not provided superelevation.P is in outward direction pressure on the outward tyre is more compared to inner tyre so outer tyre pressure bulk > inner tyre so thus if supper elevation is not provided, Damage will form Outside form of Potholes.
	Salman, 2019, Because of no superelevation provided on the curves, the vehicle will have a tendency to go outer direction and in the opposite direction, the centrifuge force will zero.
	Gauraw, 2019, What is the correct answer? Please explain me.
	Shubh, 2020, Centrifugal force will generate more pressure on outer wheels. So potholes will be in outer.
	Dushyant Rajpoot, 2020, Due to centrifugal force on the curve, the vehicle tends to turn towards outside. So excess weight acts outer tyre and generates greater pressure. Thus outer side road surface gets more damage than inner side.

If L is the length of vehicles in metres, C is the clear distance between two consecutive vehicles (stopping sight distance), V is the speed of vehicles in km/hour, the maximum number N of vehicles/hour, is
	Hardik Dabhi, 2018, Center to centre spacing of the vehicle. S = L/2 +C+ L/2.Maximum number N of vehicle/hour indicates traffic volume or capacity is given by;N = 1000V/S, So that N = 1000V/L+C.
	Harsh Shukla, 2019, Density = 1000/[Average space headway (in meters)].Traffic capacity or volume = (Velocity * Traffic density). = (1000*Velocity)/(Average space headway)S= Center to Center spacing between two consecutive vehicles = average space headway.S=L/2+L/2+C=L+C=Average space headway.Traffic density = 1000V/L+C -> Answer.
	Mohammed Asad, 2020, Thanks for the answer @Hardik Dabhi.

	Naveengowda, 2018, Given answer is correct.Surface course   ---> 25 - 50mm.Binder course  ---> 50 - 100mm.Base course  ---> 100 - 300mm.Sub base course  --->  100 - 300mm.Subgrade  ---> 150 - 300mm.
	Dhaval Darji, 2018, Surface-10cm.Base- 200mm to 300mm.Sub-base-75mm to 150mm.Subgrade -depth 600mm to the water level.
	Ravishankar V L, 2019, Sub-base materials comprise natural sand, gravel, laterite, brick metal, crushed stone or combinations thereof meeting the prescribed grading and physical requirements. The sub-base material should have a minimum CBR of 20 % and 30 % for traffic upto 2 msa and traffic exceeding 2 msa respectively. Sub-base usually consist of granular or WBM and the thickness should not be less than 150 mm for design traffic less than 10 msa and 200 mm for design traffic of 1:0 msa and above. Base The recommended designs are for unbounded granular bases which comprise conventional water bound macadam (WBM) or wet mix macadam (WMM) or equivalent confirming to MOST specifications. The materials should be of good quality with minimum thickness of 225 mm for traffic up to 2 msa an 150 mm for traffic exceeding 2 msa. Bituminous surfacing The surfacing consists of a wearing course or a binder course plus wearing course. The most commonly used wearing courses are surface dressing, open graded premix carpet, mix seal surfacing, semi-dense bituminous concrete and bituminous concrete. For binder course, MOST specifies, it is desirable to use bituminous macadam (BM) for traffic upto o 5 msa and dense bituminous macadam (DBM) for traffic more than 5 msa.
	Prajwol Pratap, 2020, 10 cm for Base course and 15 cm for Sub-base Course.
	Bjpegu, 2020, The thickness of the base ranges from 15- 30cm. Not only 10cm.
	Engineer Johny Sins, 2021, Thickness of the  base ranges from 10cm - 30cm.Here the question asked is minimum thickness,So, The answer is 10cm.
	Naveed Kamal Marwat, 2021, Surface course ---> 25 - 50mm.Binder course ---> 50 - 100mm.Base course ---> 100 - 300mm.Sub base course ---> 100 - 300mm.Subgrade ---> 150 - 300mm.

Minimum stopping distance for moving vehicles on road with a design speed of 80 km/hour, is
	Bikash Kabiraj, 2017, 60kmph----> 80m.80kmph-----> 120m.100kmph-----> 150m.
	Binod Nath, 2017, It's 180 m for design speed of 100 kmph.
	Raman, 2018, 20kmph---20m.25kmph---25m.30kmph---30m.40kmph---45m.50kmph---60m.60kmph---80m.65kmph---90m.80kmph---120m.100kmph---180m.
	Harsh Shukla, 2019, Stopping distance = vt+[v^2/(2*f*g)].v= velocity , t= reaction time.v=22.23 m/s , t= 2.5sec (given by IRC), g=9.81 m/s^2.f=braking efficiency * f(actual).Braking efficiency is considered 50% if not given.f(actual) is considered 0.7 if not given approximately.This gives f=(0.5*0.7)=0.35.Stopping distance = (22.23*2.5)+[((22.23)^2)/(2*0.35*9.81)] = 127 m ~ 120m (approx).
	Karthi, 2019, @Harsh Shukla.We can take f= 0.35 to 0.4 directly. Because f -coefficient of longitudinal friction.
	Subhasmita Patra, 2019, How v= 22.23?Please explain it.
	R. Nath, 2019, V= 80km/hr.Convert it to m/s.That is,  80(1000/3600) = 22.22m/sec.f = 0.35.t = 2.5 sec.SsD =  (v.t) +((v^2)/(2.g.f)).       =  put values.       = 127m.
	Vineela, 2020, SSD = LD+BD.Vtr + v^2/2gf.(80x0.278)*2.5+(80x0.278)^2/2x9.81x0.35.=127m ~ 120m.
	Achumi Kits, 2020, Thanks @Raman.
	Sanjay, 2020, What is the overtaking sight distance at a different speed? Can anyone explain me?

From the point of tangency before an intersection, the route markers are fixed at a distance of



A gradient along which the vehicle does not require any tractive effort to maintain a specified speed, is known as
	Loki, 2017, Anyone explain it? please.
	Kanji, 2019, Gradient :It is expressed as the ratio of the difference of heights between the two points and the distance between them.Eg: Now when you are going up on the hill by road, then you need more tractive effort. But when you are coming down, the tractive effort is negative, means your car will speed downwards. Even while going Straight a car need some amount of tractive effort. But there is a definite gradient in which you will need no tractive effort. Remember, it is not negative tractive effort but just the amount that is required to maintain the speed. So that specific gradient in which you can maintain a specific speed will be called floating gradient.
	Sushma, 2019, Thank you @Kanji.

For a comfortable travel on Highways, the centrifugal ratio should not exceed
	Piyush Singh, 2015, For Highways centrifugal ratio (v^2/gr) = 1/4 = 0.25.Maximum superelevation should be 1/4 for comfortable ride.For Railways Centrifugal ratio should be 1/8 = 0.125.
	Chhaya, 2017, Thanks @Piyush Singh.
	Ashu, 2017, Thanks @Piyush.
	Anomi, 2018, Thanks for the answer.
	Sushant Chaturvedi, 2018, Can anyone explain this centrifugal ratio?
	Narasimha, 2018, Centrifugal force P = MV^2/R,We know W = Mg, P/W = V^2/R.Where, P/W is called centrifugal ratio or impact factor.
	Swar, 2019, Thanks @Piyush and @Narasimha.
	Umang, 2019, Thank you all for explaining it.
	Vikram Gupta, 2019, What is the significance of the impact factor/centrifugal ratio?
	Reddy, 2020, But it should not exceed lateral friction (.15), considering lateral skidding at curves.
	Rons Baba, 2020, Thanks @Piyush Singh.
	Anirban Roy, 2020, We know, for superelevation:e+f= v^2/gR. The term on the RHS of the equal sign is called the centrifugal ratio. We know maximum values of:e= 0.07 and f= 0.15.So, (e+f )max= 0.22.
	Anirban Roy, 2020, Max centrifugal ratio:For highways = 1/4.For railways = 1/8.

	Asad Hayat Warraich, 2019, E is the correct answer because we consider all these factors in the design of flexible pavement. Consider the following (according to AASHTO standards):A. wheel loads: Equivalent Single Axle Load (ESAL).B. intensity of traffic: w18 (Sum of ESALs found by multiplying Axle Load Equivalency Factors with Average Daily Traffic).C. climate of the region: Drainage conditions (Drainage factors M2, M3).D. sub-grade conditions: CBR and Resilient Modulus.Hence, E is the correct answer.
	Sdo Ali Ejaz, 2019, E is the correct answer because we consider all these factors in the design of flexible pavement. Consider the following (according to AASHTO standards):A. Wheel loads: Equivalent Single Axle Load (ESAL).B. Intensity of traffic: w18 (Sum of ESALs found by multiplying Axle Load Equivalency Factors with Average Daily Traffic).C. Climate of the region: Drainage conditions (Drainage factors M2, M3).D. Sub-grade conditions: CBR and Resilient Modulus.Hence, E is the correct answer.

Degree of a road curve is defined as the angle in degrees subtended at the centre by an arc of
	Sudarsana, 2016, I think it should be 30m.
	Imran Khan, 2017, 30 is the correct option according to the definition.
	Gajen Jha, 2017, 30 m should be the correct answer.
	Rose, 2017, How?According to me, the correct answer is 30m.
	Ajay Sharma, 2017, The most common type of horizontal curve used to connect intersecting tangent (or straight) sections of highways or railroads are Circular curves. In most countries, two methods of defining circular curves are in use: the first, in general use in railroad work, defines the degree of curve of the central angle subtended by a chord of 100 ft (30.48 m) in length; the second, used in highway work, defines the degree of curve as the central angle subtended by an arc of 100 ft (30.48 m) in length.
	Vivek, 2017, The degree of curve is the central angle subtended by an arc (arc basis) or chord (chord basis) of one station. It will define the sharpness of the curve. In English system, 1 station is equal to 100 ft. In SI, 1 station is equal to 20 m. It is important to note that 100 ft is equal to 30.48 m not 20 m.
	Ankit K, 2018, 30 is correct.

On undivided urban roads with at least two traffic lanes in each direction, the centre line marking may be a single solid line of 150 mm wide or double solid line of 100 mm wide separated by a space of 100 mm.

	Haylee, 2017, I think you did not understand what Retaining wall and its Function.How can you decide 1:3? the Battering  Slope is  decided  by  According to the Standard which  means that; If  the  Battering  slope 1:2, 1:3 or 1:4 . . . . . .. . . 	Anirban Roy, 2020, The design if a retaining wall needs structural analysis definitely. But to proceed with at the beginning we apply thumb rule to get preliminary dimensions with respect to the height of the retaining wall. During such proportioning, we take a batter of 1:3. This can change during actual designing. If batter required is steeper then we keep this value. But if batter required is flatter then we accept the flatter one or sometimes we adjust the top width to make the batter proportionate and look presentable geometrically.

Interior thickness of concrete road slab for design wheel load 6300 kg and permissible flexural stress 21 kg/cm2, is
	Arnab Chanda, 2016, Please give me the explanation.
	Jogendra, 2016, Provide the explanation of the solution.
	Arya, 2016, Stress = load/area,Thickness at interior is 0.85 d.
	Jehangir Khan, 2017, Please provide the explanation.
	Sam, 2017, d = (3W/stress)^0.5,  = (3 * 6300/21)^0.5,  = 30.
	Praveen, 2017, Thickness will be 0.85 * 30 = 25.5.
	Roy, 2017, Thanks @Praveen and @Sam.
	Anbu Star Lion, 2017, Thanks @Praveen and @Sam.
	Qasim, 2018, depth of concrete=d= sqrt(3w/stress),d=sqrt(3*6300/21)=30,Now,Thickness of Concrete= 0.85*d,t=0.85*30,t=25.5cm.
	Kohli, 2018, Thanks @Qasim.
	Ammy, 2019, Thank you @Qasim.
	Akhil, 2019, Thank you all for explaining the answer.
	Prashant, 2020, What is the difference between the depth of concrete and thickness of concrete @Quasim.
	Burhan, 2020, Wheel load 3600.Stress 21√3 * 3600/21=√300=30Now     .85* d= .85 * 30 = 25.5cm.
	Anirban Roy, 2020, Using Goldbeck equation:t = (3W÷stress)^0.5.t interior = 0.85t.
	Hima, 2021, The depth and thickness of concrete pavement is the same.As cross-section of concrete pavement is Double trapezoidal. So thickness or depth at the interior of concrete pavement is equal to 0. 85 thickness or depth at sides of concrete pavement.Depth/thickness of concrete pavement is given by (3W/flexural stress). Where W = wheel load.
	Dev, 2021, depth of concrete = d = √(3w/stress),d= √(3*6300/21)=30,Now,Thickness of Concrete= 0.85*d,t=0.85*30,t=25.5cm.

If the width of a pavement slab is 7.5 m, thickness 20 cm and working stress 1400 kg/cm2, spacing of 10 mm tie bars for the longitudinal joint, is
	Jogendra, 2016, Explanation please.
	Jehangir, 2017, Someone please explain it.
	Disaku, 2017, 2400*7.5*0.2*1=1400*Ast.Ast = 2.57 c.m^2,Area of each bar. ast=(3.14*1^2)/4 =0.78,Diameter taken as 1c.m, Spacing= (ast*100)/Ast = 30.3 nearly 30 C.M.
	Chhaya, 2017, Thanks @Disaku.
	Shahul, 2018, Thanks @Disaku.
	Nasir, 2019, Can anyone explain the answer? please.
	Harsh Shukla, 2019, Density = Weight/Volume.The weight of slab = Density of concrete * Volume.Density of concrete= 2400kg/m^3.Volume = L*B*H = 1*7.5*0.2=1.5 m^3.Weight of slab = 2400*1.5 = 3600kg.This weight of slab should be equal to the force generated due to resisting stresses in the concrete slab.Force generated = stresses * Area of steel (Ast).Force generated = 1400*Ast = Weight of slab.Ast = 3600/1400 =2.57 cm^2.Considering 1cm diameter bars.Area of 1 bar = (3.14/4)*1^2=0.785.Spacing =( Area of 1 bar /Ast )*100 = 30.35cm ~ 30cm.
	Kcube, 2020, Here the coefficient of Friction between pavement and subgrade is not given or assumed to be 1 but usually, it is taken as 1.5, then answer becomes 20 cm.Ast=b*h*W*f / 100*working stress (b is in meter, h is in cm, W is in Kg/m3, working stress is in Kg/cm2).Number of Bars, N= Ast / A(of i bar),Spacing (in cm) = 100/N.
	Saqib, 2021, The answer would be 40mm.Explanation :Consider the slab of width B with tie bar placed at Centre. The left half of the tie bar will resist the frictional force acting on the left half of the slab (B/2) and the same will happen to the right side. Taking left half into the picture:Frictional force = coeff of fric X weight of left half of slab.                        = coeff of fric X (B/2)X h X 1m X Unit weight of CC.= 1.5 x 3.75 x 0.2 x 1 x 2400 = 2700kg.Resisting force = 1400xArea of steel.Therefore Area of steel per 1m length = 2700/1400 = 1.92 cm^2.Spacing = 100 * 3.14 * 1^2/(4x1.92) =   40.88 cm (taking 10mm bar as 1cm in the formula).
	Saqib, 2021, The answer would be 40mm.Explanation :Consider the slab of width B with tie bar placed at Centre. The left half of the tie bar will resist the frictional force acting on the left half of the slab (B/2) and the same will happen to the right side. Taking left half into the picture:Frictional force = coeff of fric X weight of left half of slab.                        = coeff of fric X (B/2)X h X 1m X Unit weight of CC.= 1.5 x 3.75 x 0.2 x 1 x 2400 = 2700kg.Resisting force = 1400xArea of steel.Therefore Area of steel per 1m length = 2700/1400 = 1.92 cm^2.Spacing = 100 * 3.14 * 1^2/(4x1.92) =   40.88 cm (taking 10mm bar as 1cm in the formula).
	Srushti, 2021, As per my knowledge, I think the right answer is 40cm.

In a braking test, a vehicle travelling at 36 km ph was stopped at a braking distance of 8.0 m. The average value of the vehicle's skid resistance (friction coefficient) is
	Nabin Adhikari, 2014, F = V^2/(254S) = 0.64.So correct answer might be A.
	Arun Kumar, 2016, f' = V*2 / 2gL= (36/3.6)**2/ 2 * 9.81 * 8.0 v = V /3.6 = 36/3.6= 10m/sec= 0.64m (say)
	Roy, 2017, The correct Answer is A.
	Gubendhiran, 2017, SSD=lag distance + braking distance.SSD=Vt +[(V^2)&div;(2g(f+-g))],Breaking distance=(v^2)&div;(254f+-g),We don't have gradients so leave it.8=(36^2)&div;(254f),f=0.63779=0.64,So option A is correct.
	Kshitiz, 2018, Friction coefficient value never exceed 0.4Longitudinal friction coefficient range 0.3 to o.4Lateral friction coefficient value generally 0.15You can use the formula for finding friction coefficient but it exceeds limiting value. So Ans is 0.16 correct ie it took lateral friction value.
	Raj, 2018, Yes, absolutely correct @Kshitiz. The Limiting value is 0.4. The correct option A is correct.
	Sourish, 2018, What about none of this? 16 also more than .15.
	Suman, 2018, It seems to be option D is correct as 'f' in the breaking distance formula signify longitudinal friction, which varies from 0.4 to 0.35 depending on the speed, while the lateral friction 'f' comes to design the superelevation instead. So option D be the correct answer.
	Akhila, 2019, Yes, in sight distance its always Longitudinal friction not lateral. So, Option D as it exceeds the range (0.35-0.40).
	Koushal, 2020, Here f= a/g.
	Binny Bana, 2020, A is correct.Breaking distance =v^2/2gf.8=10^2/2*9.81*f,so = 100/156.7.=.64 answer.
	Salim, 2020, f = v2/254L = 36*36/254*8 = 0.64 but the value of friction factor lies in between 0.35 to 0.40. So, the correct Ans will be (D) none of the above.
	Swain Lipan, 2021, Option c is correct. Because it asking in skid resistance coefficient.Hence.S=1/2*at^2,Hence a= g*f. S  is given 8.t = 2.5 sec. As per IRC.g = 9.81,f  = ?.If we put this formula answer comes 0.26  which is nearly equal to option C.
	Shubham Gour, 2021, Options C is correct.We know that;SSD= (V*T)+(V^2/2*g*f).First off ..V= 36*5/18=10 and t=2.5 by IRC.8 = (10*2.5) +(10^2/2*9.81*f)8 = 25+100/19.62*f 8 = 25+30.09*ff = 8/30.09f = 0.26.So, take the value is nearest in options is 0.16.
	Shubham Gour, 2021, Option c is correct.Given:- SSD :- 8 M, V=36kmph then convert.V= 36 * 5/18= 10  t=2.5 sec by IRC.SSD = (V*T)+(V^2/2gf),8 = (10*2.5)+(10^2/2*9.18*f),8 = 25+100/18.36fm8 = 30.44f,f = 8/30.44,f = 0.26.It is the nearest value options C.So, options C is correct.

Maximum number of vehicles that can pass a given point on a lane during one hour without creating unreasonable delay, is known as
	Dutesh Kumar, 2015, Practical capacity of lane.
	Bikash Kabiraj, 2017, The Maximum number of vehicle----practical capacity. The Maximum number of passenger cars----basic capacity.
	Naren, 2017, Answer is possible capacity.For practical capacity it's "with unreasonable delay".
	Naveen Kallan, 2017, Yes, it is Possible capacity.
	Sankha, 2018, Basic capacity is the maximum number of vehicles (PCU) that can pass a given point on a lane or roadway during one hour under the most nearly ideal roadway and traffic conditions which can possibly be attained. Two roads have same physical features will have same basic capacities irrespective of the traffic conditions.Possible Capacity is the maximum number of vehicles which can pass a given point on a lane or highway during one hour under the prevailing roadway and traffic conditions.This means that the possible capacity of a highway will always be lower than the basic capacity unless the prevailing conditions of the traffic, approach the ideal conditions. Therefore the possible capacity may vary from 0 to the maximum, i.e., Basic capacity.Practical Capacity is the maximum number of the vehicle that can pass a given point on a lane or roadway during one hour, without traffic density being so great as to cause unreasonable delay, hazard or restriction to the driver's freedom to manoeuvre under the prevailing roadway and traffic conditions.
	Roy, 2018, @All.(Ideal)=basic capacity, (prevailing)=possible capacity & (unreasonable delay)=practical capacity.
	Ravishankar, 2019, Good, Thanks @Roy.
	Pawan, 2019, Thanks @Bikash Kabiraj.
	@Pcivilian, 2021, Here it is saying "without creating un delay".The answer may not be correct!
	Akp, 2021, Answer B is right 100%.

Over taking time required for a vehicle with design speed 50 km ph and overtaking acceleration 1.25 m/sec2 to overtake a vehicle moving at a speed 30 km ph, is
	Manish, 2015, Square root of (4*s/a).S = 0.2*Speed of overtaken vehicle + 6 (Base length of vehicle).A = Acceleration.
	Pavan, 2016, First change the  overtaken vehicle speed into  m/sec by multiply with .278 then applyS = .7*- speed of overtaken vehicle + wheel base ( assume 6 meter)Where a = accelerationAnd T = Square root of (4* s/a)
	Leo Basil George, 2017, Overtaking time T = [2(s1+s2)/a]^1/2.Where s1 , s2 = .7v+l.v - velocity of overtaken vehicle.s1 -distance b/w overtaking and over taken vehicle.s2- distance covered  after overtaking.l = wheel Base of vehicle ~ 6.1m.a -overtaking acceleration =1.25 m/s2.v = 30 * 5/18=8.33m/ss1 = s2= .7 * 8.33+6.1 = 11.93m.T = [2x(11.93+11.93)/1.25]^1/2.  =  6.17 seconds.
	Anbarasan, 2017, FORMULAS:OSD = 0.278Vbt + 0.278Vb T + 2(.2Vb +6) + VT WHERE T = (4S/a)^0.5 and S = .2vb+6  a= acceleration= 1.25  Vb=30kmph V=50 KMPHSOLUTION :  S= .2 * 30 + 6 =12   T= (4*12/1.25)^.5= 6.197(ANSWER)  OSD= .278*30*2.5 + .278 *30*6.197+2(12)+50*6.197.
	Gubendhiran, 2017, T=√[(4S)&div;a].S=6+(0.7Vb).Vb=Design speed - 16 =50 - 16 =34 kmph,S=6 + (.7*0.278*34) =12.61 m/s,T=√[(4*12.61)&div;1.25] = 6.35 seconds.So option B was correct.
	Umesh, 2019, Thanks @Gubendhiran.
	Renis Gajipara, 2020, V= 50 km/ph = 13.88 m/s ....vehicle with design speedVb= 50 km/ph = 13.88 m/s....vehicle moving at a speeda = 1.25 m/s^2.Now,  S =0.7*VB +6 = 11.831 sec. T = √(4S÷ a) = 6.15 sec.
	Jahangir Abbas Sahil, 2021, V= 50 km/ph = 13.88 m/s -> vehicle with design speed.Vb= 30 km/ph = 13.88 m/s -> vehicle moving at a speed.a = 1.25 m/s^2.Now,S = 0.7*VB +6 = 11.831 sec.T = √(4S÷ a) = 6.14 sec.

If brakes of vehicles are effective, the vehicle-running at 30 km/hour comes to a stop in
	Arpyit Gupta, 2016, What will be the value of Coeff of long friction here? Will it not be 0.35? By taking this value and by applying.S= (V2) / (2gf).I am getting 10. Please tell me how (B) is the correct option.
	Arsh, 2016, @Arpyit. For speed 30km/hr, the Coeff = 0.4.
	Dhruba, 2017, What is the formula to calculate the answer?
	Chhaya, 2017, Here, s = V2/2gf.Where V in m/s and F = 0.35,Answer is 10.11.
	Naren, 2017, For 30kmph the value of 'f'  is 0.4. The answer is 8.9 and round of to the higher value. The answer is option A.
	Murali, 2017, Option A is correct.  s=((v*v)/(2gf)).S = 10.11.
	Starlin, 2017, V^2/(254f) =30* 30* / (254 *.35)= 10.12  next to 10 is 12 or may be 10.
	Kshitiz, 2018, All persons are wrong here. Ans is 12m.rxn distn=3*3=9.braking distance=3*0.4=3.6.So, 9+3.6=12.6 closer to 12. So 12 m is the answer.
	Naina, 2018, It's V coefficient.
	Harsh Shukla, 2019, For velocity > 60kmph, f = 0.35.For velocity <60kmph, f= 0.36.While making a vehicle stop all the kinetic energy will be converted into frictional energy i.e.0.5*m*v^2=f*m*g*L.So, L = v^2/2*g*f.L = 10.12m ~10m (Answer).
	Thakur, 2019, Value of friction coefficient will be 30. So v2/2 * 30 * 9.81.= 8.33^2/2 * 30 * 9.81.
	Thakur791, 2019, 8.33^2/2 * .30 * 9.81,= 11.78~~12.
	Nisar, 2019, Stopping dist.= (0.2 *vb+6).= (0.2*30+6) = 12.
	Pavan, 2019, Braking distance is vt. So it is 0.4 for brakes are effective in case. Now 0.4*30. And we will get 12.
	Mhathung Kikon, 2020, For speed (kmph) and longitudinal coefficient of friction (f).20-30   -> 0.4.40.  -> 0.38.50 ->0.37.60  -> 0.36.65  -> 0.36.80   -> 0.35.100  -> 0.35.
	Umesh, 2020, s = i+0.7xVb,Where i = 6m( vehicle base length).Vb = 8.33 in m/s.s = 11.83 ----> 12m.
	Krushna Akotkar, 2021, F = u^2/29*LL = 8.33*2/19*0.2 = 11.96~12m.Assume f = .20.

The distance travelled by revolving the wheel of a vehicle more than its circumferential movement, is known as
	Jagtap_705, 2016, If distance travelled is > circumferential movement (skid _ down the hill).If distance travelled is < circumferential movement (slip_ up the hill).
	Buddhi Ram Bhatt, 2017, Skidding is the lateral motion of the contact area of the tyre of a moving vehicle over the surface of road. But slip is the the distance travelled by revolving wheel of a vehicle more than its circumferential movement.



If the difference in elevation of an edge of the pavement 9 m wide and its crown is 15 cm, the camber of the pavement, is
	Abhishek, 2016, How it comes? Please explain.
	Ashu, 2016, Please explain how it can be solved.
	Dabhi Deepak, 2016, It's so easy. Simple calculation.Total pavement= 9m half = 9 * 0.5 = 4.5.Horizontal Distance ------------- Vertical  Distance. 4.5m.  --------------> 0.15m.1 m.   -------------->?0.15 / 4.5. = 1/30. Means 1 in 30.
	Pritesh Dabhade, 2017, Total width of carrige way = 9m.So camber always consider distance from crown to edge which is 4.5m here.Hence 4.5 &div; 0.15 = 30,So camber is 1 in 30.
	Mayur, 2018, The equation of straight camber:- y= W/2N.        W - width of the road,N - slope, y- ht of camber(crown),0.15=9/2N,N=9/2*0.15,N= 30. So camber is 1 in 30.
	Osman, 2018, Camber is given on half-width of the road. So, 9m/2=4.5m. Or 450cm.Now given that,  For 450 cm long there is 15 cm height.  1 cm --- 15/450 = 1/30  or  1 in 30 =>answer.
	Prabhudas Mahapatra, 2018, Ymax = W/2N.So, N = W/2 Ymax.N= 900/2*15 = 30.Gradient, G = 1 in N,G = 1 in 30.
	Roy, 2018, Thanks @Dabhi Deepak.
	Utsav, 2019, A simple way is 450/15 = 30.Hence 1 in 30.
	Sahil, 2019, Camber is ratio of height of crown to horizontal distance from centre to edge. So, first converting 15cm to m = 0.15,Camber = 0.15/4.5 = 1/30.
	Francis, 2021, camber in % will be (0.15/4.5) * 100 =  3.3 %.
	Boss, 2021, Slope = Rise/Fall.Slope = 0.15/4.5,Slope = 0.33,Hence, Slope = 1/30.

Minimum radius of curvature of National Highways or State highways in hill region free from snow, is kept
	Harsh Shukla, 2019, Minimum Radius or Rulling minimum Radius both are same.Rulling minimum radius = v^2/[g(f(max) + e(max)]f(max) = 0.15 (lateral), e(max) for hilly areas not bounded by snow = 10% = 0.1.Here v = rulling minimum velocity for hilly region which is equal to 40kmph = 11.12m/s.

	S.Shahin, 2017, B is correct. Because here even means v=L/3{ A.+An+4(A1+A3+.....+An-1)+2(A2+A4+......+An-2)}Here, A = Coross sectional Area.
	Somya, 2017, B is correct according to Simpson's 1/3rd formula.
	Ps111, 2017, C is correct.D/3 *[(1ST AREA+LAST AREA) + 4*(ODD)+ 2*(EVEN)].
	Suryashekhar Vicky, 2018, option C  is right.Ref: B.N Dutt book.
	Pratik Soni, 2018, According to B. N. Dutta "C" is correct Answer.
	Rangareddy, 2018, As per my knowledge, option 3 is correct.
	Amz, 2018, None of the above. The question lacks explanation of D, presumably equal spacing of areas. Prismoidal formula treats areas as vector ordinates and averages the areas.In the same way the area of a prism=median area (Top+Bottom)/2*height. V = D/2*[V1 + Vn + 2* (V2+V3+..+Vn-2)].
	Rishabh Machiwal, 2018, Both options B and C are right.If we take A1, A2, A3.......,An-1, An then option B is right.If we take A0, A1, A2, A3.....An-1, An then option C is right.
	Anju, 2018, B is the right option. I agree.
	Lalan, 2018, Yes, @Rishabh.In both case C is correct.
	Ashish, 2018, The answer is C.
	Ruata, 2018, Option C is the correct answer.
	Ashtaveer, 2018, Prismodal formula = h/6( A1+A2 +4Am) where Am=(A1+A2)/2.
	Aman, 2018, L/3 (1st area + last area + 4 (sum of even area) + 2 (sum of odd area). It's Simson rule.
	Indhuca, 2019, => [first area + last area + 4 Σ Even area + 2 Σ odd areas is the Right answer and it is Also Known as Simpson Rule.
	Dayananda Naik T L, 2020, I think C is the correct answer.
	Rekha, 2020, @Indhuca.Computation of area - Simpson's rule.Computation of volume- Prismoidal formula.Both equations are same.
	Alfaizal, 2021, B is the right answer I agree.

In long and short wall method of estimation, the length of long wall is the centre to centre distance between the walls and
	Mahendra Kumawat, 2016, Both A & B is the correct answer.

	Bhoor Singh, 2018, Thanks all.
	Bittu, 2019, The length of long wall, (out to out) may be calculated after adding half breadth at each end to its centre line length.Thus the length of short wall measured into in and may be found by deducting half breadth from its centre line length at each end.
	Priya Sh, 2020, I think question itself wrong. in long wall=out to out length+(breadth of item-thickness of wall ).In short wall = in to in length -(breadth of item -thickness of wall).
	Chirag Raghu, 2020, Here in question, it should be centre line length not centre to centre.So if it's centre line length then, to get the length of a long walk (out to out) may be calculated by adding half breadth at each end to it's centre line length.Similarly, for the short-wall, it will be - deducting half breadth from its centre line.

For 12 mm thick cement plastering 1 : 6 on 100 sq.m new brick work, the quantity of cement required, is
	P Takhe Lomdak, 2013, How it is calculated out please tell me the procedure?
	Mukund, 2013, Total volume = 100*.012 = 1.2cum.30% more mortar required so 1.2+ (.3*1.20) = 1.56cum.Now also to get dry volume of mortar, wet wet volume may b further increase by 25%.So volume of dry mortar = 1.6+ (1.56*.25) = 1.95cum.Now proportion = 1:6.So volume of cement = (1/7) * 1.95 = 0.2785 (ans).
	Chandu, 2014, Given volume = 100*.012 = 1.2cum.Increase mortar by 30% = 1.56cum.For volume of dry mortar increase 25%= 1.56+ (1.56*.25) = 1.95cum.Now proportion = 1:6.k+6k=7k.So volume of cement = (1/7) * 1.95 = 0.2785.This is the correct answer.
	Ajay Saini, 2014, You can take 30% for uniform wall and 25% for dry volume i.e. total 55% Now 10x10x0.012 = 1.2 hence (1.2/1+6)+(1.2/1+6)x55/100 = 0.2785.
	Pritam, 2014, How could you take, extra 30% morter?
	Piyush Singh, 2014, Volume of plaster = area * thickness = 0.012*100 = 1.2 cum.Increase this by 30 % therefore = 1.2*1.3 = 1.56 cum.For volume of dry mortar = 1.25*1.56 = 1.95 cum.Now 1 part is of cement and 6 part is of sand so,Cement = (1/7) *1.95= 0.2785 volume of cement.Volume of one bag of cement = 0.035 cum.So no of bags required = 8 bags.
	Nivas, 2014, Is is possible go around 55% volume extra for dry & wet mortar?
	Abhijit Mandal, 2016, Volume of plaster = 0.012*100 = 1.2 m^3.Add 20% depression & wastages volume of plaster = 1.2+1.2*20% = 1.2+0.24 = 1.44.Add 1/3 dry volume = 1.44+1.44*1/3 = 1.44+0.48 = 1.92 m^3.Cement = 1.92*1/7 = 0.274 m^3.
	Adebola, 2016, I think answers may vary because one may decide to allow 10-20% for wastage& 30%-35% for shrinkage.
	Brijesh Vishwakarma, 2016, Volume of plaster = 0.012 * 100 = 1.2 m^3.Add 60% for new wall depression & wastages volume of plaster = 1.2 + 1.2 * 60% = 1.92 cum.Cement = 1.92 * 1/7 = 0.274 m^3.
	Puja Mandal, 2016, According to me, it is .012 * 100 = 1.2, dry wt = 1.54 * 1.2 = 1.848, 1.848 &div; 7 = .264.
	Aaina, 2017, If it's 15mm thick then what is the answer?
	Arjun, 2017, As per my solution,  it's 33 percent extra why you are taking 30 percent?
	Sufazo, 2017, According to this answer, they taking 60% extra => 1.2 * 60/100 = 0.274.
	Shahanshah Khan, 2017, I think dry value taken so large in solution maximum take extra 20% to 30%.
	Mohit, 2017, If it is 15 mm thick than answer is .345 cum.
	Salma, 2018, 1*100*. 012*1. 54/7 =. 274.
	Adhiyaman, 2018, Thanks to all.
	Mohit Motwani, 2018, Volume of mortar required= 100*.12 = 1.2. Total 60% of volume increases for dry mortar1.2+ (.60* 1.2)= 1.92.Now volume of cement = 1/7 * 1.92= 0.2743.
	Muthu Nayak, 2018, dry vol=area*thckness*1.3*1.25.dry vol=100*0.012*1.3*1.25.           =1.95cum,Cement=1.95/1+6,Cement=0.278cum,
	Sachin Das, 2018, For 1 m3,Dry volume = 1m3,Wet volume is 35% of dry volume,So we have to take.1: 6 = 1.35 ( 1.35 m3 become 1m3 after adding water in mortor....and for concrete we take 55%....1.54m3 = 1m3).1+ 6 = 7........1.35/7 = 0.193 cement required per m3,Volume = 12/1000 * 100 = 1.2 m3,Required cement volume= 1.2 * 0.193 =0.23.
	Naveen Srivastava, 2018, 0.278 is the incorrect answer.There is no need to add an extra 25%. It's Converted to wet volume to dry volume by 1.30.The Correct ans is 100 * 0.012 * 1.30 = 1.56 * (1/7) = 0.222cum.
	Deepak, 2018, Why to increase by its 30%?
	Matiur Rahaman, 2018, Given thickness of plaster = 12mm = 12/1000 m = 0.012m.Ratio (1:6).Since you have not given assume that 100 sq M of plaster is Done.Thus volume of plaster = 100 x 0.012 =1.2 cum.Add 60 % for unevenness of plaster surface and voids.Total volume of plaster work = 1.2 + 60/100 x 1.2 =1.92 cum.Volume of cement = 1.92/(1+6) =0.274 cum.
	Ramvir Singh, 2018, @All We will take 60% extra of total Volume for water and joint filling between the bricks. So, 1 for 15mm thick plasters the answer is 0.3428 m3 and For 12 mm thick plaster it is 0.2742 m3.
	Shree, 2019, Wet mix mortar for uniform layer =12/1000*100 = 1.2 cum.Adding 30% to fill up joints, uneven surface,Then, Quantity of Mortar =1.2 + 1.2*30% =1.56 cum.Increasing 25% the dry volume = 1.56+1.56*25%= 1.95 cum.Total parts (C:S) = 1+6 =7.Then, Cement = 1.95/7 = 0.278 Cum = 0.278/0.0347 = 8 Bags Cement required.
	Bharat, 2019, Thanks all.
	Govind, 2019, Thanks everyone for explaining the solution.
	Jigme, 2019, Thank you all for explaining the answer, clearly.
	Dinesh Sunar, 2019, Area to be plaster = 100m^2.Thickness of plaster = 12mm = 0.012m.The volume of plaster = 100x0.012 = 1.2m^3.Adding 20%of wet volume,      = 1.2+1.2x20% = 1.44m^3.Increase the wet material by 1/3rd volume in order to get dry volume. The volume of dry material = 1.44+1.44x1/3.                 , ,,                       = 1.92 m^3.Now, volume of cement = 1/7x1.92 = 0.274.

The reduced levels of points, 30 metres apart along the longitudinal section of a road portion between chainages 5 and 9 are shown in the given figure. If there is a uniform up-gradient of the road 120 in 1, the chainage of the point with no filling or cutting is 
	Saikumar, 2015, How it is calculated?
	Prabhakar, 2015, How it is taken?
	Gance, 2015, 1 in 120.So for 30 m there is rise of 0.25 m.R.L of formation level at 6 chain = 100+0.25 = 100.25.Now apply the similar triangles as 1/x = 1.5/(30-X).Where, 1 = 100.25-99.25.x = 12.So the answer B.
	Ashik, 2016, Can you explain it? Please.
	Kamruz, 2017, I think it is A.
	Asmaul Husna, 2017, @Gance. Where is 1.5 from?
	Karthick, 2017, 6+ 12 chain how?
	Vishnu, 2017, Can anyone explain clearly?
	Chandra, 2017, How this will occurs?
	Maya, 2017, @6 difference b/w level is (100.25-99.25)=1.@7 RL=100.25+0.25=100.5.So difference= 102-100.5=1.5,let x be distance at no filling or cutting,distance b/w 7 and 6 is 30,By similar triangle,1 -> x,1.5 ->30-x.
	Liam Payne, 2018, The number of neutrons will be 6 so the total number becomes 6 + 6 is equal to 12.
	Mainak, 2018, 99.25+(2.75/30)*x = 100+(x+30)/120.By solving it x=12m from chainage 6 towards ch. 7, so answer will be (6+12).
	Abhishek, 2018, It is (6+8) chains.
	Debika, 2020, Please, explain this.
	Koshy, 2020, Actually, none of the answers is correct.From calculations, we get that the formation line intersects the ground surface at a distance of 12m from chainage point 6. (let us call this point 'X').Also, when chainage is represented as (A+B) chains, it refers to "A chains" and "B links". Since the distance between consecutive chainage points is 30m, we can say that a 30m metric chain is used for chaining and a 30m chain has 150 links, each 20cm (or 0.2m) long.So, number of links between the points '6' and 'X' = (distance between points '6' and 'X') / (link length).= (12)/(0.2) = 60.Hence, the correct way of representing the point 'X' is (6+60) chains.
	Sanjay, 2021, I think the correct answer is 6+14 chains.
	Misba W, 2021, Gradient given is 1:120So for 30m rise of = (1/120*30),= 0.25 m.RL of formation level @6 chain = 100.0 + 0.25. = 100.25 m.RL of formation level @7 chain = 100.25+ 0.25=100.5 m.So difference = 102-100.5 = 1.5.By similar triangles -(1/x) = 1.5/(30-x)X = 12.So the answer is (6+12) chains.

If tensile stress of a steel rod of diameter D is 1400 kg/cm2 and bond stress is 6 kg/cm2, the required bond length of the rod is
	Deepanshu, 2014, Bond length or development length = d*tensilestress/4*bond stress.D*1400/4*6 = 58.33.
	Pawan, 2014, Ld = fi sigma st / 4 tou bd.
	Salem Basha, 2016, Ld = 0.87Fy/4 τ bd.This will gives answer as 50.75.So answer will be 50 times the diameter.
	Mohan, 2017, Ld = 1400/(4 * 6).  = 58.3 =>59.
	Pawan, 2017, Bond length = tensile stress * Diameter of rod/(4 times bond strength).= 1400 * D/(4 * 6),= 1400 * D/24,= 59D.
	Pradeep Patel, 2017, Acc. to wsm Ld = (1400*D)/(4*6) = 58.33.Acc. To LSM Ld=(.87 *1400 *D)/(4*6) = 50.75.
	Kanhaiya Kr., 2017, (D*1400)&div;(4*6)=58.33,Say=59D.
	Kundan Kumar, 2018, Ld = .87fy * D/4 * τbd.
	Anand, 2018, It is [fy*tensile strength]/4*bond stress.
	Alexrampee, 2018, Fy is not equal to σ.
	Jinish Patel, 2019, The increase of length due to bending stress is called bond length. Bond length = (diameter/4)X stress/strength. = 1400x D/ 4x6. = 59 D.
	Ranu Kumar, 2020, Answer will be 50D.
	Sabina, 2020, Tensile stress = 1400.Bond stress = 6.Bond length = 1400*D/4*6 = 58.33.Hence, the answer is 59D.

For 100 sq. m cement concrete (1 : 2: 4) 4 cm thick floor, the quantity of cement required, is
	Sonu, 2013, Hi.
Please tell me, How to calculate the Qty of cement 0.940 m^3 ?
	Ratul Boruah, 2013, How to calculate the Qty of cement?
	Eng.Raj, 2013, Generally to calculate the Cement, fine aggregate and coarse aggregate the following method will be followed,
Wet Qty. of concrete = 100 Sqm * 0.04m thickness = 4.00 m^3
To get the dry volume of concrete, generally wet volume of concrete will be multiplied by 150%.
Therefore 4.00 m^3 * 150% = 6.00 m^3.
Cement Qty. = Dry volume of concrete / ( Sum of the ratio values).
= 6.00 /( 1+2+4).
= 0.857 m^3 of Cement i.e.25 No. of bags.
But here they considered 164.5% instead of 150%.
Dry volume of concrete = 4 * 164.5% = 6.58 m^3.
Cement Qty. = 6.58/ (1+2+4) = 0.94 m^3 of Cement i.e. 27.5 bags.
	Asadullah Mehsud, 2021, 0.88cum.Valume of dry cement concreat 100 * .04= 4cum,Valume of wet concreat =4 * 1.54 = 6.16,Valume of cement = 1/7 * 6.16 = 0.88cum.
	Khld, 2021, 164.5 is assumed, how?
	Khld, 2021, . 88 m^3 is the right answer.
	Ajay Sharma, 2021, (1/7) * 1.54 * 4 = 0.88m^3 = 0.9m^3.
	Nasir Waheed, 2021, 0.88 m^3 is the right answer.
	Noora S B, 2021, 1.54 is the conversion factor to convert wet to dry mix and additional they take 10% for unevenness & loss. So, total 1.64.
	Mjm Gcek, 2021, The answer is 0.88m^3.
	V C Sawathkar, 2021, For 4cm thick flooring constant of rate analysis is 6.6.6.6/(1+2+4) = 0.94285,for 2.5cm thick flooring 4.125.
	Mjm Gcek, 2021, The correct answer is 0.88m3.
	Muhammad Tariq, 2021, The correct answer is 94.Volume of wet concrete in 4cum.Volume of dry concrete is=4*1.54( constant),Total v= 6.61.Quantity of cement = 1/7 * 6.61 = 0.94.
	Deepankar, 2021, The given answer is correct.1st total volume=100 * 0.04 =4 m^3.The volume is increased by 10% due to uneven surface which gives = 4 * 1.1 = 4.4 m^3.This volume is further increased by 50% for dry volume calculation = 4.4 * 1.5 = 6.6 m^3.So total volume = 6.6 m^3.For cement = (6.6 * 1)/(1+2+4) = 0.9428 m^3.
	Sahla, 2021, 100 * .04=4cu.m of wet concrete.To produced 1cu.m of concrete 1.54cu.m of dry concrete required.1.54 * 4 = 6.16cu.m6.16/1+2+4 = .88 cu.m of cement reqd..88 * 1440 (density of cement)=1267.2 kg.Or,1267.2/50kg = 25.34=26 bag of cement.

	Ranjitha, 2016, Top width is B.Bottom width is b + sd + sd = b + 2sd.Depth of embankment is d.Area of trapezoidal = (d/2) * (b + b + 2sd) .= (d/2) * (2b + 2sd).= (d/2) * 2 * (b + sd).= bd + sd * d.= bd + s(d^2).

The slope of the oultet of 'P trap' below the horizontal is kept
	Prathu, 2018, Can anyone explain this?
	Priyanka Shah, 2019, Please explain the answer in detail.
	Srk, 2019, Explain the answer in detail.
	Riri, 2021, In most homes, there is a P-trap installed under the sinks in the bathroom and kitchens. A P-trap is a plumbing fixture that has several purposes. It traps debris that has drained from the sink and prevents it from forming a clog deep within the plumbing system and to stops sewer gases from passing in to the home. The slope of the outlet of "P trap" below the horizontal is kept at 14°.

The ground surface slopes 1 in 50 along a proposed railway embankment 150 m in length. The height of the embankment at zero chainage is 0.5 m, the width is 11 m and side slopes 2:1. If the falling gradient of the embankment is 1 in 150, the quantity of the earthwork calculated by prismoidal formula, is
	Eng.Raj, 2013, Prismoidal formula = L/6 *( A1+A2+4AM).Where,A1= Cross sectional area at 1 end = Bd1+Sd1*d1 - Sqm.A2= Cross sectional area at another end = Bd2+Sd2*d2 - Sqm.AM= Cross sectional area at middle = Bdm+Sdm*dm - Sqm.dm= (d1+d2)/2 - m.Where d1= depth at starting - m.d2= depth at ending - m.L= length of the section - m.
	Anirban, 2017, Can anyone solve clearly?
	Rishabh Machiwal, 2018, The correct Answer is option A.
	Raj, 2019, Please explain clearly.
	Irfan, 2019, Not getting it, please solve it clearly.
	Biswajit Mohanty, 2020, I solved this problem using prismoidal formula answer will be 3250 cum i.e. option A.
	Santosh, 2020, Please explain the solution clearly.
	Paramjit Paul, 2020, Can anyone solve it in detail?
	Sumit Kumar, 2021, 3250 is the right answer.
	Tarun, 2021, B is right ans. The first trapezoidal area will be 5.75 with height .5m and second one's area will be 15.3125 with height 1.25m at 37.5m away.A3 will be 26. Height 2mA4 will be 28 Height 3m A5 will be 60 Height 4mSlope 2/75.Area is calculated as trapezoid base width will be;2 Height+ width.

Berms are provided in canals if these are
	Roy, 2018, A is also the correct answer.

The cross-sectional area of the embankment of a canal fully in embankment in the given figure is 
	Tallum, 2014, A1 = (2b1+2sh)*h/2 = (b1+sh)h.Similarly, A2 = (b2+sh)h.A = A1+A2 = (b1+b2)h+2sh^2.
	Ranjitha, 2016, I agree with you, @Tallum.
	Jyoti, 2017, Here (b1+b2) + 2 sh^2.
	Roy, 2017, Explain the option C.
	Saranraj, 2017, Very useful.
	Sandeep Singh, 2018, (b1+b2)h+2sh^2.
	A.Kumaresan, 2018, Here, V = [a1+a2]/2 x h x 2.
	Jemish, 2018, The Right answer is (b1+b2) + 2sh^2.
	M. Chowdhuru, 2018, A=(b1+b2+3H)H.
	Paul, 2018, What will be the right answer? Please explain it.
	Sree, 2018, The given trapezoid can be divided into one rectangle and two triangles on both sides (to calculate the area).Tthen for the first trapezoid, rectangular area = b1 * h.                                           area of both triangles together = s * h(^2).                                           total area =A1 =  (b1*h) + (s*h(^2)).Like that for second trapezoid, area = A2 = (b2*h)+(s*h^2).trapezoid area = A1+A2 = (b1*h) + (s*h(^2)) + (b2*h)+(s*h^2).                                       = (b1+b2)h + 2sh^2.
	Smit Dodiya, 2020, A1 = (2b1+2sh)*h/2 = (b1+sh)h.Therefore, A2 = (b2+sh)h.A = A1+A2 = (b1+b2)h+2sh^2.

If to, tp and tm are the optimistic, pessimistic and most likely time estimates of an activity respectively, the expected time t of the activity will be
	Rajesh, 2016, Its a formula for expected time.
	Burhan Ali, 2017, Anyone explain it.
	Mohamed Sajid, 2018, Can anyone explain this?
	Sameer Sopori, 2018, It's simply to estimate the AVERAGE TIME, {(t1+t2+t3+t4+t5+t6)/6}.But here we are using to, tp and tm 'to' Means, expected that the project will be finished within this time.'tp' Means, there are more chances that the project will be finished within this time.'tm' Means, there are chances it will take more time, due to accidents or delays or any other reason.For example:  tomorrow I have to pour the concrete in foundation work.'to' if the weather will remain good and workers will work continuously we can finish it early in 6 hours (20% chances). 'tp' As per the general weather conditions and workers average work output, we will finish it in 8 hours (60% chances).'tm' in case of rain or snow, or any accident on site, it could take more time, and maybe it will be finished in 11 hours. (20%  chances).So the average expected time is = {to+4ta+tp/6 }  = {6+(4*8)+11}/6  =  8.16 hours.
	Yiheyis H, 2018, Thanks a lot @Sameer.
	Dharam Singh, 2018, Thanks all.
	Gajendra, 2021, Thanks @Sameer.

The time by which activity completion time can be delayed without affecting the start of succeeding activities, is known as
	Yehia, 2016, Without affecting the EARLY start of succeeding activities.
	Habib Mohamned, 2017, Without delaying the start of a following activity.
	Adarsha, 2017, Total Float.Total float is the amount of time that an activity can be delayed without delaying the project's estimated completion time.Total float assumes that.All preceding activities are finished as early as possible.All succeeding activities are started as late as possible.Total float for an activity can be determined either:Total float activity n = Late start activity n " Early start activity n.Total float activity n = Late finish activity n " Early finish activity n.Where n denotes the nth activity.Both the equation will yield the same answer.Free float.Free float is the duration of time, that an activity can be delayed without delaying the project\'s estimated completion time and without delaying the start of any succeeding activity.Free float is the property of an activity, not a network path. The Free float can be determined by:Free float n = Minimum early start of all successor activities " Early finish n.Where n denotes the nth activity.Interfering float.The Interfering float is the time available to delay an activity without delaying the project\'s estimated completion time, but delaying an activity into interfering float will delay the start of one or more following non-critical activities.Interfering float n = late finish n " Smallest early start of the succeeding activity.The aggregate of free float and interfering float is equal to the total float.Total float n = Free float n + interfering float n.
	Rahul Mehar, 2017, Its answer should be an independent float.
	Pawan, 2019, Thanks @Adarsha.
	Amu Bokato, 2019, Yes, you are right @Rahul.

The most popular type of organisation used for Civil Engineering Constructions, is
	Ranjeet, 2018, Option A is correct.
	Pramil, 2018, Please explain all other options.
	Rohan, 2019, It's Option B : Line & Staff Organisation.1. Line Organisations: Concentrated Hierarchy with orders being followed without question from top to bottom and no questions are asked regarding mannerism or way of the process. Ex. Military organisations2. Staff Organisations:Mild hierarchy with nominal power in upper level. Mostly all collegues are equal and the nature of work involves participation from all level. Very less number of times orders are given and most of times its discussion or debate based process.Ex. Scientific Organisation (ISRO, NASA).3. Line & Staff Organisation :A mix of above two, where hierarchy is present orders are also given and most of the times they are followed but if situation demands lower management can present their views and orders can be replaced. Ex. All gov Institutions like PWD, Railways, Municipal Corporations, Private Companies.
	Ginie, 2019, Thanks @Rohan.

If the total float and duration of an activity are 5 and 10 days respectively, the particular activity can be
	Tinku, 2016, Please explain this clearly.
	Dvd, 2017, Total float = LFT-EFT=LST-EST.
	Abhishek Roy, 2017, Total float = LFT - EST - DURATION.
	Priyakarthik, 2018, Not understand the solution. Please explain in detail.
	Loai, 2018, Let's say that we have two activities A, and B. However, activity A has 10 days duration that means " without considering the float "activity A has 10 days to complete and must start on time to finish within its duration 10 days.Now, with Total float, activity A has 5 days before starting this means "activity A still has extra time to start late. Because this will not affect the completion date since we talk about " Total float " Not Free Float " so option A is correct. Accordingly, starting 5 days late this will result in complete after 15 days " 10+5 days" this will lead to option C which is correct as well. Finally, activity A has 10 days +5 days this means, in case A started after 10 days. You still have 5 days to complete on time. Which lead to Option B.
	Amit, 2018, Let's say that we have two activities A, and B.However, activity A has 10 days duration that means " without considering the float "activity A has 10 days to complete and must start on time to finish within its duration 10 days.Now, with Total float, activity A has 5 days before starting this means "activity A still has extra time to start late. Because this will not affect the completion date since we talk about " Total float " Not Free Float " so option A is correct. Accordingly, starting 5 days late this will result in complete after 15 days " 10+5 days" this will lead to option C which is correct as well. Finally, activity A has 10 days +5 days this means, in case A started after 10 days. You still have 5 days to complete on time. Which lead to Option B.
	Abhishek, 2019, Total float is the amount of time of an activity which doesn't affect the estimated completion of the period of activity.Here 10 days are available for completion of an activity.The total float is 5 days. Means in 10 days 5 days can be a delay which doesn't affect the completion time of activity.Here answer will be started 5 days later.
	Abhi, 2019, @Amit @Loai.Total float means the time that can be a delay which doesn't affect the estimated completion duration.

If TL is the latest allowable event occurrence time, total activity slack(s), is equal to
	Har, 2017, LST=LATEST FINISH TIME.EST=EARLIEST FINISH TIME.LFT=LATEST FINISH TIME.EFT=EARLIEST FINISH TIME.
	Adarsha, 2017, Latest allowable event occurance time=Latest Finish Time.
	Fawaz, 2018, LST/LAST START TIME.EST/EARLIEST START TIME.LFT/LAST FINISH TIME.EFT/EARLIEST FINISH TIME.
	Romi, 2019, Late start - early start.Late finish - early finish.Latest allowable event - early finish.Total slck can be + or -ve.Total slack is the amount of time the whole project can be delayed without pushing the end date of the project. This is calculated by using early and late starts and early and late finishes for tasks. Total slack is calculated as the smaller value of the late finish minus the early finish when compared to early start minus the late start.
	Hamza, 2020, What is TL here?
	Yash Jain, 2020, Time least(TL) for an activity. @Hamza.

	Naveen B, 2015, {Op time + (4 * Most lik time) + pess time}/6.i.e {15+(4*28)+21}/6 which is equal to 24.6666 = 25.

	Kajal Tomar, 2018, Latest occurrence time calculated from backward pass method.Therefore  TL of event 4 is 75 given;Now( 75- (TE of 3-4) - (TE of2-3)),TE =( opt time + 4 *most lik time + pess time) /6.75 - (15) - (35) = 25 i.e  TL of event 2  i.e asked.

The bearing of the longest line of a wind rose is S 45° E, the bearing of the runway will be numbered
	Sri Ganesh, 2015, How to calculate?
	Merin, 2015, Divide its magnetic heading by 10, round it to the nearest whole number and you will usually get the runway number. For example, if the magnetic heading is 345° then 345/10 = 34, 5.So the runway number will be 35 which will be a runway used for landings (and takeoffs) to the north.
	Sahana, 2017, How it is 13 and 31? Explain it.
	Prabhu, 2017, S 45° E means 90 + 45 = 135 135/10 = 13.5 so its nearest value 13 and 360-45=315 315/10= 31.5 near value 31.
	Varun, 2018, Thank you for explaining it.
	C. Sougoumarane, 2018, The difference between the runway number is always 18.
	Raj Kumar Budha, 2018, How to solve this? Please explain.
	Sameer Sopori, 2018, @Sahana.The direction for both numbers 13 and 31 will be the same,Just draw a line with 130° clockwise, and then draw the line 310°. You will see both are in the same direction in two opposite coordinates.
	Jegan, 2018, Thank you @Sameer.

Pick up the correct statement from the following:



The distance between main gears is 10 m, and the distance of nose gear from centre of main gears is 30 m. If the angle of turning is 60°, the distance of centre of rotation from the nearer main gear, is
	Harri, 2016, Please give the explanation of the problem.
	Vijay, 2017, Please give explaination.
	Worrie, 2017, Sir, please give the explanation.
	Ajith, 2017, Tuning radius= t * tan(90-turning angle)+b/2.t=30 m,b=10 m,Turning radius=60,After solving turning radius=22.32 m,Distance of centre of rotation from nearer main gear=22.32-10=12.32 m.

Two single runways may be arranged so as to have



The length of runway is increased per 300 m rise above M.S.L.



If lift off distance of an craft is 2000 m, the clear way at the end of the runway should not be less than
	Satya, 2016, Can anyone explain it?
	Afzal, 2016, CL length = 1/2 (TOD - 1.15 * LOD).LOD (Lift off distance) = 1.15 * D (10.5 m or 35 ft Height) = 1.15 * 2000 = 2300 m.*TOD (Takeoff Distance) = 1.15 * D (10.5 m or 35 ft Height) = 1.15 * 2300 = 2645 m.CL Length = 0.5 * (2645 - 2300) =172.5 m.
	Rohit, 2018, Thank you @Afzal.
	Rafeeq, 2020, Thank you @Afzal.
